### User *(2026-02-25T15:17:47.091Z)*

```
Here are the results. The script found **10 WEMI-related properties** in `fabio.owl`.

---

**FaBiO WEMI-related properties (10 found)**
Source: `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/fabio.owl`

---

```
Property:      fabio:hasAccessDate
Type:          owl:DatatypeProperty
Label:         has access date
Comment:       The date on which a particular digital item, such as a PDF or an
               HTML file, has been accessed by somebody.
Domain:        fabio:Item
Range:         xsd:dateTime
subPropertyOf: dcterms:date
```

```
Property:      fabio:hasDepositDate
Type:          owl:DatatypeProperty
Label:         has deposit date
Comment:       The date on which an entity has been deposited, for example in a
               library, repository, supplementary information archive, database
               or similar place of document or information storage.
Domain:        fabio:Item
Range:         xsd:dateTime
subPropertyOf: dcterms:date
```

```
Property:      fabio:hasManifestation
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         has manifestation
Comment:       A property linking a particular work to its manifestations. This
               property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours
               present in the classical FRBR data model.
Domain:        frbr:Work
Range:         frbr:Manifestation
subPropertyOf: frbr:relatedEndeavour
```

```
Property:      fabio:hasPortrayal
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         has portrayal
Comment:       A property linking a particular work to its items. This property
               is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours present
               in the classical FRBR data model.
Domain:        frbr:Work
Range:         frbr:Item
subPropertyOf: frbr:relatedEndeavour
```

```
Property:      fabio:hasRepresentation
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         has representation
Comment:       A property linking a particular expression to its items. This
               property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours
               present in the classical FRBR data model.
Domain:        frbr:Expression
Range:         frbr:Item
subPropertyOf: frbr:relatedEndeavour
```

```
Property:      fabio:isManifestationOf
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         is manifestation of
Comment:       A property linking a particular manifestation to the work it is
               manifesting. This property is additional to the relationships
               between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.
Domain:        frbr:Manifestation
Range:         frbr:Work
subPropertyOf: frbr:relatedEndeavour
```

```
Property:      fabio:isPortrayalOf
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         is portrayal of
Comment:       A property linking a particular item to the work it portrays. This
               property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours
               present in the classical FRBR data model.
Domain:        frbr:Item
Range:         frbr:Work
subPropertyOf: frbr:relatedEndeavour
```

```
Property:      fabio:isRepresentationOf
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         is representation of
Comment:       A property linking a particular item to the expression it
               represents. This property is additional to the relationships
               between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.
Domain:        frbr:Item
Range:         frbr:Expression
subPropertyOf: frbr:relatedEndeavour
```

```
Property:      fabio:isStoredOn
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         is stored on
Comment:       This property relates a fabio:Item to the medium upon which it
               is stored.
Domain:        fabio:Item
Range:         fabio:StorageMedium
subPropertyOf: owl:topObjectProperty
```

```
Property:      fabio:stores
Type:          owl:ObjectProperty
Label:         stores
Comment:       This property relates a storage medium to the fabio:Item stored
               upon it.
Domain:        fabio:StorageMedium
Range:         fabio:Item
subPropertyOf: owl:topObjectProperty
```

---

**Notes on what was found:**

- **8 ObjectProperties, 2 DatatypeProperties.**
- The 6 cross-WEMI object properties (`hasManifestation`, `hasPortrayal`, `hasRepresentation` and their inverses) are FaBiO additions to the FRBR model — they shortcut Work-to-Manifestation and Work/Expression-to-Item links that FRBR only provides indirectly. Their domains/ranges are typed against `frbr:` (the FRBR Core vocabulary), not `fabio:` directly — so these are not found if you search only for `fabio:` class URIs in domain/range.
- The 2 datatype properties (`hasAccessDate`, `hasDepositDate`) and 2 storage properties (`isStoredOn`, `stores`) use `fabio:Item` directly as domain or range.
- No properties with `fabio:Expression` or `fabio:Manifestation` in domain/range were found — FaBiO delegates those to the `frbr:` namespace classes.

Script saved to `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/extract_fabio_wemi_properties.py`. README updated at `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/README.md`.

```
The output is 638 properties and 189 KB — too large to paste inline. The full list is written to `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/output/rda-work-properties.txt`. Here is a summary of what was found and how the script works.

---

## Results

**638 RDA Work properties** extracted from three source files:

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `xml/Elements/w.xml` | Canonical properties: English labels, definitions, scope notes |
| `xml/Elements/w/datatype.xml` | Constrained sub-properties (literal values) |
| `xml/Elements/w/object.xml` | Constrained sub-properties (URI/resource values) |

**Type breakdown:**

| Type | Count |
|---|---|
| datatype and object | 615 |
| datatype only | 18 |
| unconstrained (no sub-property) | 5 |

The 5 "unconstrained" entries are P10037–P10040 (which have no English label in the vocabulary — only Danish, French, German, etc.) and P10230 (deprecated).

The 18 datatype-only properties include things like `rdaw:P10003` (deprecated distinguishing characteristic), `rdaw:P10012` (numbering of part), and similar string-valued attributes that have no object counterpart.

**Block format per property:**
```
ID:         rdaw:P10001
Label:      has respondent
Definition: Relates a work to a person who is a candidate for a degree who defends or
            opposes a thesis provided by the praeses in an academic disputation.
Type:       datatype and object
```

---

## Files

- Script: `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/extract_rda_work_properties.py`
- Output: `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/output/rda-work-properties.txt`
- Inventory: `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/README.md` (updated)

The script takes `--vocab-dir` and `--out` arguments; defaults assume you run it from the `mocho/` project root.

## Summary

| Entity | Total properties | Datatype only | Object only | Both (datatype + object) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Expression | 559 | 30 | 0 | 529 |
| Manifestation | 429 | 103 | 0 | 326 |
| Item | 156 | 6 | 0 | 150 |
| **Total** | **1,144** | **139** | **0** | **1,005** |

All properties appear in both base namespace prefixes `rdae:` / `rdam:` / `rdai:` (the canonical element IDs). Datatype-only properties are string-valued (no object range); every object property also has a datatype variant in these files (there are no object-only properties in the 5.4.9 release).

---

(The P20336–P20571 block is the full set of person / collective agent / corporate body / family sub-properties of each agent role, systematically covering all 4 agent sub-types × ~59 roles — all shown in the terminal output above.)

---

## MANIFESTATION (429 properties)

All listed above in the terminal output. Key groupings:

- **P30001–P30014**: Core carrier/media attributes (carrier type, media type, mode of issuance, identifier, preferred citation, regional encoding, dates)
- **P30016–P30048**: Relationships between manifestations (reproductions, facsimiles, digital transfers, parts)
- **P30050–P30065**: Notes (notes on distribution, manufacture, publication, dimensions, extent, series, edition, etc.)
- **P30067–P30095**: Agent roles (broadcaster, film distributor, book designer, engraver, printer, publisher, distributor, manufacturer, producer, etc.)
- **P30096–P30131**: Numbering, title, and statement fields
- **P30132–P30213**: Edition/series designations, content types, format/technical characteristics (encoding, video format, sound, layout)
- **P30214–P30252**: Detail notes for carrier characteristics
- **P30253–P30295**: Bibliographic relationships, related-entity links, access points, appellations
- **P30296–P30466**: Remaining: fingerprint, names of agents, material/binding, content types, holdings, subcollections, finding aids, indexes — plus the full person/collective agent/corporate body/family sub-properties of all agent roles (P30336–P30451)

---

## ITEM (156 properties)

All listed in the terminal output above. Key groupings:

- **P40001**: has identifier for item
- **P40003**: has modification of item (datatype only)
- **P40004–P40025**: Agent roles (collector, depositor, donor, seller, illuminator, binder, inscriber, annotator, autographer, curator, current/former owner, custodian, honouree, restorationist, dedicatee)
- **P40026**: has custodial history of item (datatype)
- **P40028**: has note on item (datatype)
- **P40029–P40046**: Item-to-item relationships (held with, bound with, equivalent, container, filmed with, on disc with)
- **P40047–P40048**: Restrictions on access/use
- **P40049**: has manifestation exemplified
- **P40050**: has immediate source of acquisition (datatype)
- **P40061–P40067**: Subject/description relationships (subject of, described by, analysed in, commentary in, critiqued/evaluated/reviewed — some deprecated)
- **P40068–P40079**: Related-entity links (related RDA entity, work, expression, manifestation, agent, person, family, corporate body, collective agent, place, timespan, nomen)
- **P40080–P40086**: Access points and appellations
- **P40087–P40092**: Reproduction/transfer relationships (digital transfer, electronic reproduction, facsimile, preservation facsimile, reprint, reproduction)
- **P40093–P40096**: Modifier agent, binding, related entity, category
- **P40097–P40160**: Full person/collective agent/corporate body/family sub-properties of all agent roles
- **P40161–P40164**: Location, holding, on-manifestation-with, metadata relationships

---

- `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/output/rda_properties.tsv` — compact TSV (1,145 rows, one per property)

These are likely new or provisional elements not yet fully annotated in 5.4.9.

### Assistant *(2026-02-25T15:26:05.148Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/docs/fabio-wemi-rda-mapping.md",
  "content": "# FaBiO WEMI Properties → RDA Mapping\n\nFaBiO has 10 properties whose domain or range touches a WEMI entity (Work, Expression, Manifestation, Item). This document maps each to its closest RDA equivalent and flags gaps.\n\n**Sources**\n- FaBiO: `ontology/fabio.owl`\n- RDA 5.4.9: `ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements/{w,e,m,i}/datatype.xml` and `object.xml`\n\n---\n\n## Summary table\n\n| FaBiO property | Domain → Range | RDA equivalent | RDA ID | Match quality |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `fabio:hasManifestation` | Work → Manifestation | has manifestation of work | `rdaw:P10072` | ✓ direct |\n| `fabio:isManifestationOf` | Manifestation → Work | has work manifested | `rdam:P30135` | ✓ direct |\n| `fabio:hasRepresentation` | Expression → Item | has related item of expression | `rdae:P20300` | ~ approximate |\n| `fabio:isRepresentationOf` | Item → Expression | has related expression of item | `rdai:P40070` | ~ approximate |\n| `fabio:hasPortrayal` | Work → Item | has related item of work | `rdaw:P10310` | ~ approximate |\n| `fabio:isPortrayalOf` | Item → Work | has related work of item | `rdai:P40069` | ~ approximate |\n| `fabio:hasDepositDate` | Item → xsd:dateTime | has custodial history of item | `rdai:P40026` | ~ partial |\n| `fabio:hasAccessDate` | Item → xsd:dateTime | has note on item | `rdai:P40028` | ✗ no direct match |\n| `fabio:isStoredOn` | Item → StorageMedium | has carrier type / has media type | `rdam:P30001` / `rdam:P30002` | ✗ level mismatch |\n| `fabio:stores` | StorageMedium → Item | *(none)* | — | ✗ no match |\n\n---\n\n## Property-by-property notes\n\n### `fabio:hasManifestation` → `rdaw:P10072`\n**Direct match.** RDA defines \"has manifestation of work\" (`rdaw:P10072`) with exactly the same semantics: *Relates a work to a manifestation that is a physical embodiment of a work.* FaBiO frames this as an FRBR shortcut (the standard FRBR path goes Work→Expression→Manifestation), but RDA explicitly provides this direct link.\n\n### `fabio:isManifestationOf` → `rdam:P30135`\n**Direct match (inverse direction).** RDA \"has work manifested\" (`rdam:P30135`): *Relates a manifestation to a work that is embodied by a manifestation.* This is the Manifestation-side inverse of `rdaw:P10072`.\n\nNote: RDA also offers `rdam:P30139` \"has expression manifested\" for the Manifestation→Expression direction, which is the intermediate step FaBiO skips.\n\n### `fabio:hasRepresentation` → `rdae:P20300`\n**Approximate match.** FaBiO links an Expression directly to an Item. RDA provides `rdae:P20300` \"has related item of expression\": *Relates an expression to an item that is associated with an expression.* The RDA definition is broader (any association) rather than specifically meaning \"this expression is realized by this item.\" The standard RDA path is Expression→Manifestation (`rdae:P20059`) → Item (`rdam:P30xxx`). Use `rdae:P20300` where a direct shortcut is needed, but note it carries weaker semantics.\n\n### `fabio:isRepresentationOf` → `rdai:P40070`\n**Approximate match (inverse direction).** RDA \"has related expression of item\" (`rdai:P40070`): *Relates an item to an expression that is associated with an item.* Same caveat as above — the association is generic, not specifically \"this item instantiates this expression.\" The canonical RDA direction is Item→Manifestation (`rdai:P40049`) → Expression (`rdam:P30139`).\n\n### `fabio:hasPortrayal` → `rdaw:P10310`\n**Approximate match.** FaBiO links a Work directly to an Item. RDA \"has related item of work\" (`rdaw:P10310`): *Relates a work to an item that is associated with a work.* Again, a generic association property covering a three-hop path (Work→Expression→Manifestation→Item). RDA does not provide a direct Work→Item realization property.\n\n### `fabio:isPortrayalOf` → `rdai:P40069`\n**Approximate match (inverse direction).** RDA \"has related work of item\" (`rdai:P40069`): *Relates an item to a work that is associated with an item.* Same level-of-specificity caveat.\n\n### `fabio:hasDepositDate` → `rdai:P40026` (partial)\n**Partial match.** FaBiO captures the date an item was deposited in a library/repository as a typed literal (`xsd:dateTime`). RDA \"has custodial history of item\" (`rdai:P40026`) is datatype-only (free text) and covers a broader history of ownership and custody — deposit date is one possible component of that string, not a structured field. Alternatively, `rdai:P40050` \"has immediate source of acquisition of item\" mentions date of acquisition in its definition but is also free text. Neither RDA property is a structural equivalent; deposit date would need to be recorded as a substring within one of these notes.\n\n### `fabio:hasAccessDate` → *(no direct match)*\n**No direct match.** RDA has no property for \"date on which a digital item was accessed.\" This concept belongs to citation metadata (e.g., `dcterms:accessed`) rather than bibliographic description. Closest fallback is `rdai:P40028` \"has note on item\" (free text). Outside RDA, `dcterms:date` or `schema:dateAccessed` are more appropriate.\n\n### `fabio:isStoredOn` → `rdam:P30001` / `rdam:P30002` (level mismatch)\n**Level mismatch.** FaBiO attaches storage medium to an Item (`fabio:Item → fabio:StorageMedium`). In RDA, carrier and medium type are Manifestation-level properties: `rdam:P30001` \"has carrier type\" and `rdam:P30002` \"has media type.\" Individual item-level carrier notes can go into `rdai:P40028` \"has note on item,\" but RDA does not model a typed Item→medium relationship. If item-level carrier specifics matter (e.g., this copy is on a different medium than the typical manifestation), use the item note.\n\n### `fabio:stores` → *(no match)*\n**No match.** This is the inverse of `fabio:isStoredOn` (StorageMedium → Item). RDA has no StorageMedium class and no inverse carrier-to-item property.\n\n---\n\n## Cross-WEMI shortcut properties: structural note\n\nFaBiO introduces six cross-WEMI shortcuts that bypass one or two levels of the FRBR stack:\n\n| FaBiO shortcut | Levels skipped | Standard RDA path |\n|---|---|---|\n| Work → Manifestation | Expression | `rdaw:P10072` ← RDA provides this directly |\n| Manifestation → Work | Expression | `rdam:P30135` ← RDA provides this directly |\n| Expression → Item | Manifestation | `rdae:P20059` + `rdam:P30xxx` (two hops) |\n| Item → Expression | Manifestation | `rdai:P40049` + `rdam:P30139` (two hops) |\n| Work → Item | Expression + Manifestation | three hops |\n| Item → Work | Expression + Manifestation | three hops |\n\nRDA absorbed the two-level shortcut (Work↔Manifestation) but not the three-level ones or the Expression↔Item shortcuts. The \"has related X of Y\" properties in RDA cover those cases but with generic \"associated with\" semantics rather than a formal realization/embodiment claim.\n"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-02-25T15:26:55.906Z)*

Here's what the mapping found:

**Direct matches (2)**
- `fabio:hasManifestation` → `rdaw:P10072` "has manifestation of work" — RDA explicitly provides this shortcut
- `fabio:isManifestationOf` → `rdam:P30135` "has work manifested" — the Manifestation-side inverse

**Approximate matches (4)** — RDA has corresponding "has related X of Y" properties but with weaker, generic-association semantics rather than formal realization/embodiment claims:
- `fabio:hasRepresentation` (Expression→Item) → `rdae:P20300`
- `fabio:isRepresentationOf` (Item→Expression) → `rdai:P40070`
- `fabio:hasPortrayal` (Work→Item) → `rdaw:P10310`
- `fabio:isPortrayalOf` (Item→Work) → `rdai:P40069`

**No adequate match (4)**
- `fabio:hasDepositDate` — RDA has no structured date-of-deposit field; closest is `rdai:P40026` (custodial history, free text)
- `fabio:hasAccessDate` — citation concept, outside RDA's scope entirely
- `fabio:isStoredOn` — carrier info lives at Manifestation level in RDA (`rdam:P30001`/`P30002`), not Item level
- `fabio:stores` — no StorageMedium class or inverse in RDA

The mapping is saved to [docs/fabio-wemi-rda-mapping.md](docs/fabio-wemi-rda-mapping.md).

@prefix owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> .
@prefix rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#> .

@prefix swrlb: <http://www.w3.org/2003/11/swrlb#> .

@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

dc:contributor a owl:AnnotationProperty .

dc:creator a owl:AnnotationProperty .

dc:date a owl:AnnotationProperty .

dc:description a owl:AnnotationProperty .

dc:rights a owl:AnnotationProperty .

dc:title a owl:AnnotationProperty .

<http://purl.org/spar/frbr> a owl:Ontology ;
    dc:contributor "David Shotton" ;
    dc:creator "Paolo Ciccarese"^^xsd:string,
        "Silvio Peroni"^^xsd:string ;
    dc:date "2018-03-29" ;
    dc:description "This vocabulary is an expression in OWL 2 DL of the basic concepts and relations described in the IFLA report on the Functional Requirements for Bibliographic Records (FRBR), also described in Ian Davis's RDF vocabulary (http://vocab.org/frbr/core)."@en ;
    dc:rights "This work is distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution License (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/)."@en ;
    dc:title "Essential FRBR in OWL2 DL"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """The Essential FRBR in OWL2 DL Ontology (FRBR) is an expression in OWL 2 DL of the basic concepts and relations described in the IFLA report on the Functional Requirements for Bibliographic Records (FRBR), also described in Ian Davis's RDF vocabulary.

**URL:** http://purl.org/spar/frbr

**Creators**: [Paolo Ciccarese](http://orcid.org/0000-0002-5156-2703), [Silvio Peroni](http://orcid.org/0000-0003-0530-4305)

**Contributors:**: [David Shotton](http://orcid.org/0000-0001-5506-523X)

**License:** [Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode)

**Website:** http://www.sparontologies.net/ontologies/frbr"""^^xsd:string ;
    owl:priorVersion <http://purl.org/spar/frbr/2011-06-29> ;
    owl:versionIRI <http://purl.org/spar/frbr/2018-03-29> ;
    owl:versionInfo "1.0.1"^^xsd:string .

core:alternate a owl:ObjectProperty ;
    rdfs:label "has alternate"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """A manifestation having another one as alternate.

The alternate relationship involves manifestations that effectively serve as alternates for each other. The alternate relationship obtains, for example, when a publication, sound recording, video, etc. is issued in more than one format or when it is released simultaneously by different publishers in different countries."""@en ;
    rdfs:domain core:Manifestation ;
    rdfs:range core:Manifestation ;
    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
    owl:inverseOf core:alternateOf .

core:creator a owl:ObjectProperty ;
    rdfs:label "has creator"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "A work linked to its creator."@en ;
    rdfs:domain core:Work ;
    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:responsibleEntity ;
    owl:inverseOf core:creatorOf .

core:owner a owl:ObjectProperty ;
    rdfs:label "has owner"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "An item linked to its owner."@en ;
    rdfs:domain core:Item ;
    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:responsibleEntity ;
    owl:inverseOf core:ownerOf .

core:producer a owl:ObjectProperty ;
    rdfs:label "has producer"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "A manifestation linked to its prodecer."@en ;
    rdfs:domain core:Manifestation ;
    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:responsibleEntity ;
    owl:inverseOf core:producerOf .

core:realizer a owl:ObjectProperty ;
```

    89→    rdfs:comment """An item reconfigured in another one.

    91→The reconfiguration relationship is one in which one or more items are changed in such a way that a new item or items result. Most commonly, an item of one manifestation is bound with an item of a different manifestation to make a new item. """@en ;

    98→    rdfs:label "has reproduction"@en ;
    99→    rdfs:comment """A manifestation/item reproduced in another one.

   110→    rdfs:label "has subject"@en ;
   111→    rdfs:comment "A work linked to a particular subject it is talking about."@en ;

   124→skos:note a owl:AnnotationProperty ;
   125→    rdfs:label "skos:note"@en ;

   127→
   128→core:Concept a owl:Class ;
   129→    rdfs:label "concept"@en ;
   130→    rdfs:comment """An abstract notion or idea.
   131→
   132→The entity defined as concept encompasses a comprehensive range of abstractions that may be the subject of a work: fields of knowledge, disciplines, schools of thought (philosophies, religions, political ideologies, etc.), theories, processes, techniques, practices, etc.  A concept may be broad in nature or narrowly defined and precise. """@en ;
   133→    rdfs:subClassOf core:Subject .
   134→
   135→core:CorporateBody a owl:Class ;
   136→    rdfs:label "corporate body"@en ;
   137→    rdfs:comment """An organization or group of individuals and/or organizations acting as a unit.
   138→
   139→The entity defined as corporate body encompasses organizations and groups of individuals and/or organizations that are identified by a particular name, including occasional groups and groups that are constituted as meetings, conferences, congresses, expeditions, exhibitions, festivals, fairs, etc."""@en ;
   140→    rdfs:subClassOf core:ResponsibleEntity ;
   141→    owl:disjointWith core:Person .
   142→
   143→core:Event a owl:Class ;
   144→    rdfs:label "event"@en ;
   145→    rdfs:comment """An action or occurrence.
   146→
   147→The entity defined as event encompasses a comprehensive range of actions and occurrences that may be the subject of a work: historical events, epochs, periods of time, etc. """@en ;
   148→    rdfs:subClassOf core:Subject .
   149→
   150→core:Object a owl:Class ;
   151→    rdfs:label "object"@en ;
   152→    rdfs:comment """A material thing.
   153→
   154→The entity defined as object encompasses a comprehensive range of material things that may be the subject of a work: animate and inanimate objects occurring in nature; fixed, movable, and moving objects that are the product of human creation; objects that no longer exist. """@en ;
   155→    rdfs:subClassOf core:Subject .
   156→
   157→core:Person a owl:Class ;
   158→    rdfs:label "person"@en ;
   159→    rdfs:comment "An individual. The entity defined as person encompasses individuals that are deceased as well as those that are living."@en ;
   160→    rdfs:subClassOf core:ResponsibleEntity .
   161→
   162→core:Place a owl:Class ;
   163→    rdfs:label "place"@en ;
   164→    rdfs:comment """A location.
   165→
   166→The entity defined as place encompasses a comprehensive range of locations: terrestrial and extra-terrestrial; historical and contemporary; geographic features and geo-political jurisdictions. """@en ;
   167→    rdfs:subClassOf core:Subject .
   168→
   169→core:abridgement a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   170→    rdfs:label "has abridgement"@en ;
   171→    rdfs:comment """An expression abridged in another one.
   172→
   173→In the abridged expression some content of the previous expression is removed, but the result does not alter the content to the extent that it becomes a new work. The expressions resulting from such modification are generally autonomous in nature (i.e., they do not normally require reference to the prior expression in order to be used or understood). """@en ;
   174→    rdfs:domain core:Expression ;
   175→    rdfs:range core:Expression ;
   176→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   177→    owl:inverseOf core:abridgementOf .
   178→
   179→core:abridgementOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   180→    rdfs:label "is abridgement of"@en ;
   181→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the entire expression of an abridged one."@en ;
   182→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   183→
   184→core:adaption a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   185→    rdfs:label "has adaption"@en ;
   186→    rdfs:comment """A work/expression adapted in another one.
   187→
   188→This property describe the modification of an original work that is sufficient in degree to warrant their being considered as new works, rather than simply different expressions of the same work. If there exists a relation of this kind among two different expressions, they always refer to different works."""@en ;
   189→    rdfs:domain [ a owl:Class ;
   190→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   191→    rdfs:range [ a owl:Class ;
   192→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   193→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   194→    owl:inverseOf core:adaptionOf .
   195→
   196→core:adaptionOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   197→    rdfs:label "is adaption of"@en ;
   198→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the work/expression of an adapted one."@en ;
   199→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   200→
   201→core:alternateOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   202→    rdfs:label "is alternate of"@en ;
   203→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the manifestation of an alternative one."@en ;
   204→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   205→
   206→core:arrangement a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   207→    rdfs:label "has arrangement"@en ;
   208→    rdfs:comment """An expression arranged in another one.
   209→
   210→In the arranged expression some content of the previous expression is changed in some way, but the result does not alter the content to the extent that it becomes a new work. The expressions resulting from such modification are generally autonomous in nature (i.e., they do not normally require reference to the prior expression in order to be used or understood)."""@en ;
   211→    rdfs:domain core:Expression ;
   212→    rdfs:range core:Expression ;
   213→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   214→    owl:inverseOf core:arrangementOf .
   215→
   216→core:arrangementOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   217→    rdfs:label "is arrangement of"@en ;
   218→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the original expression of an arranged one."@en ;
   219→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   220→
   221→core:complement a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   222→    rdfs:label "has complement"@en ;
   223→    rdfs:comment """An expression work/expression having another one as complement.
   224→
   225→This property describes works that are intended to be combined with or inserted into the related work. In other words, they are intended to be integrated in some way with the other work, but were not part of the original conception of that prior work. If there exists a relation of this kind among two different expressions, then they always refer to different works."""@en ;

   231→    owl:inverseOf core:complementOf .

   235→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the work/expression of that is a complement of another one."@en ;
   236→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   237→
   238→core:creatorOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;

   241→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:responsibleEntityOf .

   249→    owl:inverseOf core:embodimentOf .
   250→
   251→core:exemplar a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   252→    rdfs:label "has exemplar"@en ;
   253→    rdfs:comment "A manifestation exemplified in an item."@en ;
   254→    rdfs:domain core:Manifestation ;
   255→    rdfs:range core:Item ;
   256→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   257→    owl:inverseOf core:exemplarOf .
   258→
   259→core:imitation a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   260→    rdfs:label "has imitation"@en ;
   261→    rdfs:comment """An work/expression imitated in another one.
   262→
   263→This property describes works that are intended to be an imitation another original work that is sufficient in degree to warrant their being considered as new works, rather than simply different expressions of the same work. If there exists a relation of this kind among two different expressions, then they always refer to different works."""@en ;
   264→    rdfs:domain [ a owl:Class ;
   265→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   266→    rdfs:range [ a owl:Class ;
   267→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   268→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   269→    owl:inverseOf core:imitationOf .
   270→
   271→core:imitationOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   272→    rdfs:label "is imitation of"@en ;
   273→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the work/expression of an imitated one."@en ;
   274→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   275→
   276→core:ownerOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   277→    rdfs:label "is owner of"@en ;
   278→    rdfs:comment "The owner of a particular item."@en ;
   279→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:responsibleEntityOf .
   280→
   281→core:producerOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   282→    rdfs:label "is producer of"@en ;
   283→    rdfs:comment "The producer of a particular manifestation."@en ;
   284→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:responsibleEntityOf .
   285→
   286→core:realization a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   287→    rdfs:label "has realization"@en ;
   288→    rdfs:comment "A work realized through an expression."@en ;
   289→    rdfs:domain core:Work ;
   290→    rdfs:range core:Expression ;
   291→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   292→    owl:inverseOf core:realizationOf .
   293→
   294→core:realizerOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   295→    rdfs:label "is realizer of"@en ;
   296→    rdfs:comment "The realizer of a particular expression."@en ;
   297→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:responsibleEntityOf .
   298→
   299→core:reconfigurationOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   300→    rdfs:label "is reconfiguration of"@en ;
   301→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the manifestation of a reconfigured one."@en ;
   302→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   303→
   304→core:reproductionOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   305→    rdfs:label "is reproduction of"@en ;
   306→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the manifestation/item of a reproduced one."@en ;
   307→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   308→
   309→core:revision a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   310→    rdfs:label "has revision"@en ;
   311→    rdfs:comment """An expression revised in another one.
   312→
   313→A revision has the intent to alter or update the content of the prior expression, but without changing the content so much that it becomes a new work."""@en ;
   314→    rdfs:domain core:Expression ;
   315→    rdfs:range core:Expression ;
   316→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   317→    owl:inverseOf core:revisionOf .
   318→
   319→core:revisionOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   320→    rdfs:label "is revision of"@en ;
   321→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the previous expression of a revised one."@en ;
   322→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   323→
   324→core:subjectOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   325→    rdfs:label "is subject of"@en ;
   326→    rdfs:comment "A subject a work talks abbout."@en ;
   327→    rdfs:subPropertyOf owl:topObjectProperty .
   328→
   329→core:successor a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   330→    rdfs:label "has successor"@en ;
   331→    rdfs:comment """An expression work/expression having another one as successor.
   332→
   333→The successor type of relationship involves a kind of linear progression of content from one work/expression to the other. In some cases, the content of the successor may be closely connected to the content of the preceding work, which would result in a work that is referential. In others, such as with loosely connected parts of a trilogy, the successor will be autonomous. Serial publications that result from the merger or split of their predecessors and stand on their own without requiring reference to the predecessor are also examples of autonomous works that fall within the successor relationship type. If there exists a relation of this kind among two different expressions, then they always refer to different works."""@en ;
   334→    rdfs:domain [ a owl:Class ;
   335→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   336→    rdfs:range [ a owl:Class ;
   337→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   338→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   339→    owl:inverseOf core:successorOf .
   340→
   341→core:successorOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   342→    rdfs:label "is successor of"@en ;
   343→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the previous work/expression of a succeeded one."@en ;
   344→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   345→
   346→core:summarization a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   347→    rdfs:label "has summarization"@en ;
   348→    rdfs:comment """A work/expression summarized in another one.
   349→
   350→This property describe the summarization of an original work that is sufficient in degree to warrant their being considered as new works, rather than simply different expressions of the same work. If there exists a relation of this kind among two different expressions, they always refer to different works."""@en ;
   351→    rdfs:domain [ a owl:Class ;
   352→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   353→    rdfs:range [ a owl:Class ;
   354→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   355→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   356→    owl:inverseOf core:summarizationOf .
   357→
   358→core:summarizationOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   359→    rdfs:label "is summarization of"@en ;
   360→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the original work/expression of a summarized one."@en ;
   361→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   362→
   363→core:supplement a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   364→    rdfs:label "has supplement"@en ;
   365→    rdfs:comment """An expression work/expression having another one as supplement.
   366→
   367→The supplement relationship type involves works/expressions that are intended to be used in conjunction with another work/expression. Some of these, such as indices, concordances, teachers' guides, glosses, and instruction manuals for electronic resources will be so closely associated with the content of the related work/expression that they are useless without the other work/expression."""@en ;
   368→    rdfs:domain [ a owl:Class ;
   369→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   370→    rdfs:range [ a owl:Class ;
   371→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   372→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   373→    owl:inverseOf core:supplementOf .
   374→
   375→core:supplementOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   376→    rdfs:label "is supplement of"@en ;
   377→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the work/expression of a particular supplement of it."@en ;
   378→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   379→
   380→core:transformation a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   381→    rdfs:label "has transformation"@en ;
   382→    rdfs:comment """An work/expression transformed in another one.
   383→
   384→This property describes the transformation of an original work or expression into another work or expression that is sufficiently different in degree to warrant the product of the transformation being considered as a new work or expression, rather than simply a different expression of the original work. If there exists a frbr:transformation relation between two different expressions, then they always relate to different works."""@en ;
   385→    rdfs:domain [ a owl:Class ;
   386→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   387→    rdfs:range [ a owl:Class ;
   388→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Work ) ] ;
   389→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   390→    owl:inverseOf core:transformationOf .
   391→
   392→core:transformationOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   393→    rdfs:label "is transformation of"@en ;
   394→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the original work/expression of a trasformed one."@en ;
   395→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   396→
   397→core:translation a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   398→    rdfs:label "has translation"@en ;
   399→    rdfs:comment """An expression translated in another one.
   400→
   401→It allows to refer to a literal translation, in which the intent is to render the intellectual content of the previous expression as accurately as possible."""@en ;
   402→    rdfs:domain core:Expression ;
   403→    rdfs:range core:Expression ;
   404→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   405→    owl:inverseOf core:translationOf .
   406→
   407→core:translationOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   408→    rdfs:label "is translation of"@en ;
   409→    rdfs:comment "It identifies the original expression of a translated one."@en ;
   410→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   411→
   412→core:embodimentOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   413→    rdfs:label "is embodiment of"@en ;
   414→    rdfs:comment "A manifestation that embodies an expression."@en ;
   415→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   416→
   417→core:exemplarOf a owl:FunctionalProperty,
   418→        owl:ObjectProperty ;
   419→    rdfs:label "is exemplar of"@en ;
   420→    rdfs:comment "An item that exemplifies a manifestation."@en ;
   421→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   422→
   423→core:ResponsibleEntity a owl:Class ;
   424→    rdfs:label "responsible entity"@en ;
   425→    rdfs:comment "It represents those responsible for the intellectual or artistic content, the physical production and dissemination, or the custodianship of any endeavour."@en .
   426→
   427→core:part a owl:ObjectProperty,
   428→        owl:TransitiveProperty ;
   429→    rdfs:label "has part"@en ;
   430→    rdfs:comment "A part of an endeavour."@en ;
   431→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   432→    owl:inverseOf core:partOf ;
   433→    skos:note "Unlike the FRBR version in RDF http://vocab.org/frbr/core.html the present version defines partonomy relationships transitive."@en .
   434→
   435→core:responsibleEntity a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   436→    rdfs:label "has responsible entity"@en ;
   437→    rdfs:comment "Any endeavour having a particular entity that is responsible of it."@en ;
   438→    rdfs:domain core:Endeavour ;
   439→    rdfs:range core:ResponsibleEntity ;
   440→    rdfs:subPropertyOf owl:topObjectProperty ;
   441→    owl:inverseOf core:responsibleEntityOf .
   442→
   443→owl:topObjectProperty a owl:ObjectProperty .
   444→
   445→core:Subject a owl:Class ;
   446→    rdfs:label "subject"@en ;
   447→    rdfs:comment "It represents an additional set of entities that serve as the subjects of works."@en .
   448→
   449→core:partOf a owl:ObjectProperty,
   450→        owl:TransitiveProperty ;
   451→    rdfs:label "is part of"@en ;
   452→    rdfs:comment "An endeavour incorporating another endeavour."@en ;
   453→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour ;
   454→    skos:note "Unlike the FRBR version in RDF http://vocab.org/frbr/core.html the present version defines partonomy relationships transitive."@en .
   455→
   456→core:responsibleEntityOf a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   457→    rdfs:label "is responsible entity of"@en ;
   458→    rdfs:comment "An entity that is resposible for a particular endeavour."@en ;
   459→    rdfs:subPropertyOf owl:topObjectProperty .
   460→
   461→core:Item a owl:Class ;
   462→    rdfs:label "item"@en ;
   463→    rdfs:comment """A single exemplar of a manifestation.
   464→
   465→The entity defined as item is a concrete entity. It is in many instances a single physical object (e.g., a copy of a one-volume monograph, a single audio cassette, etc.). There are instances, however, where the entity defined as item comprises more than one physical object (e.g., a monograph issued as two separately bound volumes, a recording issued on three separate compact discs, etc.). """@en ;
   466→    owl:disjointWith core:Manifestation,
   467→        core:Work ;
   468→    owl:equivalentClass [ a owl:Class ;
   469→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   470→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Item ;
   471→                        owl:onProperty core:part ] [ a owl:Restriction ;
   472→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Item ;
   473→                        owl:onProperty core:partOf ] ) ],
   474→        [ a owl:Class ;
   475→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   476→                        owl:onProperty core:exemplarOf ;
   477→                        owl:someValuesFrom core:Manifestation ] ) ] .
   478→
   479→core:Endeavour a owl:Class ;
   480→    rdfs:label "endeavour"@en ;
   481→    rdfs:comment "It describes different aspects of user interests in the products of intellectual or artistic artifact."@en ;
   482→    owl:equivalentClass [ a owl:Class ;
   483→            owl:unionOf ( core:Expression core:Item core:Manifestation core:Work ) ] .
   484→
   485→core:Manifestation a owl:Class ;
   486→    rdfs:label "manifestation"@en ;
   487→    rdfs:comment """The physical embodiment of an expression of a work.
   488→
   489→The entity defined as manifestation encompasses a wide range of materials and formats. As an entity, manifestation represents all the physical objects that bear the same characteristics, in respect to both intellectual content and physical form. """@en ;
   490→    owl:disjointWith core:Work ;
   491→    owl:equivalentClass [ a owl:Class ;
   492→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   493→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Manifestation ;
   494→                        owl:onProperty core:part ] [ a owl:Restriction ;
   495→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Manifestation ;
   496→                        owl:onProperty core:partOf ] ) ],
   497→        [ a owl:Class ;
   498→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   499→                        owl:onProperty core:embodimentOf ;
   500→                        owl:someValuesFrom core:Expression ] [ a owl:Restriction ;
   501→                        owl:onProperty core:exemplar ;
   502→                        owl:someValuesFrom core:Item ] ) ] .
   503→
   504→<urn:swrl#e1> a swrl:Variable .
   505→
   506→<urn:swrl#e2> a swrl:Variable .
   507→
   508→<urn:swrl#w1> a swrl:Variable .
   509→
   510→<urn:swrl#w2> a swrl:Variable .
   511→
   512→core:Work a owl:Class ;
   513→    rdfs:label "work"@en ;
   514→    rdfs:comment """A distinct intellectual or artistic creation.
   515→
   516→A work is an abstract entity; there is no single material object one can point to as the work. We recognize the work through individual realizations or expressions of the work, but the work itself exists only in the commonality of content between and among the various expressions of the work. When we speak of Homer's Iliad as a work, our point of reference is not a particular recitation or text of the work, but the intellectual creation that lies behind all the various expressions of the work. """@en ;
   517→    owl:equivalentClass [ a owl:Class ;
   518→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   519→                        owl:onProperty core:realization ;
   520→                        owl:someValuesFrom core:Expression ] ) ],
   521→        [ a owl:Class ;
   522→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   523→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Work ;
   524→                        owl:onProperty core:part ] [ a owl:Restriction ;
   525→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Work ;
   526→                        owl:onProperty core:partOf ] ) ] .
   527→
   528→core:realizationOf a owl:FunctionalProperty,
   529→        owl:ObjectProperty ;
   530→    rdfs:label "is realization of"@en ;
   531→    rdfs:comment "An expression that realizes a work."@en ;
   532→    rdfs:subPropertyOf core:relatedEndeavour .
   533→
   534→core:Expression a owl:Class ;
   535→    rdfs:label "expression"@en ;
   536→    rdfs:comment """The intellectual or artistic realization of a work in the form of alpha-numeric, musical, or choreographic notation, sound, image, object, movement, etc., or any combination of such forms.
   537→
   538→An expression is the specific intellectual or artistic form that a work takes each time it is "realized." Expression encompasses, for example, the specific words, sentences, paragraphs, etc. that result from the realization of a work in the form of a text, or the particular sounds, phrasing, etc. resulting from the realization of a musical work."""@en ;
   539→    owl:disjointWith core:Item,
   540→        core:Manifestation,
   541→        core:Work ;
   542→    owl:equivalentClass [ a owl:Class ;
   543→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   544→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Expression ;
   545→                        owl:onProperty core:part ] [ a owl:Restriction ;
   546→                        owl:allValuesFrom core:Expression ;
   547→                        owl:onProperty core:partOf ] ) ],
   548→        [ a owl:Class ;
   549→            owl:intersectionOf ( core:Endeavour [ a owl:Restriction ;
   550→                        owl:onProperty core:embodiment ;
   551→                        owl:someValuesFrom core:Manifestation ] [ a owl:Restriction ;
   552→                        owl:onProperty core:realizationOf ;
   553→                        owl:someValuesFrom core:Work ] ) ] .
   554→
   555→core:relatedEndeavour a owl:ObjectProperty ;
   556→    rdfs:label "has related endeavour"@en ;
   557→    rdfs:domain core:Endeavour ;
   558→    rdfs:range core:Endeavour .
   559→
   560→[] a swrl:Imp ;
   561→    swrl:body [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   562→            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   563→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   564→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   565→                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   566→            rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   567→                    rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   568→                            swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   569→                            swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ;
   570→                            swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   571→                    rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   572→                            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   573→                                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   574→                                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   575→                                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:summarization ] ;
   576→                            rdf:rest () ] ] ] ;
   577→    swrl:head [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   578→            rdf:first [ a swrl:DifferentIndividualsAtom ;
   579→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   580→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ] ;
   581→            rdf:rest () ] .
   582→
   583→[] a swrl:Imp ;
   584→    swrl:body [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   585→            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   586→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   587→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   588→                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   589→            rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   590→                    rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   591→                            swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   592→                            swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ;
   593→                            swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   594→                    rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   595→                            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   596→                                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   597→                                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   598→                                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:translation ] ;
   599→                            rdf:rest () ] ] ] ;
   600→    swrl:head [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   601→            rdf:first [ a swrl:SameIndividualAtom ;
   602→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   603→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ] ;
   604→            rdf:rest () ] .
   605→
   606→[] a owl:AllDisjointClasses ;
   607→    owl:members ( core:Concept core:Event core:Object core:Place ) .
   608→
   609→[] a swrl:Imp ;
   610→    swrl:body [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   611→            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   612→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   613→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   614→                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:complement ] ;
   615→            rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   616→                    rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   617→                            swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   618→                            swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   619→                            swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   620→                    rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   621→                            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   622→                                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   623→                                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ;
   624→                                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   625→                            rdf:rest () ] ] ] ;
   626→    swrl:head [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   627→            rdf:first [ a swrl:DifferentIndividualsAtom ;
   628→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   629→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ] ;
   630→            rdf:rest () ] .
   631→
   632→[] a swrl:Imp ;
   633→    swrl:body [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   634→            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   635→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   636→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   637→                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:adaption ] ;
   638→            rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   639→                    rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   640→                            swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   641→                            swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   642→                            swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   643→                    rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   644→                            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   645→                                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   646→                                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ;
   647→                                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   648→                            rdf:rest () ] ] ] ;
   649→    swrl:head [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   650→            rdf:first [ a swrl:DifferentIndividualsAtom ;
   651→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   652→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ] ;
   653→            rdf:rest () ] .
   654→
   655→[] a swrl:Imp ;
   656→    swrl:body [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   657→            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   658→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   659→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   660→                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   661→            rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   662→                    rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   663→                            swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   664→                            swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ;
   665→                            swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   666→                    rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   667→                            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   668→                                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   669→                                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#e2> ;
   670→                                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:supplement ] ;
   671→                            rdf:rest () ] ] ] ;
   672→    swrl:head [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   673→            rdf:first [ a swrl:DifferentIndividualsAtom ;
   674→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   675→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w2> ] ;
   676→            rdf:rest () ] .
   677→
   678→[] a swrl:Imp ;
   679→    swrl:body [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   680→            rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   681→                    swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e1> ;
   682→                    swrl:argument2 <urn:swrl#w1> ;
   683→                    swrl:propertyPredicate core:realizationOf ] ;
   684→            rdf:rest [ a swrl:AtomList ;
   685→                    rdf:first [ a swrl:IndividualPropertyAtom ;
   686→                            swrl:argument1 <urn:swrl#e2> ;

### User *(2026-02-25T15:44:38.420Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# FaBiO WEMI Properties → RDA Mapping
     2→
     3→FaBiO has 10 properties whose domain or range touches a WEMI entity (Work, Expression, Manifestation, Item). This document maps each to its closest RDA equivalent and flags gaps.
     4→
     5→**Sources**
     6→- FaBiO: `ontology/fabio.owl`
     7→- RDA 5.4.9: `ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements/{w,e,m,i}/datatype.xml` and `object.xml`
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Summary table
    12→
    13→| FaBiO property | Domain → Range | RDA equivalent | RDA ID | Match quality |
    14→|---|---|---|---|---|
    15→| `fabio:hasManifestation` | Work → Manifestation | has manifestation of work | `rdaw:P10072` | ✓ direct |
    16→| `fabio:isManifestationOf` | Manifestation → Work | has work manifested | `rdam:P30135` | ✓ direct |
    17→| `fabio:hasRepresentation` | Expression → Item | has related item of expression | `rdae:P20300` | ~ approximate |
    18→| `fabio:isRepresentationOf` | Item → Expression | has related expression of item | `rdai:P40070` | ~ approximate |
    19→| `fabio:hasPortrayal` | Work → Item | has related item of work | `rdaw:P10310` | ~ approximate |
    20→| `fabio:isPortrayalOf` | Item → Work | has related work of item | `rdai:P40069` | ~ approximate |
    21→| `fabio:hasDepositDate` | Item → xsd:dateTime | has custodial history of item | `rdai:P40026` | ~ partial |
    22→| `fabio:hasAccessDate` | Item → xsd:dateTime | has note on item | `rdai:P40028` | ✗ no direct match |
    23→| `fabio:isStoredOn` | Item → StorageMedium | has carrier type / has media type | `rdam:P30001` / `rdam:P30002` | ✗ level mismatch |
    24→| `fabio:stores` | StorageMedium → Item | *(none)* | — | ✗ no match |
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Property-by-property notes
    29→
    30→### `fabio:hasManifestation` → `rdaw:P10072`
    31→**Direct match.** RDA defines "has manifestation of work" (`rdaw:P10072`) with exactly the same semantics: *Relates a work to a manifestation that is a physical embodiment of a work.* FaBiO frames this as an FRBR shortcut (the standard FRBR path goes Work→Expression→Manifestation), but RDA explicitly provides this direct link.
    32→
    33→### `fabio:isManifestationOf` → `rdam:P30135`
    34→**Direct match (inverse direction).** RDA "has work manifested" (`rdam:P30135`): *Relates a manifestation to a work that is embodied by a manifestation.* This is the Manifestation-side inverse of `rdaw:P10072`.
    35→
    36→Note: RDA also offers `rdam:P30139` "has expression manifested" for the Manifestation→Expression direction, which is the intermediate step FaBiO skips.
    37→
    38→### `fabio:hasRepresentation` → `rdae:P20300`
    39→**Approximate match.** FaBiO links an Expression directly to an Item. RDA provides `rdae:P20300` "has related item of expression": *Relates an expression to an item that is associated with an expression.* The RDA definition is broader (any association) rather than specifically meaning "this expression is realized by this item." The standard RDA path is Expression→Manifestation (`rdae:P20059`) → Item (`rdam:P30xxx`). Use `rdae:P20300` where a direct shortcut is needed, but note it carries weaker semantics.
    40→
    41→### `fabio:isRepresentationOf` → `rdai:P40070`
    42→**Approximate match (inverse direction).** RDA "has related expression of item" (`rdai:P40070`): *Relates an item to an expression that is associated with an item.* Same caveat as above — the association is generic, not specifically "this item instantiates this expression." The canonical RDA direction is Item→Manifestation (`rdai:P40049`) → Expression (`rdam:P30139`).
    43→
    44→### `fabio:hasPortrayal` → `rdaw:P10310`
    45→**Approximate match.** FaBiO links a Work directly to an Item. RDA "has related item of work" (`rdaw:P10310`): *Relates a work to an item that is associated with a work.* Again, a generic association property covering a three-hop path (Work→Expression→Manifestation→Item). RDA does not provide a direct Work→Item realization property.
    46→
    47→### `fabio:isPortrayalOf` → `rdai:P40069`
    48→**Approximate match (inverse direction).** RDA "has related work of item" (`rdai:P40069`): *Relates an item to a work that is associated with an item.* Same level-of-specificity caveat.
    49→
    50→### `fabio:hasDepositDate` → `rdai:P40026` (partial)
    51→**Partial match.** FaBiO captures the date an item was deposited in a library/repository as a typed literal (`xsd:dateTime`). RDA "has custodial history of item" (`rdai:P40026`) is datatype-only (free text) and covers a broader history of ownership and custody — deposit date is one possible component of that string, not a structured field. Alternatively, `rdai:P40050` "has immediate source of acquisition of item" mentions date of acquisition in its definition but is also free text. Neither RDA property is a structural equivalent; deposit date would need to be recorded as a substring within one of these notes.
    52→
    53→### `fabio:hasAccessDate` → *(no direct match)*
    54→**No direct match.** RDA has no property for "date on which a digital item was accessed." This concept belongs to citation metadata (e.g., `dcterms:accessed`) rather than bibliographic description. Closest fallback is `rdai:P40028` "has note on item" (free text). Outside RDA, `dcterms:date` or `schema:dateAccessed` are more appropriate.
    55→
    56→### `fabio:isStoredOn` → `rdam:P30001` / `rdam:P30002` (level mismatch)
    57→**Level mismatch.** FaBiO attaches storage medium to an Item (`fabio:Item → fabio:StorageMedium`). In RDA, carrier and medium type are Manifestation-level properties: `rdam:P30001` "has carrier type" and `rdam:P30002` "has media type." Individual item-level carrier notes can go into `rdai:P40028` "has note on item," but RDA does not model a typed Item→medium relationship. If item-level carrier specifics matter (e.g., this copy is on a different medium than the typical manifestation), use the item note.
    58→
    59→### `fabio:stores` → *(no match)*
    60→**No match.** This is the inverse of `fabio:isStoredOn` (StorageMedium → Item). RDA has no StorageMedium class and no inverse carrier-to-item property.
    61→
    62→---
    63→
    64→## Cross-WEMI shortcut properties: structural note
    65→
    66→FaBiO introduces six cross-WEMI shortcuts that bypass one or two levels of the FRBR stack:
    67→
    68→| FaBiO shortcut | Levels skipped | Standard RDA path |
    69→|---|---|---|
    70→| Work → Manifestation | Expression | `rdaw:P10072` ← RDA provides this directly |
    71→| Manifestation → Work | Expression | `rdam:P30135` ← RDA provides this directly |
    72→| Expression → Item | Manifestation | `rdae:P20059` + `rdam:P30xxx` (two hops) |
    73→| Item → Expression | Manifestation | `rdai:P40049` + `rdam:P30139` (two hops) |
    74→| Work → Item | Expression + Manifestation | three hops |
    75→| Item → Work | Expression + Manifestation | three hops |
    76→
    77→RDA absorbed the two-level shortcut (Work↔Manifestation) but not the three-level ones or the Expression↔Item shortcuts. The "has related X of Y" properties in RDA cover those cases but with generic "associated with" semantics rather than a formal realization/embodiment claim.
    78→



```

| RDA equivalent | RDA ID | Match |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `core:abridgement` | Expression → Expression | is abridged as expression | `rdae:P20166` | ✓ direct |\n| `core:abridgementOf` | Expression Expression | is translation of | `rdae:P20141` | ✓ direct |\n\n#### Work/Expression derivation relationships (union domain/range)\n\nRDA handles these at both Work and Expression level with separate typed properties. FRBR uses a single property with a union domain/range.\n\n| FRBR property | Domain → Range | RDA Work equivalent | RDA Expression equivalent | Match |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `core:adaption` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | is adapted as work (`rdaw:P10155`) / is adaptation of work (`rdaw:P10142`) | is adapted as expression (`rdae:P20153`) / is adaptation of expression (`rdae:P20152`) | ✓ direct (split by entity) |\n| `core:adaptionOf` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | (inverse of above) | (inverse of above) | ✓ direct |\n| `core:complement` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | is complemented by work (`rdaw:P10103`) | is complemented by expression (`rdae:P20100`) | ✓ direct (split) |\n| `core:complementOf` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | (inverse) | (inverse) | ✓ direct |\n| `core:imitation` | Work∪Expr (`rdae:P20106`, deprecated) | ~ partial / deprecated |\n| `core:summarizationOf` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | *(no current Work equivalent)* | is summary of expression (`rdae:P20179`, deprecated) | ~ partial / deprecated |\n| `core:supplement` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | has supplement work (`rdaw:P10172`) / is supplement to work (`rdaw:P10154`) | has supplement expression (`rdae:P20172`) / is supplement to expression (`rdae:P20151`) | ✓ direct (split) |\n| `core:supplementOf` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | (inverse) | (inverse) | ✓ direct |\n| `core:successor` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | has sequel work (`rdaw:P10020`) / is preceded by work (`rdaw:P10156`) / is succeeded by work (`rdaw:P10170`) | has sequel expression (`rdae:P20335`) / is preceded by expression (`rdae:P20154`) / is succeeded by expression (`rdae:P20169`) | ✓ direct (split; RDA also has prequel/sequel) |\n| `core:successorOf` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | has prequel work (`rdaw:P10122`) | has prequel expression (`rdae:P20334`) | ✓ direct |\n| `core:transformation` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | is transformation of (`rdaw:P10337`) | *(no current Expression equivalent)* | ~ partial (Work only) |\n| `core:transformationOf` | Work∪Expr → Work∪Expr | (inverse of above) | *(no current Expression equivalent)* | ~ partial |\n\n#### Manifestation relationships\n\n| FRBR property | Domain → Range | RDA equivalent | RDA ID | Match |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `core:alternate` | Manifestation → Manifestation | *(none)* | — | ✗ no match |\n| `core:alternateOf` | Manifestation → Manifestation | *(none)* | — | ✗ no match |\n| `core:reproduction` | Manifestation∪Item → Manifestation∪Item | has electronic reproduction / has facsimile / has digital transfer (manifestation); has digital transfer of item / has electronic reproduction of item (item) | `rdam:P30016`, `rdam:P30031`, `rdam:P30029`; `rdai:P40087`–`rdai:P40092` | ~ approximate (RDA distinguishes reproduction type) |\n| `core:reproductionOf` | Manifestation∪Item → Manifestation∪Item | is reproduction of manifestation of (`rdam:P30043`) / is electronic reproduction of item of (`rdai:P40088`) | `rdam:P30043`; `rdai:P40088` | ~ approximate |\n\n#### Item relationships\n\n| FRBR property | Domain → Range | RDA equivalent | RDA ID | Match |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `core:reconfiguration` | Item → Item | *(none)* | — | ✗ no match |\n| `core:reconfigurationOf` | Item → Item | *(none)* | — | ✗ no match |\n\n#### Top / generic properties\n\n| FRBR property | Notes |\n|---|---|\n| `core:relatedEndeavour` | Top-level super-property for all FRBR relationships. No RDA equivalent — RDA uses typed properties throughout with no common super-property. |\n| `core:responsibleEntity` | Generic agent link. RDA replaces this with typed role properties at each WEMI level. |\n| `core:responsibleEntityOf` | Inverse of above. Same note. |\n\n---\n\n### Property-by-property notes (FRBR)\n\n**`core:realization` / `core:realizationOf`** — The canonical Work↔Expression link. `rdaw:P10078` \"has expression of work\" and `rdae:P20231` \"has work expressed\" are direct equivalents. Note: `core:realizationOf` is declared a `owl:FunctionalProperty` in FRBR (each expression realizes exactly one work); RDA does not assert this constraint.\n\n**`core:embodiment` / `core:embodimentOf`** — The Expression↔Manifestation link. `rdae:P20059` and `rdam:P30139` are direct matches.\n\n**`core:exemplar` / `core:exemplarOf`** — The Manifestation↔Item link. `rdam:P30103` \"has exemplar of manifestation\" and `rdai:P40049` \"has manifestation exemplified\" are direct matches. `core:exemplarOf` is also declared `owl:FunctionalProperty` in FRBR; RDA does not assert this.\n\n**`core:part` / `core:partOf`** — FRBR Core defines these as `owl:TransitiveProperty` with the constraint that parts are typed to the same WEMI level (Work parts are Works, etc.). RDA provides entity-specific properties (`rdaw:P10147` \"has part work\", `rdae:P20145` \"has part expression\", etc.) but does not assert transitivity. Approximate match.\n\n**`core:realizer`** — A generic \"agent responsible for this expression.\" RDA splits this into many typed contributor roles (`rdae:P20053` \"has creator agent of expression\" being the closest generic equivalent). The FRBR property collapses translator, arranger, performer, etc. into one; RDA treats these as distinct properties.\n\n**`core:producer`** — A generic \"agent responsible for this manifestation.\" RDA provides `rdam:P30081` \"has producer agent of unpublished manifestation\" for unpublished items, and `rdam:P30329` \"has creator agent of manifestation\" for published ones. Neither is an exact one-to-one equivalent.\n\n**`core:alternate`** — No RDA equivalent. The FRBR alternate relationship (manifestations that serve as substitutes for each other, e.g., different format editions) is not modeled as a first-class property in RDA. The closest available property is `rdam:P30035` \"is on carrier unit with,\" which has different semantics (physical co-location on the same carrier unit).\n\n**`core:reproduction`** — FRBR uses one property for all reproduction types across both Manifestation and Item. RDA provides a family of specific properties distinguishing electronic reproduction, facsimile, digital transfer, preservation facsimile, and reprint — separately at Manifestation and Item levels. Approximate match via the relevant RDA sub-property.\n\n**`core:reconfiguration` / `core:reconfigurationOf`** — No RDA equivalent. FRBR defines this as binding two items together to produce a new item. RDA's `rdai:P40032` \"is bound with\" describes co-location of items in the same binding but does not model the production of a new item from reconfiguration.\n\n**`core:summarization` / `core:summarizationOf`** — The RDA Expression equivalents (`rdae:P20106`, `rdae:P20179`) are deprecated in 5.4.9. No current Work-level equivalent exists. This relationship is now expected to be handled as an adaptation or transformation in RDA.\n\n**`core:transformation`** — RDA provides `rdaw:P10337` \"is transformation of\" at the Work level (and related typed sub-properties `P10341`–`P10384` for transformation by style, genre, policy, etc.). No current Expression-level equivalent exists in RDA 5.4.9.\n"
}
```

Done. The mapping document now has a full FRBR Core section added after the FaBiO section. Here's the breakdown:

**39 FRBR Core properties mapped, grouped as:**

- **Structural chain** (6): `realization`, `embodiment`, `exemplar` and inverses — all **direct matches** to `rdaw:P10078`, `rdae:P20059/P20231`, `rdam:P30103/P30139`, `rdai:P40049`
- **Partonomy** (2): `part`/`partOf` — **approximate** (RDA splits by entity type, no transitivity)
- **Agent roles** (4): `creator`, `realizer`, `producer`, `owner` — direct for creator/owner; approximate for realizer/producer (RDA uses typed role properties)
- **Expression derivations** (8): abridgement, arrangement, revision, translation — all **direct matches**
- **Work/Expression derivations** (14): adaption, complement, imitation, supplement, successor, transformation, summarization — **direct where RDA has typed equivalents**; gaps for Work-level imitation and all summarization (deprecated in RDA 5.4.9)
- **Manifestation** (4): alternate (**no match**), reproduction (**approximate**)
- **Item** (2): reconfiguration (**no match**)
- **Generic** (3): `relatedEndeavour`, `responsibleEntity`, inverse — no RDA equivalents

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/gen_import_terms.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"\ngen_import_terms.py\n\nGenerate import term lists for ROBOT from the FRBR/FaBiO → RDA mapping CSV.\n\nReads:\n  output/frbr-fabio-to-rda.csv\n\nWrites:\n  mocho-odk/imports/frbr_terms.txt   — FRBR property IRIs (direct matches) + FRBR WEMI class IRIs\n  mocho-odk/imports/fabio_terms.txt  — FaBiO property IRIs (direct matches) + FaBiO WEMI class IRIs\n  mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt    — RDA property IRIs (direct matches) + RDA WEMI class IRIs\n\nNamespaces:\n  FRBR properties/classes : http://purl.org/vocab/frbr/core#\n  FaBiO properties        : http://purl.org/spar/fabio/\n  FaBiO WEMI classes      : http://purl.org/spar/fabio/\n  RDA properties          : https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/{w,e,m,i}/\n  RDA WEMI classes        : https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/\n\nUsage:\n  python scripts/gen_import_terms.py [--csv output/frbr-fabio-to-rda.csv] [--out-dir mocho-odk/imports]\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport re\nimport xml.etree.ElementTree as ET\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Namespace / IRI helpers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nFRBR_NS  = \"http://purl.org/vocab/frbr/core#\"\nFABIO_NS = \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/\"\nRDA_BASE = \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/\"\n\nRDA_PREFIX_MAP = {\n    \"rdaw\": RDA_BASE + \"w/\",\n    \"rdae\": RDA_BASE + \"e/\",\n    \"rdam\": RDA_BASE + \"m/\",\n    \"rdai\": RDA_BASE + \"i/\",\n}\n\nFRBR_WEMI_CLASSES = [\n    FRBR_NS + \"Work\",\n    FRBR_NS + \"Expression\",\n    FRBR_NS + \"Manifestation\",\n    FRBR_NS + \"Item\",\n]\n\nFABIO_WEMI_CLASSES = [\n    FABIO_NS + \"Work\",\n    FABIO_NS + \"Expression\",\n    FABIO_NS + \"Manifestation\",\n    FABIO_NS + \"Item\",\n]\n\n\ndef rda_prop_to_iri(cell: str) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract IRI(s) from a cell like 'rdaw:P10078 \\\"has expression of work\\\"'.\"\"\"\n    iris = []\n    for token in cell.split(\";\"):\n        token = token.strip()\n        m = re.match(r'(rda[wemi]):(\\w+)', token)\n        if m:\n            prefix, local = m.groups()\n            base = RDA_PREFIX_MAP.get(prefix)\n            if base:\n                iris.append(base + local)\n    return iris\n\n\ndef frbr_prop_to_iri(local: str) -> str:\n    return FRBR_NS + local.split(\":\")[-1]\n\n\ndef fabio_prop_to_iri(local: str) -> str:\n    return FABIO_NS + local.split(\":\")[-1]\n\n\ndef get_rda_wemi_classes(c_xml: Path) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Parse c.xml and return IRIs for Work, Expression, Manifestation, Item.\"\"\"\n    targets = {\"Work\", \"Expression\", \"Manifestation\", \"Item\"}\n    found = {}\n    tree = ET.parse(c_xml)\n    for cls in tree.findall(\".//{http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#}Class\"):\n        iri = cls.get(\"{http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#}about\")\n        if not iri:\n            continue\n        for el in cls.iter():\n            lang = el.get(\"{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}lang\", \"\")\n            if lang == \"en\" and el.text and el.text.strip() in targets:\n                found[el.text.strip()] = iri\n                break\n    # Return in WEMI order\n    return [found[k] for k in (\"Work\", \"Expression\", \"Manifestation\", \"Item\") if k in found]\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Main\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--csv\", default=\"output/frbr-fabio-to-rda.csv\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--out-dir\", default=\"mocho-odk/imports\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--c-xml\",\n                        default=\"ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements/c.xml\")\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    csv_path = Path(args.csv)\n    out_dir  = Path(args.out_dir)\n    c_xml    = Path(args.c_xml)\n    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    frbr_props  = []\n    fabio_props = []\n    rda_props   = []\n\n    with open(csv_path, newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n        for row in reader:\n            if row[\"match\"].strip() != \"direct\":\n                continue\n            src = row[\"source_property\"].strip()\n            rda = row[\"rda_property\"].strip()\n\n            if src.startswith(\"frbr:\"):\n                frbr_props.append(frbr_prop_to_iri(src))\n            elif src.startswith(\"fabio:\"):\n                fabio_props.append(fabio_prop_to_iri(src))\n\n            rda_props.extend(rda_prop_to_iri(rda))\n\n    # Deduplicate, preserve order\n    def dedup(lst):\n        seen = set()\n        out = []\n        for x in lst:\n            if x not in seen:\n                seen.add(x)\n                out.append(x)\n        return out\n\n    frbr_props = dedup(frbr_props)\n    fabio_props = dedup(fabio_props)\n    rda_props   = dedup(rda_props)\n\n    # Fetch RDA WEMI class IRIs from c.xml\n    rda_wemi = get_rda_wemi_classes(c_xml) if c_xml.exists() else []\n\n    def write_terms(path: Path, props: list[str], classes: list[str]):\n        lines = classes + props\n        path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        print(f\"Wrote {len(lines)} IRIs ({len(classes)} classes + {len(props)} properties) → {path}\")\n\n    write_terms(out_dir / \"frbr_terms.txt\",  frbr_props,  FRBR_WEMI_CLASSES)\n    write_terms(out_dir / \"fabio_terms.txt\", fabio_props, FABIO_WEMI_CLASSES)\n    write_terms(out_dir / \"rda_terms.txt\",   rda_props,   rda_wemi)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```
