### Assistant *(2026-03-04T15:00:40.704Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/dct_rda_map.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"\ndct_rda_map.py\n\nRead a CSV produced by extract_ontology_terms.py, collect every term whose label\nstarts with \"dc:\" or \"dcterms:\", look each one up in the RDA → DCT sub-property\nmap (mapElement2DCT.xml), and write a flat mapping table of\n    dct_term → RDA property IRI + label.\n\n\"dc:\" labels are resolved against the dcterms: namespace\n(http://purl.org/dc/terms/) because mapElement2DCT.xml only declares dcterms:\nsuper-properties. Both the original prefix (dc: or dcterms:) and the normalised\ndcterms: IRI are recorded in the output so the source context is preserved.\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/dct_rda_map.py\n    python scripts/dct_rda_map.py --input output/ddbedm_1.csv\n    python scripts/dct_rda_map.py \\\\\n        --input output/ddbedm_1.csv \\\\\n        --map-xml ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Maps/mapElement2DCT.xml \\\\\n        --vocab-dir ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements \\\\\n        --output output/mapping_dct_to_rda\n\nInputs:\n    --input      CSV with a \"label\" column (output of extract_ontology_terms.py).\n    --map-xml    mapElement2DCT.xml from an RDA-Vocabularies release.\n    --vocab-dir  Elements/ directory of the same release (for RDA property labels).\n\nOutputs:\n    PREFIX.csv   dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label — one row per (dct_term, RDA property) pair.\n    PREFIX.json  Same rows as a JSON array.\n\nDependencies:\n    Python standard library only (xml.etree.ElementTree, argparse, csv, json, pathlib).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport xml.etree.ElementTree as ET\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Namespace constants\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nDCT_NS  = \"http://purl.org/dc/terms/\"\nDC_NS   = \"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\"\nRDF_NS  = \"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#\"\nRDFS_NS = \"http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#\"\n\n# Entity-code prefixes used in IRI paths, mapped to their entity XML filename stems.\nENTITY_CODES = [\"w\", \"e\", \"m\", \"i\", \"a\", \"n\", \"p\", \"t\", \"x\",\n                \"c\", \"rof\", \"u\", \"z\"]\n\nFIELDNAMES = [\"dct_term\", \"rda_iri\", \"rda_label\"]\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 1 — Parse mapElement2DCT.xml: build dct_iri → [rda_iri, ...] index\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef parse_dct_map(map_xml: Path) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse mapElement2DCT.xml and return an inverted index:\n        dcterms: IRI  →  list of RDA property IRIs that declare it as rdfs:subPropertyOf\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = ET.parse(map_xml)\n    root = tree.getroot()\n\n    ns = {\"rdf\": RDF_NS, \"rdfs\": RDFS_NS}\n    index: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n\n    for desc in root.findall(\"rdf:Description\", ns):\n        rda_iri = desc.get(f\"{{{RDF_NS}}}about\")\n        if not rda_iri:\n            continue\n        for sp in desc.findall(\"rdfs:subPropertyOf\", ns):\n            dct_iri = sp.get(f\"{{{RDF_NS}}}resource\")\n            if dct_iri and dct_iri.startswith(DCT_NS):\n                index[dct_iri].append(rda_iri)\n\n    return dict(index)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 2 — Parse RDA vocabulary XMLs: build rda_iri → English label index\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef build_rda_label_index(vocab_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse every entity XML file in vocab_dir and return\n        rda_iri  →  English rdfs:label string\n    Only english-tagged labels (xml:lang=\"en\") are collected; if absent the\n    label is left as an empty string.\n    \"\"\"\n    label_index: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for stem in ENTITY_CODES:\n        xml_path = vocab_dir / f\"{stem}.xml\"\n        if not xml_path.exists():\n            continue\n        tree = ET.parse(xml_path)\n        root = tree.getroot()\n        for desc in root.iter(f\"{{{RDF_NS}}}Description\"):\n            iri = desc.get(f\"{{{RDF_NS}}}about\")\n            if not iri:\n                continue\n            label = \"\"\n            for lbl in desc.findall(f\"{{{RDFS_NS}}}label\"):\n                if lbl.get(\"{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}lang\") == \"en\":\n                    label = (lbl.text or \"\").strip()\n                    break\n            label_index[iri] = label\n    return label_index\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 3 — Read input CSV and collect dc:/dcterms: labels\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef collect_dct_labels(input_csv: Path) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Read the CSV and return every unique value in the 'label' column\n    whose prefix is 'dc:' or 'dcterms:'.\n    \"\"\"\n    terms: list[str] = []\n    seen: set[str] = set()\n    with open(input_csv, newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n            lbl = row.get(\"label\", \"\").strip()\n            if lbl.startswith(\"dc:\") or lbl.startswith(\"dcterms:\"):\n                if lbl not in seen:\n                    terms.append(lbl)\n                    seen.add(lbl)\n    return terms\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 4 — Resolve label to dcterms: lookup IRI\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef to_dct_iri(label: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert a dc: or dcterms: prefixed label to its dcterms: IRI.\n    dc:X → http://purl.org/dc/terms/X  (dc/elements/1.1 terms are treated as\n                                         their dcterms equivalents because\n                                         mapElement2DCT.xml only lists dcterms:)\n    dcterms:X → http://purl.org/dc/terms/X\n    \"\"\"\n    if label.startswith(\"dcterms:\"):\n        local = label[len(\"dcterms:\"):]\n    else:  # dc:\n        local = label[len(\"dc:\"):]\n    return DCT_NS + local\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 5 — Build output rows\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef build_rows(\n    terms: list[str],\n    dct_index: dict[str, list[str]],\n    label_index: dict[str, str],\n) -> list[dict]:\n    rows: list[dict] = []\n    for term in terms:\n        dct_iri = to_dct_iri(term)\n        rda_iris = dct_index.get(dct_iri, [])\n        if not rda_iris:\n            continue\n        for rda_iri in sorted(rda_iris):\n            rows.append({\n                \"dct_term\":  term,\n                \"rda_iri\":   rda_iri,\n                \"rda_label\": label_index.get(rda_iri, \"\"),\n            })\n    rows.sort(key=lambda r: (r[\"dct_term\"], r[\"rda_iri\"]))\n    return rows\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Output writers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef write_csv(rows: list[dict], path: Path) -> None:\n    with open(path, \"w\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=FIELDNAMES)\n        writer.writeheader()\n        writer.writerows(rows)\n\n\ndef write_json(rows: list[dict], path: Path) -> None:\n    path.write_text(json.dumps(rows, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Main\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--input\", \"-i\",\n        default=\"output/ddbedm_1.csv\",\n        metavar=\"CSV\",\n        help=\"Input CSV with a 'label' column (default: output/ddbedm_1.csv).\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--map-xml\",\n        default=\"ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Maps/mapElement2DCT.xml\",\n        metavar=\"XML\",\n        help=\"Path to mapElement2DCT.xml.\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--vocab-dir\",\n        default=\"ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements\",\n        metavar=\"DIR\",\n        help=\"RDA Elements/ directory containing entity XML files for label lookup.\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--output\", \"-o\",\n        default=\"output/mapping_dct_to_rda\",\n        metavar=\"PREFIX\",\n        help=\"Output file prefix; writes PREFIX.csv and PREFIX.json.\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    input_csv = Path(args.input)\n    map_xml   = Path(args.map_xml)\n    vocab_dir = Path(args.vocab_dir)\n    out_pfx   = Path(args.output)\n\n    for p, name in [(input_csv, \"input CSV\"), (map_xml, \"map XML\"), (vocab_dir, \"vocab dir\")]:\n        if not p.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"{name} not found: {p}\")\n\n    # Parse sources\n    dct_index   = parse_dct_map(map_xml)\n    label_index = build_rda_label_index(vocab_dir)\n    terms       = collect_dct_labels(input_csv)\n\n    print(f\"DCT terms found in {input_csv.name}: {len(terms)}\")\n    print(f\"DCT terms with RDA mappings in XML: \"\n          f\"{sum(1 for t in terms if to_dct_iri(t) in dct_index)}\")\n\n    rows = build_rows(terms, dct_index, label_index)\n\n    out_pfx.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    csv_path  = out_pfx.with_suffix(\".csv\")\n    json_path = out_pfx.with_suffix(\".json\")\n    write_csv(rows, csv_path)\n    write_json(rows, json_path)\n\n    print(f\"Wrote {len(rows)} rows → {csv_path}\")\n    print(f\"Wrote {len(rows)} rows → {json_path}\")\n\n    # Per-term summary\n    from collections import Counter\n    counts = Counter(r[\"dct_term\"] for r in rows)\n    for term, n in sorted(counts.items()):\n        print(f\"  {term}: {n} RDA properties\")\n\n    # Terms with no match\n    no_match = [t for t in terms if to_dct_iri(t) not in dct_index]\n    if no_match:\n        print(f\"\\nNo RDA mapping found for ({len(no_match)} terms):\")\n        for t in no_match:\n            print(f\"  {t}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

### User *(2026-03-04T15:01:11.452Z)*

**Tool result**
```
   196→## scripts/gen_vra_mapping.py
   197→
   198→**Purpose**: Generate the VRA Core 4.0 → RDA mapping tables (CSV and JSON). VRA Core's class hierarchy uses `schema:CreativeWork` and `foaf:Agent` — not FRBR — so the automated `gen_frbr_target_mapping.py` produces no output for it. This script provides the hand-curated alignment following the same static ROWS pattern as `gen_nonwemi_mapping.py`. Covers 7 VRA classes and 65 properties across creator roles, part-whole, derivative, subject, place, identification, and physical/technical groups; 48 approximate mappings and 24 no-match entries.
   199→
   200→**Usage**:
   201→```bash
   202→# Default output (output/mapping_vra_to_rda.{csv,json}):
   203→python scripts/gen_vra_mapping.py
   204→
   205→# Custom output prefix:
   206→python scripts/gen_vra_mapping.py --output output/mapping_vra_to_rda
   207→```
   208→
   209→**Options**:
   210→| Flag | Default | Description |
   211→|------|---------|-------------|
   212→| `--output` | `output/mapping_vra_to_rda` | Output file prefix; writes `PREFIX.csv` and `PREFIX.json` |
   213→
   214→**Inputs**: None — all mapping data is embedded in the `ROWS` list.
   215→
   216→**Outputs**:
   217→- `PREFIX.csv` — 72 rows; columns: `source, rda, domain, range, match, remarks`
   218→- `PREFIX.json` — same rows; `rda` field as a list (split on `;`)
   219→- stdout — row count, output paths, and match-level summary (direct / approximate / no match)
   220→
   221→**Dependencies**: Python standard library only (`argparse`, `csv`, `json`, `pathlib`, `collections.Counter`).
   222→
   223→**Caveats**: The mapping is hand-curated against VRA Core 4.0 (`ontology/vra-core-v4.0.owl`). VRA's 30+ creator-role properties all collapse to `rdaw:P10065` has creator agent of work; role granularity is lost. Event-mediated date properties (`wasCreated`, `wasProduced`, etc.) have no RDA structural equivalent and are marked no match. See `docs/vra-frbr-mapping.md`, `docs/vra-crm-mapping.md`, `docs/vra-fabio-mapping.md`, and `docs/vra-rda-mapping.md` for the full alignment analysis.
   224→
   225→



```

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/README.md",
  "old_string": "## scripts/gen_vra_mapping.py",
  "new_string": "## scripts/dct_rda_map.py\n\n**Purpose**: Read a CSV produced by `extract_ontology_terms.py`, collect every term whose `label` column starts with `dc:` or `dcterms:`, look up the corresponding RDA sub-properties in `mapElement2DCT.xml` (from the RDA-Vocabularies release), and write a flat mapping table. `dc:` labels are resolved against the `dcterms:` namespace because `mapElement2DCT.xml` only declares `dcterms:` super-properties; both the original prefix and the resolved IRI are preserved in the output. RDA property labels are fetched from the entity XML files in the same release.\n\n**Usage**:\n```bash\n# Defaults (reads output/ddbedm_1.csv, writes output/mapping_dct_to_rda.{csv,json}):\npython scripts/dct_rda_map.py\n\n# Custom input and output:\npython scripts/dct_rda_map.py \\\n    --input output/ddbedm_1.csv \\\n    --output output/mapping_dct_to_rda\n```\n\n**Options**:\n| Flag | Default | Description |\n|------|---------|-------------|\n| `--input` / `-i` | `output/ddbedm_1.csv` | CSV with a `label` column (output of `extract_ontology_terms.py`) |\n| `--map-xml` | `ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Maps/mapElement2DCT.xml` | Path to `mapElement2DCT.xml` |\n| `--vocab-dir` | `ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements` | RDA Elements directory for label lookup |\n| `--output` / `-o` | `output/mapping_dct_to_rda` | Output file prefix; writes `PREFIX.csv` and `PREFIX.json` |\n\n**Inputs**:\n- `--input` CSV: must have a `label` column (as written by `extract_ontology_terms.py`)\n- `mapElement2DCT.xml`: maps RDA property IRIs → `dcterms:` super-properties via `rdfs:subPropertyOf`\n- Entity XML files (`w.xml`, `e.xml`, `m.xml`, `i.xml`, `a.xml`, …): source of English `rdfs:label` values for RDA properties\n\n**Outputs**:\n- `PREFIX.csv` — columns: `dct_term`, `rda_iri`, `rda_label`; one row per (dct_term, RDA property) pair\n- `PREFIX.json` — same rows as a JSON array\n- stdout — count of dc:/dcterms: terms found and matched, per-term property counts, and terms with no RDA mapping\n\n**Dependencies**: Python standard library only (`xml.etree.ElementTree`, `argparse`, `csv`, `json`, `pathlib`, `collections`).\n\n**Caveats**: `mapElement2DCT.xml` only covers `dcterms:` property IRIs (not `dc:elements/1.1`); `dc:X` labels are looked up as `dcterms:X`. DCT class terms (`dcterms:LinguisticSystem`, `dcterms:PeriodOfTime`, etc.) appear in the no-match list because the map contains only property sub-property relations. Broad DCT terms like `dcterms:contributor` or `dcterms:creator` expand to hundreds of RDA sub-properties; use the output as a starting point for selecting the most specific applicable property.\n\n---\n\n## scripts/gen_vra_mapping.py"
}

**New script: [scripts/dct_rda_map.py](mocho/scripts/dct_rda_map.py)**
- Reads any `extract_ontology_terms.py` CSV, collects `dc:` and `dcterms:` labels
- Inverts `mapElement2DCT.xml` (RDA→DCT) to a DCT→RDA lookup

### Assistant *(2026-03-04T15:45:36.355Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/notes/mapping_dct_rda_workflow.md",
  "content": "# Workflow: Generating RDA Terms from DCT Mappings in DDB EDM\n\n## Context\n\nThe Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek (DDB) publishes its metadata profile as an extension\nof the Europeana Data Model (EDM) in `ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl`. The DDB EDM ontology\nreuses Dublin Core properties (`dc:`, `dcterms:`) as object properties applied to EDM\nentities. The goal of this workflow is to trace those DC/DCT properties to their\ncorresponding RDA sub-properties and include those RDA IRIs in the MIREOT import list\n(`mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt`).\n\nThe mapping chain is:\n\n```\nddbedm_1.0.ttl\n    ↓  extract_ontology_terms.py\noutput/ddbedm_1.csv          (dc: / dcterms: labels extracted)\n    ↓  dct_rda_map.py\noutput/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv  (DCT term → RDA sub-property pairs)\n    ↓  manual append (grep -vFf dedup)\nmocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt\n```\n\n---\n\n## Step 1 — Extract vocabulary terms from DDB EDM\n\n```bash\npython scripts/extract_ontology_terms.py \\\n    --ontology ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl \\\n    --output output/ddbedm_1\n```\n\n**Output:** `output/ddbedm_1.csv` — 98 terms (33 classes, 41 object properties,\n12 datatype properties, 12 annotation properties).\n\n**Key behaviour:** When a term has no `rdfs:label`, the script falls back to\n`g.qname(uri)`, which yields prefixed names like `dc:creator`, `dcterms:subject`,\n`edm:ProvidedCHO`, `crm:E39_Actor`, etc. Terms whose namespace prefix is not bound\nin the graph fall back further to the bare local name.\n\nThe `dc:` and `dcterms:` labels arise from the graph's built-in namespace bindings\nfor `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/` (`dc:`) and `http://purl.org/dc/terms/`\n(`dcterms:`), which are declared in the Turtle file's `@prefix` block.\n\n**DC/DCT terms found in ddbedm_1.csv (34 total):**\n\n| Prefix | Terms |\n|---|---|\n| `dc:` | `contributor`, `coverage`, `creator`, `date`, `description`, `format`, `language`, `publisher`, `relation`, `subject`, `title`, `type` |\n| `dcterms:` | `alternative`, `bibliographicCitation`, `created`, `date`, `extent`, `hasPart`, `isPartOf`, `issued`, `language`, `medium`, `provenance`, `rights`, `spatial`, `subject`, `temporal` |\n| `dcterms:` (classes) | `LinguisticSystem`, `LocationPeriodOrJurisdiction`, `PeriodOfTime`, `PhysicalMedium`, `PhysicalResource`, `ProvenanceStatement`, `RightsStatement` |\n\n---\n\n## Step 2 — Map DCT terms to RDA sub-properties\n\n```bash\npython scripts/dct_rda_map.py \\\n    --input output/ddbedm_1.csv \\\n    --map-xml ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Maps/mapElement2DCT.xml \\\n    --vocab-dir ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements \\\n    --output output/mapping_dct_to_rda\n```\n\n**Outputs:** `output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv` / `.json`\n\n**Schema:** `dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label` — one row per (source term, RDA property)\npair.\n\n### How it works\n\n`mapElement2DCT.xml` is published with the RDA Vocabularies release. Each entry\ndeclares an RDA property as a sub-property of a DCT term:\n\n```xml\n<rdf:Description rdf:about=\"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/P10051\">\n  <rdfs:subPropertyOf rdf:resource=\"http://purl.org/dc/terms/creator\"/>\n</rdf:Description>\n```\n\nThe script **inverts** this map: for each DCT IRI it collects all RDA properties\nthat declare it as their super-property.\n\n**`dc:` prefix resolution:** `mapElement2DCT.xml` only contains `dcterms:`\n(`http://purl.org/dc/terms/`) IRIs. `dc:elements/1.1` properties (e.g. `dc:creator`)\nare looked up via their `dcterms:` equivalent (`dcterms:creator`). The original\nprefix is preserved in the output `dct_term` column, so `dc:creator` and\n`dcterms:creator` appear as distinct rows with the same set of RDA sub-properties.\n\n**RDA labels** are resolved from the entity XML files (`w.xml`, `e.xml`, `m.xml`,\n`i.xml`, `a.xml`, …) using `rdfs:label xml:lang=\"en\"`.\n\n### Results\n\n| Outcome | Count |\n|---|---|\n| DC/DCT terms with ≥1 RDA mapping | 23 |\n| DC/DCT terms with no RDA mapping | 11 |\n| Total (dct_term, RDA IRI) pairs | 979 |\n\n**Not mapped — DCT class terms (7):** Classes have no entries in `mapElement2DCT.xml`\nbecause it only maps properties. `dcterms:LinguisticSystem`,\n`dcterms:LocationPeriodOrJurisdiction`, `dcterms:PeriodOfTime`,\n`dcterms:PhysicalMedium`, `dcterms:PhysicalResource`, `dcterms:ProvenanceStatement`,\n`dcterms:RightsStatement`.\n\n**Not mapped — properties absent from XML (4):** `dcterms:bibliographicCitation`,\n`dcterms:provenance`, `dcterms:spatial`, `dcterms:temporal` are not listed as\nsuper-properties of any RDA property in the 5.4.9 release.\n\n### High-fanout terms\n\nBroad DCT terms expand to large numbers of RDA sub-properties:\n\n| DCT term | RDA sub-properties |\n|---|---|\n| `dc:contributor` / `dcterms:contributor` | 360 (each) |\n| `dc:creator` / `dcterms:creator` | 232 (each) |\n| `dc:publisher` / `dcterms:publisher` | 96 (each) |\n| `dc:format` / `dcterms:format` | 59 (each) |\n| `dc:description` / `dcterms:description` | 46 (each) |\n| `dc:subject` / `dcterms:subject` | 42 (each) |\n\nThese counts reflect the RDA registry's full sub-property hierarchy for each DCT\nterm, spanning all WEMI levels and role specialisations. The output CSV serves as a\nstarting-point vocabulary; implementers should select the most specific applicable\nRDA property for each metadata element.\n\n---\n\n## Step 3 — Append new RDA IRIs to rda_terms.txt\n\n```bash\ntail -n +2 output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv \\\n  | cut -d',' -f2 \\\n  | sort -u \\\n  | grep -vFf mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt \\\n  >> mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt\n```\n\nThis extracts the `rda_iri` column, deduplicates, and appends only IRIs not already\npresent. The `-vFf` flag treats existing file lines as fixed strings to exclude.\n\n**Result:**\n\n| Metric | Value |\n|---|---|\n| IRIs before this step | 220 |\n| New IRIs added from DCT mapping | 852 |\n| Total IRIs after | 1072 |\n| Duplicate IRIs | 0 |\n\n---\n\n## How rda_terms.txt fits into the broader pipeline\n\n`mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt` accumulates RDA property and class IRIs from all\nmapping files produced by the mocho scripts. It is the input to the ODK/ROBOT MIREOT\nstep that generates `mocho-odk/imports/rda_import.owl`, importing selected RDA terms\ninto the mocho ontology.\n\nThe file is populated from two distinct sources:\n\n**Source A — `gen_all_import_terms.py`** (lines 1–220, run earlier):\nScans `output/mapping_*_to_*.csv` files with a `match` column, collects all `rda`\nor `rda_uri` values with match = `direct` or `approximate`, and writes them to\n`rda_terms.txt` (and other `*_terms.txt` files). Covers ACO, CRM, FaBiO, FaBiO\nnon-WEMI, FRBR, MO, RiC-O, and VRA mappings.\n\n**Source B — manual DCT append** (lines 221–1072, this workflow):\nThe `mapping_dct_to_rda.csv` uses a different schema (`dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label`\nwith no `match` column), so `gen_all_import_terms.py` silently skips it. The DCT\nRDA IRIs must be appended manually after running `dct_rda_map.py`.\n\nTo re-run the full DCT → `rda_terms.txt` update from scratch:\n\n```bash\n# 1. Regenerate term extract from DDB EDM\npython scripts/extract_ontology_terms.py --ontology ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl\n\n# 2. Regenerate DCT→RDA mapping\npython scripts/dct_rda_map.py\n\n# 3. Append new RDA IRIs (dedup against existing file)\ntail -n +2 output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv \\\n  | cut -d',' -f2 \\\n  | sort -u \\\n  | grep -vFf mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt \\\n  >> mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt\n```\n\n---\n\n## Known gaps and follow-up actions\n\n- **`gen_all_import_terms.py` does not handle the DCT schema.** To fully automate\n  this step, extend the script with a handler for files matching\n  `mapping_dct_to_rda.csv` (column name `rda_iri` instead of `rda`).\n\n- **dc:class terms have no RDA class equivalents** in `rda_terms.txt`. DCT class\n  IRIs like `dcterms:LinguisticSystem` map conceptually to RDA value vocabulary\n  concepts, not to RDA entity classes; they are intentionally excluded.\n\n- **High-fanout terms** (`dcterms:contributor`, `dcterms:creator`) will import\n  hundreds of RDA properties into mocho. Review the import list before running\n  ROBOT to confirm the scope is intentional.\n\n- **`dcterms:spatial`, `dcterms:temporal`, `dcterms:provenance`** are used by DDB\n  EDM but have no RDA sub-property declarations in the 5.4.9 release. They may\n  require manual mapping or a later RDA version.\n"
}
```

## Approved Plan:
# Plan: Run extract_ontology_terms.py on ddbedm-full.owl

## Context
The user wants to extract all declared classes and properties from the DDB EDM full
ontology (`ddbedm-full.owl`) using the existing `extract_ontology_terms.py` script.
The ontology is an RDF/XML OWL file (48 KB, version 2026-03-09) in a separate ODK
project at `~/Documents/claude/ddbedm-odk/ddbedm/ddbedm-full.owl`.

## Command

Run from the mocho project root (where the script lives):

```bash
cd /Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho
python scripts/extract_ontology_terms.py \
    --ontology /Users/mta/Documents/claude/ddbedm-odk/ddbedm/ddbedm-full.owl
```

Default output prefix is `output/<stem>`, so this writes to:
- `mocho/output/ddbedm-full.csv`
- `mocho/output/ddbedm-full.json`

## Critical files
- Script: `mocho/scripts/extract_ontology_terms.py`
- Input: `~/Documents/claude/ddbedm-odk/ddbedm/ddbedm-full.owl`
- Output: `mocho/output/ddbedm-full.csv` / `mocho/output/ddbedm-full.json`

## Verification
Check stdout for triple count, term count, and type breakdown. Spot-check the CSV
for `dc:`, `dcterms:`, `edm:`, `ddb:`, and `crm:` prefixed labels.

```

Done. Output written to `mocho/output/ddbedm-full.csv` and `mocho/output/ddbedm-full.json`.

**92 terms** from 401 triples:
- 45 classes
- 27 object properties
- 11 annotation properties
- 9 datatype properties

Notable: `dcterms:` classes now have definitions pulled in (e.g. `dcterms:LinguisticSystem`, `dcterms:ProvenanceStatement`).

### Assistant *(2026-03-09T15:11:30.807Z)*

**Tool call:** `Bash`
```json
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'EOF'\nimport csv\n\nA_PREFIXES = (\"edm:\", \"ddb:\", \"dcterms:\", \"dc:\", \"geo:\")\nB_PREFIXES = (\"crm:\",)\n\na_iris = []\nb_iris = []\n\nwith open(\"/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/output/ddbedm-full.csv\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        lbl = row[\"label\"].strip()\n        iri = row[\"iri\"].strip()\n        if any(lbl.startswith(p) for p in A_PREFIXES):\n            a_iris.append(iri)\n        elif any(lbl.startswith(p) for p in B_PREFIXES):\n            b_iris.append(iri)\n\nout = \"/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/imports/edm_terms.txt\"\nwith open(out, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n    f.write(\"\\n\".join(a_iris) + \"\\n\")\n    f.write(\"\\n\".join(b_iris) + \"\\n\")\n\nprint(f\"A (edm/ddb/dcterms/dc/geo): {len(a_iris)}\")\nprint(f\"B (crm, appended):          {len(b_iris)}\")\nprint(f\"Total: {len(a_iris) + len(b_iris)}\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Write edm_terms.txt with A prefixes then append B (crm)"
}
```

     1→##
     2→## mocho-odk.yaml
     3→## ODK project configuration for the Middle Ontology for Cultural Heritage Objects (MOCHO)
     4→##
     5→## Documentation: https://github.com/INCATools/ontology-development-kit/blob/master/docs/project-schema.md
     6→## Seed command:  bash seed-via-docker.sh -C mocho-odk.yaml
     7→##
     8→
     9→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→# Project identity
    11→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→id: mocho
    14→title: "Middle Ontology for Cultural Heritage Objects"
    15→description: >
    16→  A middle ontology bridging the Europeana Data Model (EDM) as top-level
    17→  ontology with domain ontologies: RDA, Records in Contexts Ontology (RiC-O),
    18→  Music Ontology, Audio Commons Ontology (ACO), and CIDOC CRM via LRMoo/FRBRoo.
    19→  The WEMI model (Work, Expression, Manifestation, Item) serves as the central
    20→  conceptual framework, with RDA entity classes as the canonical middle-layer.
    21→
    22→github_org: ise-fizkarlsruhe
    23→repo: mocho
    24→git_main_branch: main
    25→
    26→# Ontology IRI base.  Terms use the namespace mocho: <uribase/#>
    27→uribase: https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho
    28→
    29→license: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/deed.en
    30→
    31→# ORCID or GitHub handle of the primary maintainer
    32→contact: TODO
    33→
    34→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    35→# OBO Foundry settings
    36→# mocho is not an OBO Foundry ontology; suppress PURL and registry metadata.
    37→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→
    39→create_obo_metadata: false
    40→
    41→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42→# Edit file and serialisation
    43→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44→
    45→edit_format: owl              # Source edit file in OWL functional syntax
    46→import_component_format: ofn  # Serialise import modules as OWL Functional
    47→
    48→export_formats:
    49→  - owl
    50→  - ttl
    51→
    52→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    53→# Reasoning
    54→# CIDOC CRM and LRMoo use OWL 2 DL constructs (nominals, inverse properties,
    55→# self-restrictions) that are outside OWL EL.  Use HermiT for correctness.
    56→# Switch to ELK only if the build is restricted to an EL subset.
    57→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58→
    59→reasoner: HermiT
    60→
    61→robot_java_args: "-Xmx8G"
    62→
    63→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→# Release configuration
    65→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    66→
    67→release_artefacts:
    68→  - full    # merged, reasoned release with all imports inlined
    69→  - base    # axioms about mocho-native terms only, without imports
    70→
    71→primary_release: full
    72→
    73→ensure_valid_rdfxml: true
    74→
    75→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    76→# Namespaces considered home to this ontology
    77→# Used by ROBOT filter and report commands to distinguish native vs. external terms.
    78→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    79→
    80→namespaces:
    81→  - https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#
    82→
    83→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→# Import group
    85→#
    86→# All source ontologies are locally mirrored; mirror_from uses file:// URIs
    87→# so the ODK Makefile's curl step copies from the local ontology/ directory.
    88→# module_type: mirror uses the mirrored file directly as the import module
    89→# (no SLME seed extraction), appropriate for full external vocabularies.
    90→#
    91→# NOTE: mirror_from paths are absolute.  If the project root moves, update
    92→# them here or replace with Makefile variables via a custom mocho.Makefile.
    93→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    94→
    95→import_group:
    96→  products:
    97→
    98→    ## -----------------------------------------------------------------------

   139→    ## rda_all.owl is an OWL wrapper that declares owl:imports for all RDA
   140→    ## element namespaces:
   141→    ##   Elements/{a,c,e,i,m,n,p,t,u,w,x}/ and their /datatype/ and /object/
   142→    ##   sub-groups — resolving to 144 XML files in xml/Elements/.
   143→    ## The IRI-to-file mapping is in RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/catalog-v001.xml.
   144→    ##
   145→    ## mirror_type: custom — ODK will NOT auto-generate a curl recipe.
   146→    ## A custom Makefile target in mocho.Makefile must produce
   147→    ## $(MIRRORDIR)/rda.owl by running ROBOT with the RDA catalog, e.g.:
   148→    ##
   149→    ##   $(MIRRORDIR)/rda.owl: | $(MIRRORDIR)
   150→    ##       $(ROBOT) merge \
   151→    ##           --catalog /path/to/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/catalog-v001.xml \
   152→    ##           --input /path/to/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/rda_all.owl \
   153→    ##           --output $@
   154→    ##
   155→    ## Maps/ and termList/ subdirectories are SKOS/CSV value-vocabulary files,
   156→    ## not OWL ontologies, and are intentionally excluded.
   157→    ## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   158→    #- id: rda
   159→    #  description: "RDA Vocabularies 5.4.9 — all element namespaces (Work, Expression, Manifestation, Item, Agent, Nomen, Place, Timespan properties and sub-types)"
   160→    #  mirror_type: custom
   161→    #  module_type: mirror
   162→    #  robot_settings:
   163→    #    memory_gb: 6
   164→    ## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   165→    ## RiC-O — Records in Contexts Ontology v1.1
   166→    ## (https://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#)
   167→    ## Source: ontology/RiC-O_1-1.rdf
   168→    ## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   169→    #- id: rico
   170→    #  description: "Records in Contexts Ontology v1.1"
   171→    #  mirror_from: "file:///Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/RiC-O_1-1.rdf"
   172→    #  module_type: mirror
   173→
   174→    ## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   175→    ## ACO — Audio Commons Ontology  (https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#)
   176→    ## Source: ontology/aco.owl
   177→    ## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   178→    #- id: aco
   179→    #  description: "Audio Commons Ontology"
   180→    #  mirror_from: "file:///Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/aco.owl"
   181→    # module_type: mirror
   182→
   183→    ## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   184→    ## Music Ontology  (http://purl.org/ontology/mo/)
   185→    ## Source: ontology/musicontology.n3
   186→    ##
   187→    ## Serialised as N3 (extended Turtle).  ROBOT reads it via the OWLAPI/Rio
   188→    ## RDF4J parser; the mirror step saves it as imports/mo.owl (content is

    84→
    85→### Why
    86→SLME BOT preserves annotation *assertions* on extracted terms automatically. The `annotation_properties` field additionally forces the annotation property *declarations* (`owl:AnnotationProperty` axioms) into the module — necessary for validators and reasoners that check property declarations.
    87→

    92→
    93→### CRM — 6 annotation properties
    94→`CIDOC_CRM_v7.1.3.owl` declares no custom annotation properties; it uses standard vocabulary terms throughout: `rdfs:label`, `rdfs:comment`, `skos:prefLabel`, `skos:notation`, `skos:scopeNote`, `owl:versionInfo`.

Perfect! I've found substantial content. Let me compile the findings from the session transcript.

## Summary of mocho-odk.yaml Configuration from Session Transcript

Based on the session transcript at `/Users/mta/.claude/projects/-Users-mta-Documents-claude/00625bb5-5e26-4f71-b1cb-a43ec7acedb2.jsonl`, here are the key YAML configuration changes for ddbedm-odk.yaml:

### 1. Import Group Structure

The configuration uses **local file mirrors** with **SLME/BOT extraction**:

```yaml
import_group:
  products:

    # EDM — Europeana Data Model v5.2.4
    - id: edm
      mirror_from: "file:///Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/ddbedm-odk/ontology/edm_5.2.4.ttl"
      module_type: SLME
      module_type_slme: BOT
      slme_individuals: false
      terms_file: imports/edm_terms.txt

    # CIDOC CRM — v7.1.3
    - id: crm
      mirror_from: "file:///Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/ddbedm-odk/ontology/CIDOC_CRM_v7.1.3.owl"
      module_type: SLME
      module_type_slme: BOT
      slme_individuals: false
      terms_file: imports/crm_terms.txt
```

### 2. Module Type Configuration

- **`module_type: SLME`** — Semantic Lightweight Module Extraction
- **`module_type_slme: BOT`** — Bottom extraction strategy (only imports what the terms depend on)
- **`slme_individuals: false`** — Don't include named individuals from source ontologies

### 3. annotation_properties (EDM Import)

The EDM import includes 23 annotation properties to retain during extraction:

```yaml
annotation_properties:
  - http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/description
  - http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/modified
  - http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/title
  - http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued
  - http://purl.org/dc/terms/provenance
  - http://purl.org/dc/terms/temporal
  - http://purl.org/vocab/vann/changes
  - http://purl.org/vocab/vann/example
  - http://purl.org/vocab/vann/preferredNamespacePrefix
  - http://purl.org/vocab/vann/preferredNamespaceUri
  - http://purl.org/vocommons/voaf#toDoList
  - http://www.cidoc-crm.org/rdfs/cidoc-crm#P12_occurred_in_the_presence_of
  - http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/country
  - http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/dataProvider
  - http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/userTag
  - http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/year
  - http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#example
  - http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#note
  - http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#scopeNote
  - http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#relatedWebPage
  - http://www.w3.org/ns/radion#versionNotes
  - http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/homepage
  - http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/name
```

### 4. annotation_properties (CRM Import)

The CRM import includes 6 annotation properties:

```yaml
annotation_properties:
  - http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label
  - http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment
  - http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#prefLabel
  - http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#notation
  - http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#scopeNote
  - http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#versionInfo
```

### 5. Additional Imports Configured

The session also shows configuration for additional vocabulary imports:
- **DC** (Dublin Core Elements) — `imports/dc_terms.txt`
- **DCTerms** (Dublin Core Terms) — `imports/dcterms_terms.txt`
- **FRBR** (Functional Requirements for Bibliographic Records) — `imports/frbr_terms.txt`
- **OAI-ORE** (Object Reuse and Exchange) — `imports/ore_terms.txt`
- **SKOS** (Simple Knowledge Organization System) — `imports/skos_terms.txt`
- **GEO** (WGS84 Geo/Basic Geo Vocabulary) — `imports/geo_terms.txt`

All follow the same SLME/BOT pattern with local file mirrors.

### 6. Generated Makefile Rules

The transcript shows that custom Makefile rules are generated for each import with the `--term` flags to preserve annotation_properties during the ROBOT remove step, following the pattern:

```makefile
$(ROBOT) annotate --input $< --remove-annotations \
  odk:normalize --add-source true \
  extract --term-file $(IMPORTDIR)/edm_terms.txt \
  ... \
  remove $(foreach p, $(ANNOTATION_PROPERTIES), --term $(p)) \
  ... \
  odk:normalize --base-iri http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo \
  ... \
  repair --merge-axiom-annotations true
```

### 7. Key Configuration Notes

- **Local mirroring**: All imports use `file://` URIs pointing to the local `ontology/` directory instead of remote HTTP URLs
- **terms_file paths**: Each import references a corresponding `imports/*_terms.txt` file specifying which IRIs to extract
- **SLME/BOT strategy**: Bottom extraction ensures only logically dependent terms are imported, minimizing module size while preserving closure

1. Primary Request and Intent:

a. **Fix `docs/rico-rda-mapping.md`**: Correct factual errors in the existing file (wrong input ontology, version, counts, criterion codes, missing Expression for RecordSet).

d. **Update `mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt`**: Append all `rda_iri` values from `output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv` not already in the file.

e. **Create `notes/mapping_dct_rda_workflow.md`**: Document the full DCT→RDA→rda_terms.txt workflow.

f. **Run `extract_ontology_terms.py` on `ddbedm-full.owl`**: From the ddbedm-odk project at `~/Documents/claude/ddbedm-odk/ddbedm/ddbedm-full.owl`. Write to `mocho/output/ddbedm-full.{csv,json}`.

g. **Update `mocho-odk/imports/edm_terms.txt`**: From `output/ddbedm-full.csv`, dump edm:/ddb:/dcterms:/dc:/geo: IRIs (overwrite), then append crm: IRIs.

h. **Update `mocho-odk/mocho-odk.yaml`** (in progress at summary point): Based on session `00625bb5-5e26-4f71-b1cb-a43ec7acedb2` and `~/Documents/claude/ddbedm-odk/notes/session-01-setup.md`. Change `ddbedm` import from `module_type: mirror` to SLME/BOT pattern with `terms_file`, `annotation_properties`, and updated `mirror_from`.

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **ODK (Ontology Development Kit)**: YAML-driven ontology project configuration (`mocho-odk.yaml`)
- **ROBOT SLME/BOT**: Semantic Lightweight Module Extraction with Bottom strategy — extracts only logically necessary terms; contrasted with `module_type: mirror` (verbatim copy)
- **`module_type: SLME`** + **`module_type_slme: BOT`** + **`slme_individuals: false`** + **`terms_file`**: ODK pattern for seeded module extraction
- **`annotation_properties`** in ODK import config: forces `owl:AnnotationProperty` declarations into the extracted module
- **`mapElement2DCT.xml`**: RDA-Vocabularies file that maps RDA property IRIs to DCT super-properties via `rdfs:subPropertyOf`; inverted to produce DCT→RDA lookup
- **`g.qname(uri)`**: rdflib method to get prefixed name from graph's namespace bindings
- **DDB EDM**: Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek Europeana Data Model; `ddbedm-full.owl` is the ODK-generated merged/reasoned release
- **`edm_terms.txt`**: MIREOT seed file for the EDM import module in mocho-odk; 59 IRIs (51 edm/ddb/dcterms/dc/geo + 8 crm)
- **`rda_terms.txt`**: MIREOT seed file for RDA import; grew from 220 to 1072 lines after DCT mapping additions

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`mocho/docs/rico-rda-mapping.md`** (modified — 6 corrections):
  - Version: `v0.2` → `v1.1`
  - Input ontology: `aco.owl` → `RiC-O_1-1.rdf`
  - Added E criterion to criteria table: `| E | Transitive subtype | FRBR anchor reached via 2+ hops... |`
  - RecordPart criterion: `(B)` → `(E)` (two places: main table and summary table)
  - RecordSet summary: `rdac:C10001 Work` → `rdac:C10001 Work + rdac:C10006 Expression`
  - Counts: `527+`/`629+` → `527`/`629`

- **`mocho/scripts/extract_ontology_terms.py`** (modified):
  - Added `_prefixed_name()` helper:
    ```python
    def _prefixed_name(uri: URIRef, g: Graph) -> str:
        """Return prefix:localname using the graph's namespace bindings, falling back to local name."""
        try:
            return g.qname(uri)
        except Exception:
            return _local_name(str(uri))
    ```
  - Changed label fallback in `extract_terms()`:
    ```python
    label = _best_literal(uri, g, RDFS.label) or _prefixed_name(uri, g)
    ```
  - Updated docstring: `prefix:localname from IRI if absent, bare local name if prefix unknown`

- **`mocho/scripts/dct_rda_map.py`** (created — new script):
  - Key constants: `DCT_NS = "http://purl.org/dc/terms/"`, `DC_NS = "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/"`, `ENTITY_CODES = ["w", "e", "m", "i", "a", "n", "p", "t", "x", "c", "rof", "u", "z"]`
  - `parse_dct_map(map_xml)`: parses `mapElement2DCT.xml`, builds `dct_iri → [rda_iri, ...]` inverted index
  - `build_rda_label_index(vocab_dir)`: parses all entity XML files, builds `rda_iri → English label`
  - `collect_dct_labels(input_csv)`: reads CSV, collects unique `dc:`/`dcterms:` labels from `label` column
  - `to_dct_iri(label)`: converts `dc:X` or `dcterms:X` to `http://purl.org/dc/terms/X`
  - `build_rows(terms, dct_index, label_index)`: cross-references and builds output rows
  - Output schema: `dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label`
  - Defaults: `--input output/ddbedm_1.csv`, `--map-xml ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Maps/mapElement2DCT.xml`, `--vocab-dir ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements`, `--output output/mapping_dct_to_rda`

- **`mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv`** (regenerated):
  - Was: 78 rows (dcterms: only, 78 rows from prior unknown source)
  - Now: 979 rows (23 matched dc:/dcterms: terms × varying sub-properties)
  - 11 no-match terms: 7 DCT classes + dcterms:bibliographicCitation, dcterms:provenance, dcterms:spatial, dcterms:temporal

- **`mocho/mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt`** (updated):
  - Was: 220 lines
  - Now: 1072 lines (+852 unique RDA IRIs from mapping_dct_to_rda.csv)
  - Append command: `tail -n +2 output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv | cut -d',' -f2 | sort -u | grep -vFf mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt >> mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt`

- **`mocho/notes/mapping_dct_rda_workflow.md`** (created):
  - 3-step workflow: `ddbedm_1.0.ttl` → `ddbedm_1.csv` → `mapping_dct_to_rda.csv` → `rda_terms.txt`
  - Table of DC/DCT terms found (34 total), matched (23), unmatched (11)

---

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **`docs/rico-rda-mapping.md` had 6 factual errors**: Wrong input ontology (`aco.owl`), wrong version (`v0.2`), approximate counts with `+` suffix, RecordPart criterion `(B)` instead of `(E)`, RecordSet missing Expression, missing E criterion from criteria table. All fixed with targeted `Edit` calls.

- **`edm_terms.txt` overwrite tool error**: Attempted `Write` tool on `edm_terms.txt` before reading it — got "File has not been read yet" error. Resolved by using Python inline script via Bash (no file read required for overwrite logic).

---

5. Problem Solving:

- **DCT inversion**: `mapElement2DCT.xml` maps RDA→DCT; script inverts this to DCT→RDA by grouping all RDA properties that share the same DCT `rdfs:subPropertyOf` IRI.
- **dc: vs dcterms: resolution**: XML only has `http://purl.org/dc/terms/` URIs. `dc:X` labels from ddbedm_1.csv resolved by treating them as `dcterms:X` equivalents. Original prefix preserved in output `dct_term` column.
- **High fanout**: Broad terms like `dc:contributor` expand to 360 RDA sub-properties. Documented in workflow note and README as caveat.
- **DCT class terms appearing in no-match**: 7 DCT class labels (`dcterms:LinguisticSystem`, etc.) appear in ddbedm_1.csv due to `g.qname()` prefixing; correctly produce no-match since `mapElement2DCT.xml` only has property sub-property entries.
- **Plan mode for simple task**: When user asked to run `extract_ontology_terms.py` on `ddbedm-full.owl`, plan mode was active. Created minimal plan, received approval, executed.
- **edm_terms.txt — safe CSV parsing**: Used Python `csv.DictReader` to filter by label prefix, avoiding shell `cut`/`grep` fragility with quoted CSV fields.

     1→#!/bin/sh

     3→set -e

     5→echo "This script only works with ODK 1.3.2 and later. For ODK 1.3.1 or earlier, use https://raw.githubusercontent.com/INCATools/ontology-development-kit/v1.3.1/seed-via-docker.sh"

     8→ODK_TAG=${ODK_TAG:-latest}
     9→ODK_GITNAME=${ODK_GITNAME:-$(git config --get user.name)}
    10→ODK_GITEMAIL=${ODK_GITEMAIL:-$(git config --get user.email)}

**Tool result**
```
     1→#!/bin/sh
     2→# Wrapper script for docker.
     3→#
     4→# This is used primarily for wrapping the GNU Make workflow.
     5→# Instead of typing "make TARGET", type "./run.sh make TARGET".
     6→# This will run the make workflow within a docker container.
     7→#
     8→# The assumption is that you are working in the src/ontology folder;
     9→# we therefore map the whole repo (../..) to a docker volume.
    10→#
    11→# To use singularity instead of docker, please issue
    12→# export USE_SINGULARITY=<any-value>
    13→# before running this script.
    14→#
    15→# See README-editors.md for more details.
    16→
    17→case "$@" in
    18→*update_repo*)
    19→    # Execute a copy of this script so that it can be overwritten
    20→    # during the update process
    21→    mkdir -p tmp
    22→    sed -n '/^set -e/,$p' $0 > tmp/$0
    23→    exec /bin/sh tmp/$0 "$@"
    24→    ;;
    25→esac
    26→
    27→set -e
    28→
    29→# Check for spaces in the current directory path
    30→for item in $PWD ; do
    31→    if [ "$item" != "$PWD" ]; then
    32→        echo "${0##*/}: error: repository path must not contain whitespace characters" >&2
    33→        exit 1
    34→    fi
    35→done
    36→
    37→if [ -f run.sh.conf ]; then
    38→    . ./run.sh.conf
    39→fi
    40→
    41→# Look for a GitHub token
    42→if [ -n "$GH_TOKEN" ]; then
    43→    :
    44→elif [ -f ../../.github/token.txt ]; then
    45→    GH_TOKEN=$(cat ../../.github/token.txt)
    46→elif [ -f $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ontology-development-kit/github/token ]; then
    47→    GH_TOKEN=$(cat $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ontology-development-kit/github/token)
    48→elif [ -f "$HOME/Library/Application Support/ontology-development-kit/github/token" ]; then
    49→    GH_TOKEN=$(cat "$HOME/Library/Application Support/ontology-development-kit/github/token")
    50→fi
    51→
    52→# SSH agent socket
    53→# On macOS, we cannot use $SSH_AUTH_SOCK directly,
    54→# we need to use a "magic" socket instead.
    55→case "$(uname)" in
    56→Darwin)
    57→    ODK_SSH_AUTH_SOCKET=/run/host-services/ssh-auth.sock
    58→    ;;
    59→*)
    60→    ODK_SSH_AUTH_SOCKET=$SSH_AUTH_SOCK
    61→    ;;
    62→esac
    63→ODK_SSH_BIND=
    64→if [ -n "$ODK_SSH_AUTH_SOCKET" ]; then
    65→    ODK_SSH_BIND=",$ODK_SSH_AUTH_SOCKET:/run/host-services/ssh-auth.sock"
    66→fi
    67→
    68→ODK_IMAGE=${ODK_IMAGE:-odkfull}
    69→TAG_IN_IMAGE=$(echo $ODK_IMAGE | awk -F':' '{ print $2 }')
    70→if [ -n "$TAG_IN_IMAGE" ]; then
    71→  # Override ODK_TAG env var if IMAGE already includes a tag
    72→  ODK_TAG=$TAG_IN_IMAGE
    73→  ODK_IMAGE=$(echo $ODK_IMAGE | awk -F':' '{ print $1 }')
    74→fi
    75→ODK_TAG=${ODK_TAG:-latest}
    76→
    77→ODK_JAVA_OPTS=${ODK_JAVA_OPTS:--Xmx8G}
    78→
    79→ODK_DEBUG=${ODK_DEBUG:-no}
    80→
    81→ODK_USER_ID=${ODK_USER_ID:-$(id -u)}
    82→ODK_GROUP_ID=${ODK_GROUP_ID:-$(id -g)}
    83→
    84→# Convert OWLAPI_* environment variables to the OWLAPI as Java options
    85→# See http://owlcs.github.io/owlapi/apidocs_4/org/semanticweb/owlapi/model/parameters/ConfigurationOptions.html
    86→# for a list of allowed options
    87→OWLAPI_OPTIONS_NAMESPACE=org.semanticweb.owlapi.model.parameters.ConfigurationOptions
    88→for owlapi_var in $(env | sed -n s/^OWLAPI_//p) ; do
    89→    ODK_JAVA_OPTS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS -D$OWLAPI_OPTIONS_NAMESPACE.${owlapi_var%=*}=${owlapi_var#*=}"
    90→done
    91→
    92→# Proxy settings for Java applications
    93→[ -z "$HTTP_PROXY" ] && [ -n "$http_proxy" ] && HTTP_PROXY=$http_proxy
    94→if [ -n "$HTTP_PROXY" ]; then
    95→    proxy_host=$(echo $HTTP_PROXY | sed -E 's,^https?://,,; s,:[0-9]+$,,')
    96→    proxy_port=$(echo $HTTP_PROXY | sed -E 's,^.*:([0-9]+)$,\1,')
    97→    ODK_JAVA_OPTS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS -Dhttp.proxyHost=$proxy_host"
    98→    [ "$proxy_port" != "$proxy_host" ] && ODK_JAVA_OPTS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS -Dhttp.proxyPort=$proxy_port"
    99→fi
   100→[ -z "$HTTPS_PROXY" ] && [ -n "$https_proxy" ] && HTTPS_PROXY=$https_proxy
   101→if [ -n "$HTTPS_PROXY" ]; then
   102→    proxy_host=$(echo $HTTPS_PROXY | sed -E 's,^https?://,,; s,:[0-9]+$,,')
   103→    proxy_port=$(echo $HTTPS_PROXY | sed -E 's,^.*:([0-9]+)$,\1,')
   104→    ODK_JAVA_OPTS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS -Dhttps.proxyHost=$proxy_host"
   105→    [ "$proxy_port" != "$proxy_host" ] && ODK_JAVA_OPTS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS -Dhttps.proxyPort=$proxy_port"
   106→fi
   107→[ -z "$NO_PROXY" ] && [ -n "$no_proxy" ] && NO_PROXY=$no_proxy
   108→if [ -n "$NO_PROXY" ] ; then
   109→    no_proxy_hosts=$(echo $NO_PROXY | tr ',' '|')
   110→    ODK_JAVA_OPTS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS -Dhttp.nonProxyHosts=$no_proxy_hosts"
   111→fi
   112→
   113→TIMECMD=
   114→if [ x$ODK_DEBUG = xyes ]; then
   115→    # If you wish to change the format string, take care of using
   116→    # non-breaking spaces (U+00A0) instead of normal spaces, to
   117→    # prevent the shell from tokenizing the format string.
   118→    echo "Running obolibrary/${ODK_IMAGE}:${ODK_TAG} with '${ODK_JAVA_OPTS}' as options for ROBOT and other Java-based pipeline steps."
   119→    TIMECMD="/usr/bin/time -f ### DEBUG STATS ###\nElapsed time: %E\nPeak memory: %M kb"
   120→fi
   121→rm -f tmp/debug.log
   122→
   123→VOLUME_BIND=$PWD/../../:/work$ODK_SSH_BIND
   124→WORK_DIR=/work/src/ontology
   125→
   126→# Support for OAK cache sharing
   127→if [ -n "$ODK_SHARE_OAK_CACHE" ]; then
   128→    case "$ODK_SHARE_OAK_CACHE" in
   129→    user)
   130→        # We assume the cache is in its default location; if it is not,
   131→        # ODK_SHARE_OAK_CACHE must point to the actual location.
   132→        ODK_SHARE_OAK_CACHE="$HOME/.data/oaklib"
   133→        ;;
   134→    repo)
   135→        ODK_SHARE_OAK_CACHE="$PWD/tmp/oaklib"
   136→        ;;
   137→    esac
   138→    [ $ODK_USER_ID -eq 0 ] && OAK_DEST=/root/.data/oaklib || OAK_DEST=/home/odkuser/.data/oaklib
   139→    VOLUME_BIND="$VOLUME_BIND,$ODK_SHARE_OAK_CACHE:$OAK_DEST"
   140→fi
   141→
   142→if [ -n "$ODK_BINDS" ]; then
   143→    VOLUME_BIND="$VOLUME_BIND,$ODK_BINDS"
   144→fi
   145→
   146→if [ -n "$USE_SINGULARITY" ]; then
   147→    
   148→    singularity exec --cleanenv $ODK_SINGULARITY_OPTIONS \
   149→        --env "ROBOT_JAVA_ARGS=$ODK_JAVA_OPTS,JAVA_OPTS=$ODK_JAVA_OPTS,SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/run/host-services/ssh-auth.sock,ODK_USER_ID=$ODK_USER_ID,ODK_GROUP_ID=$ODK_GROUP_ID,ODK_DEBUG=$ODK_DEBUG" \
   150→        --bind $VOLUME_BIND \
   151→        -W $WORK_DIR \
   152→        docker://obolibrary/$ODK_IMAGE:$ODK_TAG $TIMECMD "$@"
   153→else
   154→    BIND_OPTIONS="-v $(echo $VOLUME_BIND | sed 's/,/ -v /g')"
   155→    docker run $ODK_DOCKER_OPTIONS $BIND_OPTIONS -w $WORK_DIR \
   156→        -e ROBOT_JAVA_ARGS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS" -e JAVA_OPTS="$ODK_JAVA_OPTS" -e SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/run/host-services/ssh-auth.sock -e ODK_USER_ID=$ODK_USER_ID -e ODK_GROUP_ID=$ODK_GROUP_ID -e ODK_DEBUG=$ODK_DEBUG \
   157→        --rm -ti obolibrary/$ODK_IMAGE:$ODK_TAG $TIMECMD "$@"
   158→fi
   159→
   160→case "$@" in
   161→*update_repo*|*release*)
   162→    echo "Please remember to update your ODK image from time to time: https://oboacademy.github.io/obook/howto/odk-update/."
   163→    ;;
   164→esac



```

### User *(2026-03-09T15:31:34.640Z)*

**Tool result**
```
Perfect! Now I have good information. Let me compile my findings based on the ODK documentation I've reviewed.

## Summary of ODK Documentation on Local Files, Docker Volumes, and Native Execution

Based on the ODK repository documentation, here are the findings:

### 1. **mirror_from with file:// URIs - How They Work**

**Finding**: ODK documentation does not explicitly support or document `file://` URIs for `mirror_from` configuration. The documentation in `project-schema.md` (line 140) defines `mirror_from` as:

> **`mirror_from`** *(string)*: if specified this URL is used rather than the default OBO PURL for the main OWL product.

The examples in the codebase show only HTTP/HTTPS URLs (e.g., ROBOT plugins use `https://github.com/...`). **ODK expects URLs, not file paths**. When used with Docker, `mirror_from` URLs are downloaded **inside the container** using standard HTTP mechanisms (cURL), not resolved from the host filesystem.

### 2. **Standard Way to Use Local Files Without Docker Volume Issues**

**Recommended Pattern** (from `UsingRobotPlugins.md` and project schema):

For local source ontologies, ODK recommends placing them in a **plugins directory** at the repository root. From `UsingRobotPlugins.md` (lines 42-44):

> Any Jar file found in the repository's top-level `plugins` directory (if such a directory exists) will automatically be found by the ODK, without requiring any change to the ODK configuration or the custom Makefile.

**For ontology files specifically**, the standard approach is:
- **Imports directory**: Default location is `imports/` (configurable in config via `import_group.directory`, default is `"imports/"`)
- **Components directory**: Default location is `components/` 
- **These directories should reside within the `src/ontology` folder** (or parent), ensuring they're within the Docker volume mount

The `run.sh` script (shown in `docker/robot/run.sh`) mounts the current working directory as a volume:
```bash
docker run -v $PWD:/work -w /work ...
```

This means anything in the project root or subdirectories is accessible inside the container.

### 3. **Running Make Targets Natively Without Docker**

**Native ODK Support** (from `README.md`, lines 139-148):

ODK provides native execution capability through **ODK Core**:

> You can use the `odk install` command of the [ODK Core](https://github.com/INCATools/odkcore) module to install a "native ODK environment" that allows using the ODK without using Docker.

**Key limitations**:
- Native ODK environments are **only supported for GNU/Linux and macOS**
- **Windows requires Docker**

To use native ODK:
1. Install ODK Core via `odk install`
2. This provides native access to ROBOT and other tools
3. See [ODK Core documentation](https://github.com/INCATools/odkcore) for details

### 4. **Standard Directory Structure for Local Source Ontologies**

Per `project-schema.md` (line 122), the recommended directory structure is:

```
src/ontology/
├── imports/          # Default location for import modules
├── components/       # Default location for components
├── subsets/          # Default location for subsets
├── mappings/         # Default location for SSSOM mappings
├── patterns/         # Default location for patterns
└── [ontology]-odk.yaml
```

This structure keeps everything **within the src/ontology folder**, which is accessible to Docker since the entire project is mounted.

### 5. **Docker Volume Sharing for Caches**

**Recent Addition** (CHANGELOG.md, v1.6, lines 75-77):

ODK now supports sharing local OAK caches with the container:

```bash
# Share user's default OAK cache
ODK_SHARE_OAK_CACHE=user sh run.sh make [target]

# Share custom cache location
ODK_SHARE_OAC_CACHE=/path/to/cache sh run.sh make [target]
```

### Key Takeaway for Local Ontology Management

**Don't use `mirror_from` for local files**. Instead:
1. Place local source ontologies in the `imports/` directory (or use `components/` for component ontologies)
2. Manually configure them in your `-edit` file with appropriate `owl:imports` declarations, or
3. Use `mirror_from` with HTTP/HTTPS URLs pointing to accessible online locations
4. For plugin JARs, place them in the top-level `plugins/` directory

The Docker volume automatically mounts the entire project root at `/work` inside the container, so anything in your repository structure is accessible.
agentId: a81fa06a13817d9aa (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)
<usage>total_tokens: 51388
tool_uses: 33
duration_ms: 236757</usage>
```

### Assistant *(2026-03-09T15:33:01.055Z)*

Key findings:

**ODK docs**: `mirror_from` is documented for HTTP/HTTPS URLs only — `file://` is not the standard approach. For local files, ODK expects them to be inside the repo structure (accessible at `/work` inside Docker).

**Your `file://` paths are also broken for frbr/fabio** — `run.sh` mounts `target/mocho/` as `/work`, so anything outside that directory (including `mocho/ontology/*.owl` and `ddbedm-odk/`) is invisible to Docker. The imports have never been generated.

**Native execution**: ODK Core installs ROBOT + tools natively on macOS. `robot` is not currently installed.

**The Makefile also needs re-seeding** — it still lists `ddb_edm` (the old id) and doesn't know about the SLME/BOT config.

The cleanest path without changing any configs or outputs:

1. **Re-seed** with Docker (`seed-via-docker.sh -C mocho-odk.yaml`) — this only generates scaffolding, no file:// access needed
2. **Install ROBOT natively** (single JAR, no Docker): `brew install robot` or download from robot.obolibrary.org
3. **Run `make refresh-ddbedm`** natively (without `run.sh`) — ROBOT will resolve the `file://` URIs on the host filesystem

Want to proceed with that plan, or use HTTP/HTTPS URLs in `mirror_from` instead (e.g., point to the GitHub raw URL for `ddbedm-full.owl` once it's pushed)?

### Assistant *(2026-03-09T18:04:16.728Z)*

Let me clarify the situation before proceeding:

The Makefile in `target/` is stale — it was generated from the old yaml (before `rico_import.rdf` was corrected) so it still looks for `rico.rdf`. ODK's re-seed didn't regenerate it because of config-hash caching.

**The clean fix**: delete the stale Makefile so the next seed regenerates it with the correct `rico_import.rdf` path from the current yaml. This is not editing the Makefile — it's forcing ODK to regenerate it.

```bash
rm /Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/target/mocho/src/ontology/Makefile
bash seed-via-docker.sh -C mocho-odk.yaml
```

The `rico.rdf` copy I added to `ontology/` can be removed after that.

Want me to proceed this way?

INFO:root:OUT: robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml convert -I file:///work/ontology/ddbedm_import.owl -o tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl
mkdir -p mirror
if [ -f tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl ]; then if cmp -s tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl mirror/ddbedm.owl ; then echo "Mirror identical, ignoring."; else echo "Mirrors different, updating." &&\
	cp tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl mirror/ddbedm.owl; fi; fi
Mirrors different, updating.
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml convert --input mocho-edit.owl --format ofn --output tmp/mocho-preprocess.owl
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml remove --input tmp/mocho-preprocess.owl --select imports --trim false \
	 merge  \
	       --output tmp/merged-mocho-edit.ofn
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml query --input tmp/merged-mocho-edit.ofn --format --csv \
	       --query ../sparql/terms.sparql tmp/pre_seed.txt
cat tmp/pre_seed.txt | sort | uniq > tmp/seed.txt
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml annotate --input mirror/ddbedm.owl --remove-annotations \
	 odk:normalize --add-source true \
	 extract --term-file imports/ddbedm_terms.txt --term-file tmp/seed.txt \
	         --force true --copy-ontology-annotations true \
	         --individuals include \
	         --method BOT \
	 remove  --term rdfs:label  --term rdfs:comment  --term rdfs:isDefinedBy  --term skos:note  --term skos:definition  --term skos:example  --term skos:scopeNote  --term dcterms:description \
	        --term-file imports/ddbedm_terms.txt --term-file tmp/seed.txt \
	        --select complement --select annotation-properties \
	 odk:normalize --base-iri https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk \
	               --subset-decls true --synonym-decls true \
	 repair --merge-axiom-annotations true \
	 annotate --ontology-iri https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho/imports/ddbedm_import.owl annotate -V https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho/releases/2026-03-09/imports/ddbedm_import.owl --annotation owl:versionInfo 2026-03-09 convert -f ofn --output imports/ddbedm_import.owl.tmp.owl && mv imports/ddbedm_import.owl.tmp.owl imports/ddbedm_import.owl

robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml merge --input tmp/mocho-preprocess.owl  \
	reason --reasoner HermiT --equivalent-classes-allowed asserted-only --exclude-tautologies structural \
	relax --include-subclass-of true \
	reduce -r HermiT --include-subproperties true \
	 annotate --ontology-iri https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho/mocho-full.owl annotate -V https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho/releases/2026-03-09/mocho-full.owl --annotation owl:versionInfo 2026-03-09 --output mocho-full.owl.tmp.owl && mv mocho-full.owl.tmp.owl mocho-full.owl

	convert -o mocho.owl.tmp.owl && mv mocho.owl.tmp.owl mocho.owl
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml annotate --input mocho.owl --ontology-iri https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho.json annotate -V https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho/releases/2026-03-09/mocho.json --annotation owl:versionInfo 2026-03-09 \
	convert --check false -f json -o mocho.json.tmp.json &&\
	mv mocho.json.tmp.json mocho.json

	convert --check false -f ttl -o mocho.ttl.tmp.ttl && mv mocho.ttl.tmp.ttl mocho.ttl
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml merge --input tmp/mocho-preprocess.owl  \
reason --reasoner HermiT --equivalent-classes-allowed asserted-only --exclude-tautologies structural --annotate-inferred-axioms false \
relax --include-subclass-of true \
reduce -r HermiT --include-subproperties true \
remove --base-iri https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho# --axioms external --preserve-structure false --trim false \
 \

```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/gen_rda_imports.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"\ngen_rda_imports.py\n\nFor each RDA element namespace prefix present in mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt,\nmerge the corresponding RDA Vocabularies XML source files (classes + properties)\ninto one OWL/RDF file and write a filtered IRI list.\n\nRDA XML layout (relative to --vocab-dir):\n  {prefix}.xml              — class declarations for that element namespace\n  {prefix}/object.xml       — object property declarations\n  {prefix}/datatype.xml     — datatype property declarations\n\nIRIs in rda_terms.txt look like:\n  https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/P20074   → prefix 'e'\n  https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/P10051   → prefix 'w'\n\nOnly IRIs whose namespace matches https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/ are processed;\nany other IRIs in rda_terms.txt are written to a separate remainder file.\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/gen_rda_imports.py\n    python scripts/gen_rda_imports.py \\\\\n        --terms  mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt \\\\\n        --vocab-dir ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements \\\\\n        --out-dir mocho-odk/imports\n\nOutputs (one per prefix found in rda_terms.txt):\n    OUT_DIR/rda{prefix}_imports.xml   — merged OWL/RDF source (classes + properties)\n    OUT_DIR/rda{prefix}_terms.txt     — filtered IRI list for that prefix\n\nAlso outputs:\n    OUT_DIR/rda_other_terms.txt       — IRIs not matching the Elements namespace\n\nDependencies:\n    rdflib  (pip install rdflib)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\ntry:\n    from rdflib import Graph\nexcept ImportError:\n    raise SystemExit(\"rdflib is required: pip install rdflib\")\n\nRDA_ELEMENTS_BASE = \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/\"\n\n\ndef parse_terms(terms_file: Path) -> tuple[dict[str, list[str]], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Read rda_terms.txt and split into:\n      - prefix_map: {prefix -> [IRI, ...]}  for Elements/ IRIs\n      - other: [IRI, ...]                   for all other IRIs\n    \"\"\"\n    prefix_map: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n    other: list[str] = []\n\n    with open(terms_file, encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n        for line in fh:\n            iri = line.strip()\n            if not iri:\n                continue\n            if iri.startswith(RDA_ELEMENTS_BASE):\n                rest = iri[len(RDA_ELEMENTS_BASE):]      # e.g. \"e/P20074\"\n                prefix = rest.split(\"/\")[0]               # e.g. \"e\"\n                prefix_map[prefix].append(iri)\n            else:\n                other.append(iri)\n\n    return dict(prefix_map), other\n\n\ndef merge_prefix(prefix: str, vocab_dir: Path) -> Graph:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse and merge all XML source files for a given prefix:\n      {prefix}.xml, {prefix}/object.xml, {prefix}/datatype.xml\n    Returns a merged rdflib Graph.\n    \"\"\"\n    g = Graph()\n    sources = [\n        vocab_dir / f\"{prefix}.xml\",\n        vocab_dir / prefix / \"object.xml\",\n        vocab_dir / prefix / \"datatype.xml\",\n    ]\n    for src in sources:\n        if src.exists():\n            print(f\"  Parsing {src} …\")\n            g.parse(str(src))\n        else:\n            print(f\"  (skipping missing {src})\")\n    return g\n\n\ndef write_imports_xml(g: Graph, out_path: Path) -> None:\n    g.serialize(destination=str(out_path), format=\"xml\")\n\n\ndef write_terms_txt(iris: list[str], out_path: Path) -> None:\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(iris) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--terms\",\n        default=\"mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt\",\n        metavar=\"FILE\",\n        help=\"Input IRI list (default: mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt).\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--vocab-dir\",\n        default=\"ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements\",\n        metavar=\"DIR\",\n        help=\"RDA Vocabularies xml/Elements directory (default: ontology/RDA-Vocabularies-5.4.9/xml/Elements).\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--out-dir\",\n        default=\"mocho-odk/imports\",\n        metavar=\"DIR\",\n        help=\"Output directory (default: mocho-odk/imports).\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    terms_file = Path(args.terms)\n    vocab_dir  = Path(args.vocab_dir)\n    out_dir    = Path(args.out_dir)\n\n    for p, name in [(terms_file, \"terms file\"), (vocab_dir, \"vocab dir\")]:\n        if not p.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"{name} not found: {p}\")\n\n    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    prefix_map, other = parse_terms(terms_file)\n\n    print(f\"RDA Elements prefixes found: {sorted(prefix_map)}\")\n    for prefix, iris in sorted(prefix_map.items()):\n        print(f\"  {prefix}: {len(iris)} IRIs\")\n    print(f\"Non-Elements IRIs: {len(other)}\")\n\n    # Per-prefix: merge XML + write outputs\n    for prefix in sorted(prefix_map):\n        iris = prefix_map[prefix]\n        print(f\"\\n--- Prefix '{prefix}' ({len(iris)} IRIs) ---\")\n\n        g = merge_prefix(prefix, vocab_dir)\n        print(f\"  Merged graph: {len(g)} triples\")\n\n        xml_out  = out_dir / f\"rda{prefix}_imports.xml\"\n        txt_out  = out_dir / f\"rda{prefix}_terms.txt\"\n\n        write_imports_xml(g, xml_out)\n        write_terms_txt(iris, txt_out)\n        print(f\"  Wrote {xml_out}\")\n        print(f\"  Wrote {txt_out}\")\n\n    # Write remainder\n    if other:\n        remainder = out_dir / \"rda_other_terms.txt\"\n        write_terms_txt(other, remainder)\n        print(f\"\\nWrote {len(other)} non-Elements IRIs → {remainder}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

INFO:root:OUT: robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml convert -I file:///work/ontology/ddbedm_import.owl -o tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl
mkdir -p mirror
if [ -f tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl ]; then if cmp -s tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl mirror/ddbedm.owl ; then echo "Mirror identical, ignoring."; else echo "Mirrors different, updating." &&\
	cp tmp/mirror-ddbedm.owl mirror/ddbedm.owl; fi; fi
Mirrors different, updating.
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml convert --input mocho-edit.owl --format ofn --output tmp/mocho-preprocess.owl
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml remove --input tmp/mocho-preprocess.owl --select imports --trim false \
	 merge  \
	       --output tmp/merged-mocho-edit.ofn
robot --catalog catalog-v001.xml query --input tmp/merged-mocho-edit.ofn --format --csv \
	       --query ../sparql/terms.sparql tmp/pre_seed.txt

	        --term-file imports/ddbedm_terms.txt --term-file tmp/seed.txt \
	        --select complement --select annotation-properties \
	 odk:normalize --base-iri https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk \
	               --subset-decls true --synonym-decls true \
	 repair --merge-axiom-annotations true \
	 annotate --ontology-iri https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho/imports/ddbedm_import.owl annotate -V https://github.com/anntanp/mocho-odk/mocho/releases/2026-03-09/imports/ddbedm_import.owl --annotation owl:versionInfo 2026-03-09 convert -f ofn --output imports/ddbedm_import.owl.tmp.owl && mv imports/ddbedm_import.owl.tmp.owl imports/ddbedm_import.owl

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The session covered the full mocho-odk ODK seeding workflow and culminated in:
- Updating `mocho-odk.yaml` to change `ddbedm` from `module_type: mirror` to SLME/BOT with correct `file:///work/ontology/` paths
- Re-seeding the ODK project multiple times, debugging YAML and Docker errors each time
- Creating `scripts/gen_rda_imports.py` to merge RDA element XML source files per namespace prefix and generate filtered IRI lists
- Adding 5 RDA import entries (rdaw, rdae, rdam, rdai, rdaa) to `mocho-odk.yaml`
- Adding custom SLME extract rules to `mocho.Makefile` (the ODK extension point) for the RDA imports
- Successfully running `make all_assets` producing all 12 import modules with no violations
- Final pending request: **git commit and push the mocho project**

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **ODK (Ontology Development Kit)**: YAML-driven ontology project scaffolding via `seed-via-docker.sh`; generates `Makefile`, `catalog-v001.xml`, `run.sh`, etc. in `target/`
- **SLME/BOT**: Semantic Lightweight Module Extraction with Bottom strategy — ROBOT `extract --method BOT`
- **`mocho-odk.yaml`**: ODK configuration file; controls import_group products, mirror_from URLs, module types, terms_file references
- **`mocho.Makefile`**: ODK user customization extension point — included at line 834 of the auto-generated Makefile; overrides stubs
- **`file:///work/...` URIs**: Docker-accessible paths; `/work` is mounted from `mocho-odk/` by `seed-via-docker.sh` and from `target/mocho/` by `run.sh`
- **Custom module stubs**: ODK generates error stubs for imports whose `mirror_from` source is in `imports/` (not `ontology/`); must be overridden in `mocho.Makefile`
- **RDA Vocabularies XML structure**: Classes in `xml/Elements/{prefix}.xml`; properties in `xml/Elements/{prefix}/object.xml` and `datatype.xml`
- **rdflib**: Used for parsing and merging RDA XML OWL files
- **`slme_individuals: "false"`**: ODK requires quoted string, not YAML boolean

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`mocho/mocho-odk/mocho-odk.yaml`** — Main ODK configuration (modified extensively):
  - `ddbedm` entry: `mirror_from: "file:///work/ontology/ddbedm_import.owl"`, `module_type: SLME`, `module_type_slme: BOT`, `slme_individuals: "false"`, `terms_file: imports/ddbedm_terms.txt`
  - `fabio`, `rico`, `aco`, `mo`, `ebucoreplus` similarly converted to SLME/BOT with `file:///work/ontology/` paths
  - 5 new RDA import entries added:
    ```yaml
    - id: rdaw
      description: "RDA Work element properties"
      mirror_from: "file:///work/imports/rdaw_imports.xml"
      module_type: SLME
      module_type_slme: BOT
      slme_individuals: "false"
      terms_file: imports/rdaw_terms.txt
    # ... same pattern for rdae, rdam, rdai, rdaa
    ```
  - `annotation_properties` moved to group level: `['rdfs:label', 'rdfs:comment', 'rdfs:isDefinedBy', 'skos:note', 'skos:definition', 'skos:example', 'skos:scopeNote', 'dcterms:description']`

- **`mocho/mocho-odk/imports/ddbedm_terms.txt`** — 51 IRIs (edm:, ddb:, dcterms:, dc:, geo: prefixes from ddbedm-full.owl); renamed from `edm_terms.txt` by user
