### User *(2026-03-18T15:51:01.402Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→"""
     2→gen_import_terms.py
     3→
     4→Generate import term lists for ROBOT from one or more FRBR/FaBiO → RDA mapping CSVs.
     5→
     6→Reads (one or more, passed via --csv):
     7→  output/mapping_frbr_fabio_to_rda.csv          — WEMI property mapping
     8→  output/mapping_frbr_fabio_nonwemi_to_rda.csv  — non-WEMI class/property mapping
     9→
    10→Writes (merges with existing content — does not overwrite unrelated terms):
    11→  mocho-odk/imports/frbr_terms.txt   — FRBR class/property IRIs
    12→  mocho-odk/imports/fabio_terms.txt  — FaBiO class/property IRIs
    13→  mocho-odk/imports/rda_terms.txt    — RDA class/property IRIs
    14→
    15→Only rows with match = "direct" or "approximate" are included.
    16→
    17→Namespaces handled:
    18→  frbr:  → http://purl.org/vocab/frbr/core#
    19→  fabio: → http://purl.org/spar/fabio/
    20→  rdac:  → http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/   (classes use http)
    21→  rdaw:  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/
    22→  rdae:  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/
    23→  rdam:  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/
    24→  rdai:  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/
    25→  rdaa:  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/
    26→
    27→Usage:
    28→  python scripts/gen_import_terms.py \\
    29→      --csv output/mapping_frbr_fabio_to_rda.csv \\
    30→      --csv output/mapping_frbr_fabio_nonwemi_to_rda.csv \\
    31→      [--out-dir mocho-odk/imports]
    32→"""
    33→
    34→import argparse
    35→import csv
    36→import re
    37→from pathlib import Path
    38→
    39→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40→# Namespace / IRI helpers
    41→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42→
    43→FRBR_NS  = "http://purl.org/vocab/frbr/core#"
    44→FABIO_NS = "http://purl.org/spar/fabio/"
    45→
    46→PREFIX_MAP = {
    47→    "rdac":  "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/",   # classes: http
    48→    "rdaw":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/",
    49→    "rdae":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/",
    50→    "rdam":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/",
    51→    "rdai":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/",
    52→    "rdaa":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/",
    53→}
    54→
    55→# Pattern: prefixed IRI optionally followed by quoted label
    56→RDA_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r'(rda[cwemia]):(\w+)')
    57→
    58→
    59→def rda_cell_to_iris(cell: str) -> list[str]:
    60→    """Extract RDA IRI(s) from a cell like 'rdaw:P10065 "label"; rdae:P20053 "label"'."""
    61→    iris = []
    62→    for token in cell.split(";"):
    63→        m = RDA_TOKEN_RE.search(token)
    64→        if m:
    65→            prefix, local = m.groups()
    66→            base = PREFIX_MAP.get(prefix)
    67→            if base:
    68→                iris.append(base + local)
    69→    return iris
    70→
    71→
    72→def source_to_iri(src: str) -> "str | None":
    73→    """Expand a prefixed source IRI. Strips ' (class)' suffix."""
    74→    src = src.strip().removesuffix(" (class)")
    75→    if src.startswith("frbr:"):
    76→        return FRBR_NS + src[len("frbr:"):]
    77→    if src.startswith("fabio:"):
    78→        return FABIO_NS + src[len("fabio:"):]
    79→    return None
    80→
    81→
    82→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    83→# Merge helpers
    84→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→
    86→def read_existing(path: Path) -> list[str]:
    87→    """Read existing IRI list, returning lines stripped of whitespace."""
    88→    if not path.exists():
    89→        return []
    90→    return [line.strip() for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
    91→            if line.strip()]
    92→
    93→
    94→def dedup_merge(existing: list[str], new: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    95→    """Append new IRIs not already present; preserve existing order."""
    96→    seen = set(existing)
    97→    result = list(existing)
    98→    for iri in new:
    99→        if iri not in seen:
   100→            seen.add(iri)
   101→            result.append(iri)
   102→    return result
   103→
   104→
   105→def write_terms(path: Path, iris: list[str]) -> None:
   106→    path.write_text("\n".join(iris) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   107→
   108→
   109→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→# Main
   111→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   112→
   113→def main():
   114→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
   115→    parser.add_argument("--csv", dest="csvs", action="append",
   116→                        default=["output/mapping_frbr_fabio_to_rda.csv",
   117→                                 "output/mapping_frbr_fabio_nonwemi_to_rda.csv"],
   118→                        help="Mapping CSV to read (repeatable; defaults to both WEMI and non-WEMI CSVs)")
   119→    parser.add_argument("--out-dir", default="mocho-odk/imports")
   120→    args = parser.parse_args()
   121→
   122→    out_dir = Path(args.out_dir)
   123→    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   124→
   125→    frbr_new  = []
   126→    fabio_new = []
   127→    rda_new   = []
   128→
   129→    for csv_file in args.csvs:
   130→        with open(csv_file, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   131→            for row in csv.DictReader(f):
   132→                if row["match"].strip() not in ("direct", "approximate"):
   133→                    continue
   134→
   135→                src = row["source"].strip()
   136→                rda = row["rda"].strip()
   137→
   138→                iri = source_to_iri(src)
   139→                if iri:
   140→                    if src.startswith("frbr:"):
   141→                        frbr_new.append(iri)
   142→                    elif src.startswith("fabio:"):
   143→                        fabio_new.append(iri)
   144→
   145→                rda_new.extend(rda_cell_to_iris(rda))
   146→
   147→    # Deduplicate new lists (preserve first-seen order)
   148→    def dedup(lst):
   149→        seen, out = set(), []
   150→        for x in lst:
   151→            if x not in seen:
   152→                seen.add(x)
   153→                out.append(x)
   154→        return out
   155→
   156→    frbr_new  = dedup(frbr_new)
   157→    fabio_new = dedup(fabio_new)
   158→    rda_new   = dedup(rda_new)
   159→
   160→    # Merge with existing files
   161→    frbr_path  = out_dir / "frbr_terms.txt"
   162→    fabio_path = out_dir / "fabio_terms.txt"
   163→    rda_path   = out_dir / "rda_terms.txt"
   164→
   165→    frbr_out  = dedup_merge(read_existing(frbr_path),  frbr_new)
   166→    fabio_out = dedup_merge(read_existing(fabio_path), fabio_new)
   167→    rda_out   = dedup_merge(read_existing(rda_path),   rda_new)
   168→
   169→    write_terms(frbr_path,  frbr_out)
   170→    write_terms(fabio_path, fabio_out)
   171→    write_terms(rda_path,   rda_out)
   172→
   173→    print(f"frbr_terms.txt  : {len(frbr_out)} IRIs ({len(frbr_new)} from CSVs)")
   174→    print(f"fabio_terms.txt : {len(fabio_out)} IRIs ({len(fabio_new)} from CSVs)")
   175→    print(f"rda_terms.txt   : {len(rda_out)} IRIs ({len(rda_new)} from CSVs)")
   176→
   177→
   178→if __name__ == "__main__":
   179→    main()
   180→



```

**Tool result**
```
     1→"""
     2→gen_all_import_terms.py
     3→
     4→Scan all output/mapping_*_to_*.csv files and generate or update
     5→mocho-odk/imports/<ontology>_terms.txt files.
     6→
     7→Source ontology IRIs (from the `source` column) are written to the file named
     8→for their prefix:
     9→    aco:   → aco_terms.txt
    10→    crm:   → crm_terms.txt
    11→    dc:    → dct_terms.txt
    12→    dcterms: → dct_terms.txt
    13→    fabio: → fabio_terms.txt
    14→    frbr:  → frbr_terms.txt
    15→    mo:    → mo_terms.txt
    16→    rico:  → rico_terms.txt
    17→
    18→RDA target IRIs (from the `rda` / `rda_uri` / `rda_iri` column) are merged
    19→into rda_terms.txt regardless of which mapping file they come from.
    20→
    21→Only rows with match = "direct" or "approximate" are included for files using
    22→the standard schema (source, rda, domain, range, match, remarks) or the
    23→extended CRM schema (adds source_uri, source_label, wemi_level, rda_uri,
    24→bridge columns). For the DCT mapping schema (dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label —
    25→no match column) all rows are included, since the file is already filtered to
    26→relevant terms during generation.
    27→
    28→Existing file contents are preserved; new IRIs are appended (deduplicated).
    29→
    30→Usage:
    31→    python scripts/gen_all_import_terms.py [--output-dir output]
    32→                                            [--out-dir mocho-odk/imports]
    33→
    34→    Run from the mocho/ project root.
    35→
    36→Inputs:
    37→    output/mapping_*_to_*.csv   All cross-ontology mapping files matching this
    38→                                glob pattern. Files with the rda_all_classes /
    39→                                rda_classes / rda_properties naming have no
    40→                                `match` column and are silently skipped.
    41→
    42→Outputs:
    43→    <out-dir>/<ontology>_terms.txt   One file per source ontology prefix.
    44→    <out-dir>/rda_terms.txt          Merged RDA target IRIs.
    45→
    46→Dependencies:
    47→    Python standard library only (csv, argparse, pathlib, re).
    48→"""
    49→
    50→import argparse
    51→import csv
    52→import re
    53→from pathlib import Path
    54→from typing import Optional
    55→
    56→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    57→# Namespace tables
    58→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→
    60→# Source ontology prefix → full namespace URI
    61→SOURCE_PREFIX_TO_NS: dict[str, str] = {
    62→    "aco":      "https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#",
    63→    "crm":      "http://www.cidoc-crm.org/rdfs/cidoc-crm#",
    64→    "dc":       "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/",
    65→    "dcterms":  "http://purl.org/dc/terms/",
    66→    "fabio":    "http://purl.org/spar/fabio/",
    67→    "frbr":     "http://purl.org/vocab/frbr/core#",
    68→    "mo":       "http://purl.org/ontology/mo/",
    69→    "rico":     "https://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#",
    70→}
    71→
    72→# Source prefix → output file stem (dc: and dcterms: share one file)
    73→PREFIX_TO_STEM: dict[str, str] = {
    74→    "aco":      "aco",
    75→    "crm":      "crm",
    76→    "dc":       "dct",
    77→    "dcterms":  "dct",
    78→    "fabio":    "fabio",
    79→    "frbr":     "frbr",
    80→    "mo":       "mo",
    81→    "rico":     "rico",
    82→}
    83→
    84→# RDA target prefix → full namespace URI (classes use http; properties use https)
    85→RDA_PREFIX_TO_NS: dict[str, str] = {
    86→    "rdac":  "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/",
    87→    "rdaw":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/",
    88→    "rdae":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/",
    89→    "rdam":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/",
    90→    "rdai":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/",
    91→    "rdaa":  "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/",
    92→}
    93→
    94→RDA_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r'(rdac|rdaw|rdae|rdam|rdai|rdaa):(\w+)')
    95→
    96→STANDARD_MATCH_VALUES = {"direct", "approximate"}
    97→
    98→
    99→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   100→# Helpers
   101→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   102→
   103→def expand_source(prefixed: str) -> tuple:
   104→    """
   105→    Expand a prefixed source value like 'ac:AudioClip (class)' or 'frbr:part'
   106→    to (full_iri, file_stem).  Returns (None, None) if the prefix is unknown.
   107→    """
   108→    prefixed = prefixed.strip().removesuffix(" (class)")
   109→    if ":" not in prefixed:
   110→        return None, None
   111→    prefix, local = prefixed.split(":", 1)
   112→    ns = SOURCE_PREFIX_TO_NS.get(prefix)
   113→    stem = PREFIX_TO_STEM.get(prefix)
   114→    if ns and stem:
   115→        return ns + local, stem
   116→    return None, None
   117→
   118→
   119→def rda_cell_to_iris(cell: str) -> list[str]:
   120→    """Extract RDA IRIs from a semicolon-separated `rda` cell value."""
   121→    iris = []
   122→    for token in cell.split(";"):
   123→        m = RDA_TOKEN_RE.search(token)
   124→        if m:
   125→            prefix, local = m.groups()
   126→            ns = RDA_PREFIX_TO_NS.get(prefix)
   127→            if ns:
   128→                iris.append(ns + local)
   129→    return iris
   130→
   131→
   132→def read_existing(path: Path) -> list[str]:
   133→    """Read an existing IRI list; return stripped, non-empty lines."""
   134→    if not path.exists():
   135→        return []
   136→    return [ln.strip() for ln in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
   137→            if ln.strip()]
   138→
   139→
   140→def dedup(lst: list) -> list:
   141→    """Remove duplicates while preserving insertion order."""
   142→    seen: set[str] = set()
   143→    out: list[str] = []
   144→    for x in lst:
   145→        if x not in seen:
   146→            seen.add(x)
   147→            out.append(x)
   148→    return out
   149→
   150→
   151→def dedup_merge(existing: list, new: list) -> list:
   152→    """Append items from `new` that are not already in `existing`."""
   153→    seen = set(existing)
   154→    result = list(existing)
   155→    for iri in new:
   156→        if iri not in seen:
   157→            seen.add(iri)
   158→            result.append(iri)
   159→    return result
   160→
   161→
   162→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   163→# Per-file processors
   164→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   165→
   166→def process_standard(
   167→    csv_path: Path,
   168→    source_new: dict[str, list[str]],
   169→    rda_new: list[str],
   170→) -> int:
   171→    """
   172→    Process a standard-schema CSV (source, rda, domain, range, match, remarks).
   173→    Also handles the extended CRM schema (source_uri, rda_uri extra columns).
   174→    Returns the number of source IRIs added.
   175→    """
   176→    count = 0
   177→    with open(csv_path, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   178→        for row in csv.DictReader(f):
   179→            match = row.get("match", "").strip()
   180→            if match not in STANDARD_MATCH_VALUES:
   181→                continue
   182→
   183→            # Source IRI: prefer source_uri (CRM schema), else expand prefix
   184→            src_uri = row.get("source_uri", "").strip()
   185→            src_str = row.get("source", "").strip()
   186→
   187→            if src_uri:
   188→                # CRM schema: full URI provided; derive stem from prefixed source
   189→                prefix = src_str.split(":")[0] if ":" in src_str else ""
   190→                stem = PREFIX_TO_STEM.get(prefix)
   191→                if stem:
   192→                    source_new.setdefault(stem, []).append(src_uri)
   193→                    count += 1
   194→            elif src_str:
   195→                iri, stem = expand_source(src_str)
   196→                if iri and stem:
   197→                    source_new.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)
   198→                    count += 1
   199→
   200→            # RDA target: prefer rda_uri (CRM schema), else parse rda cell
   201→            rda_uri = row.get("rda_uri", "").strip()
   202→            if rda_uri:
   203→                rda_new.append(rda_uri)
   204→            else:
   205→                rda_cell = row.get("rda", "").strip()
   206→                rda_new.extend(rda_cell_to_iris(rda_cell))
   207→
   208→    return count
   209→
   210→
   211→def process_dct(
   212→    csv_path: Path,
   213→    source_new: dict[str, list[str]],
   214→    rda_new: list[str],
   215→) -> int:
   216→    """
   217→    Process the DCT mapping CSV (dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label schema).
   218→    All rows are included (no match column; the file is pre-filtered).
   219→    Returns the number of source IRIs added.
   220→    """
   221→    count = 0
   222→    with open(csv_path, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   223→        for row in csv.DictReader(f):
   224→            dct_term = row.get("dct_term", "").strip()
   225→            rda_iri  = row.get("rda_iri",  "").strip()
   226→
   227→            if dct_term:
   228→                iri, stem = expand_source(dct_term)
   229→                if iri and stem:
   230→                    source_new.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)
   231→                    count += 1
   232→
   233→            if rda_iri:
   234→                rda_new.append(rda_iri)
   235→
   236→    return count
   237→
   238→
   239→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   240→# Main
   241→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   242→
   243→def main() -> None:
   244→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
   245→    parser.add_argument(
   246→        "--output-dir",
   247→        default="output",
   248→        metavar="PATH",
   249→        help="Directory containing mapping_*.csv files. Default: %(default)s",
   250→    )
   251→    parser.add_argument(
   252→        "--out-dir",
   253→        default="mocho-odk/imports",
   254→        metavar="PATH",
   255→        help="Directory to write terms.txt files. Default: %(default)s",
   256→    )
   257→    args = parser.parse_args()
   258→
   259→    output_dir = Path(args.output_dir)
   260→    out_dir    = Path(args.out_dir)
   261→    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   262→
   263→    source_new: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
   264→    rda_new:    list[str] = []
   265→
   266→    csv_files = sorted(output_dir.glob("mapping_*_to_*.csv"))
   267→    if not csv_files:
   268→        raise SystemExit(f"No mapping_*_to_*.csv files found in {output_dir}")
   269→
   270→    for csv_path in csv_files:
   271→        # Detect schema by checking column names
   272→        with open(csv_path, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   273→            header = next(csv.reader(f), [])
   274→
   275→        if "dct_term" in header:
   276→            count = process_dct(csv_path, source_new, rda_new)
   277→        elif "source" in header and "match" in header:
   278→            count = process_standard(csv_path, source_new, rda_new)
   279→        else:
   280→            print(f"  {csv_path.name}: skipped (unrecognised schema)")
   281→            continue
   282→
   283→        print(f"  {csv_path.name}: {count} source IRI(s)")
   284→
   285→    # Deduplicate collected lists before merging
   286→    source_new = {k: dedup(v) for k, v in source_new.items()}
   287→    rda_new    = dedup(rda_new)
   288→
   289→    print()
   290→
   291→    # Write / update source ontology term files
   292→    for stem, new_iris in sorted(source_new.items()):
   293→        out_path = out_dir / f"{stem}_terms.txt"
   294→        merged   = dedup_merge(read_existing(out_path), new_iris)
   295→        out_path.write_text("\n".join(merged) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   296→        print(f"  {out_path.name}: {len(merged)} IRIs total  ({len(new_iris)} from CSVs)")
   297→
   298→    # Write / update rda_terms.txt
   299→    rda_path   = out_dir / "rda_terms.txt"
   300→    rda_merged = dedup_merge(read_existing(rda_path), rda_new)
   301→    rda_path.write_text("\n".join(rda_merged) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   302→    print(f"  rda_terms.txt: {len(rda_merged)} IRIs total  ({len(rda_new)} from CSVs)")
   303→
   304→
   305→if __name__ == "__main__":
   306→    main()
   307→



```

  "content": "\"\"\"\ngen_rda_terms.py\n\nRead all output/mapping_*to_rda.csv files and write per-namespace\nodk-imports/rda*_terms.txt files.\n\nRouting rules (applied to the expanded RDA IRI):\n  Elements/<L>/          → rda<L>_terms.txt   (e.g. rdaw_terms.txt)\n  Elements/<L>/object/   → rda<L>o_terms.txt  (e.g. rdawo_terms.txt)\n  Elements/<L>/datatype/ → rda<L>d_terms.txt  (e.g. rdawd_terms.txt)\n\nCURIE prefix → base URI:\n  rdac / rdaco → http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/  (classes: http)\n  rdaa         → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/\n  rdae         → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/\n  rdai         → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/\n  rdam         → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/\n  rdaw         → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/\n\nOnly rows with match = \"direct\" or \"approximate\" are included.\nExisting file contents are preserved; new IRIs are appended (deduplicated).\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/gen_rda_terms.py\n    python scripts/gen_rda_terms.py \\\\\n        --csv-dir output \\\\\n        --out-dir odk-imports\n\nInputs:\n    <csv-dir>/mapping_*to_rda.csv\n\nOutputs:\n    <out-dir>/rda<letter>_terms.txt    one file per Elements namespace letter\n    <out-dir>/rda<letter>o_terms.txt   object sub-properties (if any)\n    <out-dir>/rda<letter>d_terms.txt   datatype sub-properties (if any)\n\nDependencies: Python standard library only.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CURIE → base URI\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nPREFIX_TO_NS: dict[str, str] = {\n    \"rdac\":  \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/\",   # classes: http\n    \"rdaco\": \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/\",   # ACO-CSV alias for rdac\n    \"rdaa\":  \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/\",\n    \"rdae\":  \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/\",\n    \"rdai\":  \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/\",\n    \"rdam\":  \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/\",\n    \"rdaw\":  \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/\",\n}\n\n# Matches any known RDA prefix followed by a local name (possibly with sub-path)\n_PREFIXES = \"|\".join(re.escape(p) for p in PREFIX_TO_NS)\nCURIE_RE = re.compile(rf'({_PREFIXES}):([\\w/]+)')\n\n# Matches the Elements path in an expanded IRI\nELEMENTS_RE = re.compile(\n    r'rdaregistry\\.info/Elements/([a-z])(?:/(object|datatype))?/'\n)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# IRI helpers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef expand_rda_cell(cell: str) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract and expand all RDA CURIEs from a semicolon-separated cell.\"\"\"\n    iris: list[str] = []\n    for token in cell.split(\";\"):\n        m = CURIE_RE.search(token)\n        if m:\n            prefix, local = m.group(1), m.group(2)\n            ns = PREFIX_TO_NS[prefix]\n            iris.append(ns + local)\n    return iris\n\n\ndef iri_to_stem(iri: str) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Map an expanded RDA IRI to an output file stem.\n\n    Examples:\n      http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/C10001      → 'rdac'\n      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/P10072     → 'rdaw'\n      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/object/P10072   → 'rdawo'\n      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/datatype/P10072 → 'rdawd'\n    \"\"\"\n    m = ELEMENTS_RE.search(iri)\n    if not m:\n        return None\n    letter = m.group(1)\n    subpath = m.group(2)  # 'object', 'datatype', or None\n    if subpath == \"object\":\n        return f\"rda{letter}o\"\n    if subpath == \"datatype\":\n        return f\"rda{letter}d\"\n    return f\"rda{letter}\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# File I/O\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef read_existing(path: Path) -> list[str]:\n    if not path.exists():\n        return []\n    return [ln.strip() for ln in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n            if ln.strip()]\n\n\ndef dedup(lst: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    seen: set[str] = set()\n    out: list[str] = []\n    for x in lst:\n        if x not in seen:\n            seen.add(x)\n            out.append(x)\n    return out\n\n\ndef dedup_merge(existing: list[str], new: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    seen = set(existing)\n    result = list(existing)\n    for iri in new:\n        if iri not in seen:\n            seen.add(iri)\n            result.append(iri)\n    return result\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CSV processing\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nVALID_MATCH = {\"direct\", \"approximate\"}\n\n\ndef process_csv(csv_path: Path, collected: dict[str, list[str]]) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse one mapping CSV and accumulate RDA IRIs into `collected`.\n    Returns the number of IRIs extracted.\n    \"\"\"\n    with open(csv_path, newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(fh)\n        header = reader.fieldnames or []\n\n        # DCT schema: dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label — no match column\n        if \"rda_iri\" in header:\n            count = 0\n            for row in reader:\n                iri = row.get(\"rda_iri\", \"\").strip()\n                if not iri:\n                    continue\n                stem = iri_to_stem(iri)\n                if stem:\n                    collected.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)\n                    count += 1\n            return count\n\n        # Standard / CRM schema: match column present\n        if \"match\" not in header:\n            return 0\n\n        count = 0\n        for row in reader:\n            if row.get(\"match\", \"\").strip() not in VALID_MATCH:\n                continue\n\n            # CRM schema provides rda_uri directly; standard schema uses rda cell\n            rda_uri = row.get(\"rda_uri\", \"\").strip()\n            if rda_uri:\n                iris = [rda_uri]\n            else:\n                iris = expand_rda_cell(row.get(\"rda\", \"\").strip())\n\n            for iri in iris:\n                stem = iri_to_stem(iri)\n                if stem:\n                    collected.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)\n                    count += 1\n\n    return count\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Main\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--csv-dir\",\n        default=\"output\",\n        metavar=\"DIR\",\n        help=\"Directory containing mapping_*to_rda.csv files. Default: %(default)s\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--out-dir\",\n        default=\"odk-imports\",\n        metavar=\"DIR\",\n        help=\"Output directory for rda*_terms.txt files. Default: %(default)s\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    csv_dir = Path(args.csv_dir)\n    out_dir = Path(args.out_dir)\n    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    csv_files = sorted(csv_dir.glob(\"mapping_*to_rda.csv\"))\n    if not csv_files:\n        raise SystemExit(f\"No mapping_*to_rda.csv files found in {csv_dir}\")\n\n    collected: dict[str, list[str]] = {}\n\n    for csv_path in csv_files:\n        n = process_csv(csv_path, collected)\n        print(f\"  {csv_path.name}: {n} RDA IRI(s)\")\n\n    print()\n\n    # Deduplicate collected lists\n    for stem in list(collected):\n        collected[stem] = dedup(collected[stem])\n\n    # Merge with existing files and write\n    for stem, new_iris in sorted(collected.items()):\n        out_path = out_dir / f\"{stem}_terms.txt\"\n        merged = dedup_merge(read_existing(out_path), new_iris)\n        out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(merged) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        print(f\"  {out_path.name}: {len(merged)} IRIs  ({len(new_iris)} from CSVs)\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}

Produces one file per source ontology prefix (`aco_terms.txt`, `crm_terms.txt`, `dct_terms.txt`, `fabio_terms.txt`, `frbr_terms.txt`, `mo_terms.txt`, `rico_terms.txt`) plus a merged `rda_terms.txt` for all RDA target IRIs. /Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/README.md:265:**Purpose**: Generate the VRA Core 4.0 → RDA mapping tables (CSV and JSON). VRA Core's class hierarchy uses `schema:CreativeWork` and `foaf:Agent` — not FRBR — so the automated `gen_frbr_target_mapping.py` produces no output for it. This script provides the hand-curated alignment following the same static ROWS pattern as `gen_nonwemi_mapping.py`. Covers 7 VRA classes and 65 properties across creator roles, part-whole, derivative, subject, place, identification, and physical/technical groups; 48 approximate mappings and 24 no-match entries.

### Assistant *(2026-03-18T15:55:48.365Z)*

```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/gen_rda_terms.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"\ngen_rda_terms.py\n\nRead all output/mapping_*to_rda.csv files and write per-namespace\nodk-imports/rda*_terms.txt files.\n\nRouting rules (applied to the expanded RDA IRI):\n  Elements/<L>/          → rda<L>_terms.txt   (e.g. rdaw_terms.txt)\n  Elements/<L>/object/   → rda<L>o_terms.txt  (e.g. rdawo_terms.txt)\n  Elements/<L>/datatype/ → rda<L>d_terms.txt  (e.g. rdawd_terms.txt)\n\nCURIE prefix → base URI:\n  rdac  → http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/  (classes: http, not https)\n  rdaa  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/\n  rdae  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/\n  rdai  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/\n  rdam  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/\n  rdaw  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/\n\nOnly rows with match = \"direct\" or \"approximate\" are included, except for\nCSVs with the DCT schema (dct_term / rda_iri columns), which are pre-filtered.\nExisting file contents are preserved; new IRIs are appended (deduplicated).\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/gen_rda_terms.py\n    python scripts/gen_rda_terms.py \\\\\n        --csv-dir output \\\\\n        --out-dir odk-imports\n\nInputs:\n    <csv-dir>/mapping_*to_rda.csv\n\nOutputs:\n    <out-dir>/rda<letter>_terms.txt    one file per Elements namespace letter\n    <out-dir>/rda<letter>o_terms.txt   object sub-properties (if any)\n    <out-dir>/rda<letter>d_terms.txt   datatype sub-properties (if any)\n\nDependencies: Python standard library only.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CURIE → base URI\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nPREFIX_TO_NS: dict[str, str] = {\n    \"rdac\": \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/\",   # classes: http\n    \"rdaa\": \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/\",\n    \"rdae\": \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/\",\n    \"rdai\": \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/\",\n    \"rdam\": \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/\",\n    \"rdaw\": \"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/\",\n}\n\n_PREFIXES_PATTERN = \"|\".join(re.escape(p) for p in PREFIX_TO_NS)\nCURIE_RE = re.compile(rf'({_PREFIXES_PATTERN}):([\\w/]+)')\n\n# Matches the Elements path in an expanded IRI\nELEMENTS_RE = re.compile(\n    r'rdaregistry\\.info/Elements/([a-z])(?:/(object|datatype))?/'\n)\n\nVALID_MATCH = {\"direct\", \"approximate\"}\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# IRI helpers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef expand_rda_cell(cell: str) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract and expand all RDA CURIEs from a semicolon-separated cell.\"\"\"\n    iris: list[str] = []\n    for token in cell.split(\";\"):\n        m = CURIE_RE.search(token)\n        if m:\n            prefix, local = m.group(1), m.group(2)\n            iris.append(PREFIX_TO_NS[prefix] + local)\n    return iris\n\n\ndef iri_to_stem(iri: str) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Map an expanded RDA IRI to an output file stem, or None if unrecognised.\n\n    Examples:\n      http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/C10001           → 'rdac'\n      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/P10072          → 'rdaw'\n      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/object/P10072   → 'rdawo'\n      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/datatype/P10072 → 'rdawd'\n    \"\"\"\n    m = ELEMENTS_RE.search(iri)\n    if not m:\n        return None\n    letter, subpath = m.group(1), m.group(2)\n    if subpath == \"object\":\n        return f\"rda{letter}o\"\n    if subpath == \"datatype\":\n        return f\"rda{letter}d\"\n    return f\"rda{letter}\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# File I/O\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef read_existing(path: Path) -> list[str]:\n    if not path.exists():\n        return []\n    return [ln.strip() for ln in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n            if ln.strip()]\n\n\ndef dedup(lst: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    seen: set[str] = set()\n    out: list[str] = []\n    for x in lst:\n        if x not in seen:\n            seen.add(x)\n            out.append(x)\n    return out\n\n\ndef dedup_merge(existing: list[str], new: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    seen = set(existing)\n    result = list(existing)\n    for iri in new:\n        if iri not in seen:\n            seen.add(iri)\n            result.append(iri)\n    return result\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CSV processing\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef process_csv(csv_path: Path, collected: dict[str, list[str]]) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse one mapping CSV and accumulate RDA IRIs into `collected`.\n    Returns the number of IRIs extracted.\n    \"\"\"\n    with open(csv_path, newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(fh)\n        fieldnames = reader.fieldnames or []\n\n        # DCT schema: dct_term / rda_iri columns, no match filter needed\n        if \"rda_iri\" in fieldnames:\n            count = 0\n            for row in reader:\n                iri = row.get(\"rda_iri\", \"\").strip()\n                stem = iri_to_stem(iri)\n                if stem:\n                    collected.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)\n                    count += 1\n            return count\n\n        # Standard schema: source / rda / match columns\n        if \"match\" not in fieldnames:\n            return 0\n\n        count = 0\n        for row in reader:\n            if row.get(\"match\", \"\").strip() not in VALID_MATCH:\n                continue\n\n            # CRM-extended schema provides rda_uri directly\n            rda_uri = row.get(\"rda_uri\", \"\").strip()\n            iris = [rda_uri] if rda_uri else expand_rda_cell(row.get(\"rda\", \"\").strip())\n\n            for iri in iris:\n                stem = iri_to_stem(iri)\n                if stem:\n                    collected.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)\n                    count += 1\n\n    return count\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Main\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--csv-dir\",\n        default=\"output\",\n        metavar=\"DIR\",\n        help=\"Directory containing mapping_*to_rda.csv files (default: %(default)s)\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--out-dir\",\n        default=\"odk-imports\",\n        metavar=\"DIR\",\n        help=\"Output directory for rda*_terms.txt files (default: %(default)s)\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    csv_dir = Path(args.csv_dir)\n    out_dir = Path(args.out_dir)\n    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    csv_files = sorted(csv_dir.glob(\"mapping_*to_rda.csv\"))\n    if not csv_files:\n        raise SystemExit(f\"No mapping_*to_rda.csv files found in {csv_dir}\")\n\n    collected: dict[str, list[str]] = {}\n\n    for csv_path in csv_files:\n        n = process_csv(csv_path, collected)\n        print(f\"  {csv_path.name}: {n} RDA IRI(s)\")\n\n    print()\n\n    for stem in list(collected):\n        collected[stem] = dedup(collected[stem])\n\n    for stem, new_iris in sorted(collected.items()):\n        out_path = out_dir / f\"{stem}_terms.txt\"\n        merged = dedup_merge(read_existing(out_path), new_iris)\n        out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(merged) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        print(f\"  {out_path.name}: {len(merged)} IRIs  ({len(new_iris)} from CSVs)\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-03-18T15:57:14.406Z)*

Done. Summary of what was done:

**Bug fix**: `mapping_aco_to_rda.csv` had a typo — `rdaco:` instead of `rdac:` in 10 places. Fixed with `sed`. No script used that alias; the CSV was hand-authored incorrectly.

**New script**: [scripts/gen_rda_terms.py](scripts/gen_rda_terms.py)
- Reads all `output/mapping_*to_rda.csv`
- Routes each RDA IRI to `odk-imports/rda<letter>_terms.txt` (or `rda<letter>o_terms.txt` / `rda<letter>d_terms.txt` for object/datatype sub-properties, if they appear in future CSVs)
- Handles both standard schema (`rda` cell with CURIEs) and DCT schema (`rda_iri` column)
- Merges with existing file contents; run is idempotent

Results: 6 per-namespace files updated (rdaa: 9, rdac: 8, rdae: 350, rdai: 36, rdam: 302, rdaw: 372 IRIs). No object/datatype sub-property files were created because none appear in the current CSVs.

   440→# Row generation
   441→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   442→
   443→def build_class_rows(
   444→    g: Graph,
   445→    target_ns: str,
   446→    target_prefix: str,
   447→    frbr_to_rda: dict[str, dict],
   448→) -> list[dict]:
   449→    rows = []
   450→
   451→    for cls_uri in sorted(g.subjects(RDF.type, OWL.Class)):
   452→        if not isinstance(cls_uri, URIRef) or not str(cls_uri).startswith(target_ns):
   453→            continue
   454→
   455→        prefixed = iri_to_prefixed(str(cls_uri), target_prefix, target_ns)
   456→        source = prefixed + " (class)"
   457→        comment = get_first_sentence(cls_uri, g)
   458→
   459→        cls_str = str(cls_uri)
   460→        if cls_str in FRBR_CLASS_ANCHORS:
   461→            lbl, group, rda_key = FRBR_CLASS_ANCHORS[cls_str]
   462→            anchor, is_direct = lbl, True
   463→        else:
   464→            anchor, group, rda_key, is_direct = find_class_anchor(cls_uri, g)
   465→        rda = ""
   466→        match = "no match"
   467→        remarks = ""
   468→
   469→        if group == "G1" and rda_key:
   470→            wemi_entry = WEMI_CLASS_TO_RDA.get(rda_key)
   471→            if wemi_entry:
   472→                rda, frbr_rda_match = wemi_entry
   473→                via_foaf = anchor and anchor.startswith("foaf:")
   474→                if is_direct and not via_foaf and frbr_rda_match == "direct":
   475→                    match = "direct"
   476→                    remarks = f"Direct rdfs:subClassOf {anchor}."
   477→                else:
   478→                    match = "approximate"
   479→                    if anchor == prefixed:
   480→                        # Class is itself the alignment anchor (e.g. rico:RecordResource)
   481→                        parts = [p.strip() for p in rda.split(";")]
   482→                        remarks = "[B: cross-standard alignment] Aligned with " + "; ".join(parts) + "."
   483→                    else:
   484→                        crit = "[E: transitive subtype] " if not is_direct else ""
   485→                        remarks = (
   486→                            f"{crit}Via {prefixed} → {anchor} → {rda.split()[0]}; "
   487→                            "no specific RDA sub-class for this subtype."
   488→                        )
   489→
   490→        elif group == "G2" and rda_key:
   491→            entry = frbr_to_rda.get(rda_key, {})
   492→            rda = entry.get("rda", "")
   493→            if rda:
   494→                via_foaf = anchor and anchor.startswith("foaf:")
   495→                is_frbr_direct = entry.get("match") == "direct"
   496→                if is_direct and not via_foaf and is_frbr_direct:
   497→                    match = "direct"
   498→                    remarks = f"Direct rdfs:subClassOf {anchor}."
   499→                else:
   500→                    match = "approximate"
   501→                    crit = "[E: transitive subtype] " if not is_direct else "[B: semantic narrowing] "
   502→                    proxy_note = f" (foaf proxy: {anchor} ≈ {rda_key.replace(' (class)', '')})" if via_foaf else ""
   503→                    remarks = (
   504→                        f"{crit}Via {anchor}{proxy_note} → {rda.split()[0]}."
   505→                    )
   506→            else:
   507→                match = "no match"
   508→                remarks = f"Via {anchor} (FRBR Group 2); no RDA class equivalent in mapping CSV."
   509→
   510→        elif group == "G3" and rda_key:
   511→            entry = frbr_to_rda.get(rda_key, {})
   512→            rda = entry.get("rda", "")
   513→            if rda:
   514→                match = "approximate"
   515→                remarks = f"[E: transitive subtype] Via {anchor} (FRBR Group 3) → {rda.split()[0]}."
   516→            else:
   517→                match = "no match"
   518→                remarks = f"Via {anchor} (FRBR Group 3); no RDA class equivalent."
   519→

     1→"""
     2→gen_rda_terms.py

     4→Read all output/mapping_*to_rda.csv files and write per-namespace

     6→
     7→Routing rules (applied to the expanded RDA IRI):
     8→  Elements/<L>/          → rda<L>_terms.txt   (e.g. rdaw_terms.txt)
     9→  Elements/<L>/object/   → rda<L>o_terms.txt  (e.g. rdawo_terms.txt)

### User *(2026-03-18T16:35:24.763Z)*

**Tool result**
```
   181→## scripts/gen_all_import_terms.py
   182→
   183→**Purpose**: Scan all `output/mapping_*_to_*.csv` files and generate or update `mocho-odk/imports/<ontology>_terms.txt` files. Produces one file per source ontology prefix (`aco_terms.txt`, `crm_terms.txt`, `dct_terms.txt`, `fabio_terms.txt`, `frbr_terms.txt`, `mo_terms.txt`, `rico_terms.txt`) plus a merged `rda_terms.txt` for all RDA target IRIs. New IRIs are appended to existing files; nothing is overwritten or removed.
   184→
   185→**Usage**:
   186→```bash
   187→# Default: reads output/mapping_*_to_*.csv, writes to mocho-odk/imports/:
   188→python scripts/gen_all_import_terms.py
   189→
   190→# Custom paths:
   191→python scripts/gen_all_import_terms.py \
   192→    --output-dir output \
   193→    --out-dir mocho-odk/imports
   194→```
   195→
   196→**Options**:
   197→| Flag | Default | Description |
   198→|------|---------|-------------|
   199→| `--output-dir` | `output` | Directory containing `mapping_*_to_*.csv` files |
   200→| `--out-dir` | `mocho-odk/imports` | Directory to write `*_terms.txt` files |
   201→
   202→**Inputs**: All `output/mapping_*_to_*.csv` files. Three schemas are handled:
   203→- Standard (`source, rda, domain, range, match, remarks`): rows with `match` = direct or approximate only
   204→- Extended CRM (`adds source_uri, rda_uri`): same match filter; full URIs used directly
   205→- DCT (`dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label`): all rows included (file is pre-filtered during generation)
   206→- Vocabulary extraction files (`mapping_rda_*.csv`) have no `match` column and are silently skipped
   207→
   208→**Outputs**:
   209→- `<out-dir>/aco_terms.txt` — Audio Commons Ontology IRIs
   210→- `<out-dir>/crm_terms.txt` — CIDOC CRM class IRIs



```

   212→- `<out-dir>/fabio_terms.txt` — FaBiO IRIs
   213→- `<out-dir>/frbr_terms.txt` — FRBR Core IRIs
   214→- `<out-dir>/mo_terms.txt` — Music Ontology IRIs
   215→- `<out-dir>/rico_terms.txt` — RiC-O IRIs
   216→- `<out-dir>/rda_terms.txt` — RDA class and property IRIs (merged from all mapping files)
   217→
   218→**Dependencies**: Python standard library only (`csv`, `argparse`, `pathlib`, `re`).
   219→
   220→**Caveats**: RDA class IRIs use `http://` and property IRIs use `https://` (matching the RDA registry conventions used in existing mapping files). Adding a new source ontology requires extending the `SOURCE_PREFIX_TO_NS` and `PREFIX_TO_STEM` tables in the script.
   221→
   222→---
   223→
   224→## scripts/dct_rda_map.py
   225→
   226→**Purpose**: Read a CSV produced by `extract_ontology_terms.py`, collect every term whose `label` column starts with `dc:` or `dcterms:`, look up the corresponding RDA sub-properties in `mapElement2DCT.xml` (from the RDA-Vocabularies release), and write a flat mapping table. `dc:` labels are resolved against the `dcterms:` namespace because `mapElement2DCT.xml` only declares `dcterms:` super-properties; both the original prefix and the resolved IRI are preserved in the output. RDA property labels are fetched from the entity XML files in the same release.
   227→
   228→**Usage**:
   229→```bash



```

  "new_string": "---\n\n## scripts/gen_rda_terms.py\n\n**Purpose**: Read all `output/mapping_*to_rda.csv` files and write per-namespace `mocho-odk/imports/rda*_terms.txt` files, routing each RDA IRI by its `Elements/<letter>/` path. Replaces the single merged `rda_terms.txt` produced by `gen_all_import_terms.py` with one file per RDA namespace. New IRIs are appended to existing files; nothing is overwritten or removed.\n\n**Usage**:\n```bash\n# Default: reads output/mapping_*to_rda.csv, writes to mocho-odk/imports/:\npython scripts/gen_rda_terms.py\n\n# Custom paths:\npython scripts/gen_rda_terms.py \\\n    --csv-dir output \\\n    --out-dir mocho-odk/imports\n```\n\n**Options**:\n| Flag | Default | Description |\n|------|---------|-------------|\n| `--csv-dir` | `output` | Directory containing `mapping_*to_rda.csv` files |\n| `--out-dir` | `mocho-odk/imports` | Directory to write `rda*_terms.txt` files |\n\n**Routing**:\n| IRI path pattern | Output file |\n|-----------------|-------------|\n| `Elements/<L>/` | `rda<L>_terms.txt` |\n| `Elements/<L>/object/` | `rda<L>o_terms.txt` |\n| `Elements/<L>/datatype/` | `rda<L>d_terms.txt` |\n\n**Outputs** (one per namespace letter found across all CSVs):\n- `rdac_terms.txt` — RDA classes (`http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/`)\n- `rdaa_terms.txt` — RDA Agent properties\n- `rdae_terms.txt` — RDA Expression properties\n- `rdai_terms.txt` — RDA Item properties\n- `rdam_terms.txt` — RDA Manifestation properties\n- `rdaw_terms.txt` — RDA Work properties\n\n**Dependencies**: Python standard library only (`csv`, `argparse`, `pathlib`, `re`).\n\n---\n\n## scripts/dct_rda_map.py"
}

### User *(2026-03-18T17:14:26.586Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→"""
     2→gen_rda_terms.py
     3→
     4→Read all output/mapping_*to_rda.csv files and write per-namespace
     5→mocho-odk/imports/rda*_terms.txt files.
     6→
     7→Routing rules (applied to the expanded RDA IRI):
     8→  Elements/<L>/          → rda<L>_terms.txt   (e.g. rdaw_terms.txt)
     9→  Elements/<L>/object/   → rda<L>o_terms.txt  (e.g. rdawo_terms.txt)
    10→  Elements/<L>/datatype/ → rda<L>d_terms.txt  (e.g. rdawd_terms.txt)
    11→
    12→CURIE prefix → base URI:
    13→  rdac  → http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/  (classes: http, not https)
    14→  rdaa  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/
    15→  rdae  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/
    16→  rdai  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/
    17→  rdam  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/
    18→  rdaw  → https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/
    19→
    20→Only rows with match = "direct" or "approximate" are included, except for
    21→CSVs with the DCT schema (dct_term / rda_iri columns), which are pre-filtered.
    22→Existing file contents are preserved; new IRIs are appended (deduplicated).
    23→
    24→Usage:
    25→    python scripts/gen_rda_terms.py
    26→    python scripts/gen_rda_terms.py \\
    27→        --csv-dir output \\
    28→        --out-dir mocho-odk/imports
    29→
    30→Inputs:
    31→    <csv-dir>/mapping_*to_rda.csv
    32→
    33→Outputs:
    34→    <out-dir>/rda<letter>_terms.txt    one file per Elements namespace letter
    35→    <out-dir>/rda<letter>o_terms.txt   object sub-properties (if any)
    36→    <out-dir>/rda<letter>d_terms.txt   datatype sub-properties (if any)
    37→
    38→Dependencies: Python standard library only.
    39→"""
    40→
    41→import argparse
    42→import csv
    43→import re
    44→from pathlib import Path
    45→from typing import Optional
    46→
    47→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→# CURIE → base URI
    49→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→
    51→PREFIX_TO_NS: dict[str, str] = {
    52→    "rdac": "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/",   # classes: http
    53→    "rdaa": "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/",
    54→    "rdae": "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/",
    55→    "rdai": "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/i/",
    56→    "rdam": "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/",
    57→    "rdaw": "https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/",
    58→}
    59→
    60→_PREFIXES_PATTERN = "|".join(re.escape(p) for p in PREFIX_TO_NS)
    61→CURIE_RE = re.compile(rf'({_PREFIXES_PATTERN}):([\w/]+)')
    62→
    63→# Matches the Elements path in an expanded IRI
    64→ELEMENTS_RE = re.compile(
    65→    r'rdaregistry\.info/Elements/([a-z])(?:/(object|datatype))?/'
    66→)
    67→
    68→VALID_MATCH = {"direct", "approximate"}
    69→
    70→
    71→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→# IRI helpers
    73→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→def expand_rda_cell(cell: str) -> list[str]:
    76→    """Extract and expand all RDA CURIEs from a semicolon-separated cell."""
    77→    iris: list[str] = []
    78→    for token in cell.split(";"):
    79→        m = CURIE_RE.search(token)
    80→        if m:
    81→            prefix, local = m.group(1), m.group(2)
    82→            iris.append(PREFIX_TO_NS[prefix] + local)
    83→    return iris
    84→
    85→
    86→def iri_to_stem(iri: str) -> Optional[str]:
    87→    """
    88→    Map an expanded RDA IRI to an output file stem, or None if unrecognised.
    89→
    90→    Examples:
    91→      http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/C10001           → 'rdac'
    92→      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/P10072          → 'rdaw'
    93→      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/object/P10072   → 'rdawo'
    94→      https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/datatype/P10072 → 'rdawd'
    95→    """
    96→    m = ELEMENTS_RE.search(iri)
    97→    if not m:
    98→        return None
    99→    letter, subpath = m.group(1), m.group(2)
   100→    if subpath == "object":
   101→        return f"rda{letter}o"
   102→    if subpath == "datatype":
   103→        return f"rda{letter}d"
   104→    return f"rda{letter}"
   105→
   106→
   107→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→# File I/O
   109→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→def read_existing(path: Path) -> list[str]:
   112→    if not path.exists():
   113→        return []
   114→    return [ln.strip() for ln in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
   115→            if ln.strip()]
   116→
   117→
   118→def dedup(lst: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   119→    seen: set[str] = set()
   120→    out: list[str] = []
   121→    for x in lst:
   122→        if x not in seen:
   123→            seen.add(x)
   124→            out.append(x)
   125→    return out
   126→
   127→
   128→def dedup_merge(existing: list[str], new: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   129→    seen = set(existing)
   130→    result = list(existing)
   131→    for iri in new:
   132→        if iri not in seen:
   133→            seen.add(iri)
   134→            result.append(iri)
   135→    return result
   136→
   137→
   138→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→# CSV processing
   140→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   141→
   142→def process_csv(csv_path: Path, collected: dict[str, list[str]]) -> int:
   143→    """
   144→    Parse one mapping CSV and accumulate RDA IRIs into `collected`.
   145→    Returns the number of IRIs extracted.
   146→    """
   147→    with open(csv_path, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
   148→        reader = csv.DictReader(fh)
   149→        fieldnames = reader.fieldnames or []
   150→
   151→        # DCT schema: dct_term / rda_iri columns, no match filter needed
   152→        if "rda_iri" in fieldnames:
   153→            count = 0
   154→            for row in reader:
   155→                iri = row.get("rda_iri", "").strip()
   156→                stem = iri_to_stem(iri)
   157→                if stem:
   158→                    collected.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)
   159→                    count += 1
   160→            return count
   161→
   162→        # Standard schema: source / rda / match columns
   163→        if "match" not in fieldnames:
   164→            return 0
   165→
   166→        count = 0
   167→        for row in reader:
   168→            if row.get("match", "").strip() not in VALID_MATCH:
   169→                continue
   170→
   171→            # CRM-extended schema provides rda_uri directly
   172→            rda_uri = row.get("rda_uri", "").strip()
   173→            iris = [rda_uri] if rda_uri else expand_rda_cell(row.get("rda", "").strip())
   174→
   175→            for iri in iris:
   176→                stem = iri_to_stem(iri)
   177→                if stem:
   178→                    collected.setdefault(stem, []).append(iri)
   179→                    count += 1
   180→
   181→    return count
   182→
   183→
   184→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   185→# Main
   186→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   187→
   188→def main() -> None:
   189→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
   190→    parser.add_argument(
   191→        "--csv-dir",
   192→        default="output",
   193→        metavar="DIR",
   194→        help="Directory containing mapping_*to_rda.csv files (default: %(default)s)",
   195→    )
   196→    parser.add_argument(
   197→        "--out-dir",
   198→        default="mocho-odk/imports",
   199→        metavar="DIR",
   200→        help="Output directory for rda*_terms.txt files (default: %(default)s)",
   201→    )
   202→    args = parser.parse_args()
   203→
   204→    csv_dir = Path(args.csv_dir)
   205→    out_dir = Path(args.out_dir)
   206→    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   207→
   208→    csv_files = sorted(csv_dir.glob("mapping_*to_rda.csv"))
   209→    if not csv_files:
   210→        raise SystemExit(f"No mapping_*to_rda.csv files found in {csv_dir}")
   211→
   212→    collected: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
   213→
   214→    for csv_path in csv_files:
   215→        n = process_csv(csv_path, collected)
   216→        print(f"  {csv_path.name}: {n} RDA IRI(s)")
   217→
   218→    print()
   219→
   220→    for stem in list(collected):
   221→        collected[stem] = dedup(collected[stem])
   222→
   223→    for stem, new_iris in sorted(collected.items()):
   224→        out_path = out_dir / f"{stem}_terms.txt"
   225→        merged = dedup_merge(read_existing(out_path), new_iris)
   226→        out_path.write_text("\n".join(merged) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   227→        print(f"  {out_path.name}: {len(merged)} IRIs  ({len(new_iris)} from CSVs)")
   228→
   229→
   230→if __name__ == "__main__":
   231→    main()
   232→



```

<out-dir>/rda<letter>d_terms.txt   datatype sub-properties\n\nDependencies: Python standard library only.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing re.compile(rf'({_PREFIXES_PATTERN}):([\\w/]+)')\n\n# Matches the Elements path in an expanded IRI\nELEMENTS_RE = re.compile(\n    r'rdaregistry\\.info/Elements/([a-z])(?:/(object|datatype))?/'\n)\n\n# Matches rdf:about=\"...\" in XML source files\nABOUT_RE = re.compile(r'rdf:about=\"([^\"]*)\"')\n\nVALID_MATCH = {\"direct\", \"approximate\"}\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# XML index: maps local ID → which file it lives in\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass XmlIndex:\n    \"\"\"\n    Lazy cache of local IDs declared in each rda-5.4.9 XML Elements file.\n    Keyed by file path; values are frozensets of local IDs (e.g. 'P10072').\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, xml_dir: Path) -> None:\n        self._xml_dir = xml_dir\n        self._cache: dict[Path, frozenset] = {}\n\n    def _ids(self, rel: str) -> frozenset:\n        p = self._xml_dir / rel\n        if p not in self._cache:\n            if not p.exists():\n                self._cache[p] = frozenset()\n # Property IRI without sub-path: validate against XML\n        stem = self.route(local_id, letter)\n        if stem is None:\n            return None\n\n        # Construct canonical IRI with sub-path if needed\n        base_ns = f\"https://rdaregistry.info/Elements/{letter}/\"\n        if stem == f\"rda{letter}d\":\n            canonical = base_ns + f\"datatype/{local_id}\"\n        elif stem == f\"rda{letter}o\":\n            canonical = base_ns + f\"object/{local_id}\"\n        else:\n            canonical = iri  # keep original (preserves http/https of source)\n        return stem, out.append(x)\n    return out\n\n\ndef dedup_merge(existing: list[str], new: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    seen = set(existing)\n    result = list(existing)\n    for iri in new:\n        if iri not in seen:\n            seen.add(iri)\n            result.append(iri)\n    return result\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CSV processing\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef expand_rda_cell(cell: str) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract and expand all RDA CURIEs from a semicolon-separated cell.\"\"\"\n    iris: list[str] = []\n    for token in cell.split(\";\"):\n        m = CURIE_RE.search(token)\n        if m:\n            prefix, local = m.group(1), m.group(2)\n int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse one mapping CSV and accumulate (stem → [iri]) into `collected`.\n    IRIs not found in any XML are appended to `skipped`.\n    Returns the number of IRIs successfully ### Assistant *(2026-03-18T17:21:24.992Z)*

The script derives the filename from `vann:preferredNamespacePrefix` in the ontology, which the ACO file declares as `ac` — not `aco`. So without `--ns-prefix aco`, it will still produce `mapping_ac_to_rda.csv` if re-run.

The `--ns-prefix aco` flag must be passed explicitly every time. Two options:

**A.** Add a prefix alias table to the script so `ac` maps to `aco` for the ACO namespace.

**B.** Just document the explicit invocation and rely on the caller to always pass `--ns-prefix aco`.

Which do you prefer?

### Assistant *(2026-03-18T17:35:22.217Z)*

```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/gen_doco_terms.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"\ngen_doco_terms.py\n\nExtract DoCO class URIs from a ddb-type2fabio.json mapping file and write\nthem to mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt.\n\nReads type_to_fabio.<term>.ontology_uri and collects every value whose IRI\nstarts with http://purl.org/spar/doco/. Existing file contents are preserved;\nnew IRIs are appended (deduplicated).\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/gen_doco_terms.py\n    python scripts/gen_doco_terms.py \\\\\n        --json path/to/ddb-type2fabio.json \\\\\n        --out  mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt\n\nInputs:\n    ddb-type2fabio.json   DDB type → FaBiO/DoCO mapping produced by the\n                          goethe-faust project\n\nOutputs:\n    mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt\n\nDependencies: Python standard library only.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport json\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nDOCO_NS = \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\"\nDEFAULT_JSON = (\n    Path.home() / \"Documents/claude/goethe-faust/output/ddb-type2fabio.json\"\n)\nDEFAULT_OUT = Path(\"mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt\")\n\n\ndef read_existing(path: Path) -> list:\n    if not path.exists():\n        return []\n    return [ln.strip() for ln in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n            if ln.strip()]\n\n\ndef dedup_merge(existing: list, new: list) -> list:\n    seen = set(existing)\n    result = list(existing)\n    for iri in new:\n        if iri not in seen:\n            seen.add(iri)\n            result.append(iri)\n    return result\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--json\",\n        default=str(DEFAULT_JSON),\n        metavar=\"FILE\",\n        help=\"ddb-type2fabio.json input file (default: %(default)s)\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--out\",\n        default=str(DEFAULT_OUT),\n        metavar=\"FILE\",\n        help=\"Output terms file (default: %(default)s)\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    json_path = Path(args.json)\n    out_path  = Path(args.out)\n\n    if not json_path.exists():\n        raise SystemExit(f\"Input file not found: {json_path}\")\n\n    data = json.loads(json_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n    type_to_fabio = data.get(\"type_to_fabio\", {})\n\n    new_iris = sorted({\n        entry[\"ontology_uri\"]\n        for entry in type_to_fabio.values()\n        if isinstance(entry.get(\"ontology_uri\"), str)\n        and entry[\"ontology_uri\"].startswith(DOCO_NS)\n    })\n\n    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    merged = dedup_merge(read_existing(out_path), new_iris)\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(merged) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    print(f\"  {out_path.name}: {len(merged)} IRIs  ({len(new_iris)} from JSON)\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/scripts/gen_doco_terms.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"\ngen_doco_terms.py\n\nExtract DoCO class URIs from two mapping files and write them to\nmocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt.\n\nSources:\n  ddb-type2fabio.json  — reads type_to_fabio.<term>.ontology_uri, collects\n                         every value starting with http://purl.org/spar/doco/\n\n  type2class.json      — reads <term>.class (list of strings/nested lists),\n                         extracts every token matching doco:\\w+ via regex and\n                         expands to http://purl.org/spar/doco/<LocalName>\n\nExisting file contents are preserved; new IRIs are appended (deduplicated).\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/gen_doco_terms.py\n    python scripts/gen_doco_terms.py \\\\\n        --type2fabio path/to/ddb-type2fabio.json \\\\\n        --type2class path/to/type2class.json \\\\\n        --out        mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt\n\nInputs:\n    ddb-type2fabio.json   DDB type → FaBiO/DoCO mapping (goethe-faust project)\n    type2class.json       DDB type → ontology class mapping (goethe-faust project)\n\nOutputs:\n    mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt\n\nDependencies: Python standard library only.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nDOCO_NS    = \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\"\nDOCO_CURIE = re.compile(r'doco:(\\w+)')\n\nDEFAULT_TYPE2FABIO = (\n    Path.home() / \"Documents/claude/goethe-faust/output/ddb-type2fabio.json\"\n)\nDEFAULT_TYPE2CLASS = (\n    Path.home() / \"Documents/claude/goethe-faust/data/type2class.json\"\n)\nDEFAULT_OUT = Path(\"mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt\")\n\n\ndef read_existing(path: Path) -> list:\n    if not path.exists():\n        return []\n    return [ln.strip() for ln in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n            if ln.strip()]\n\n\ndef dedup_merge(existing: list, new: list) -> list:\n    seen = set(existing)\n    result = list(existing)\n    for iri in new:\n        if iri not in seen:\n            seen.add(iri)\n            result.append(iri)\n    return result\n\n\ndef from_type2fabio(path: Path) -> set:\n    \"\"\"Collect doco IRIs from type_to_fabio.<term>.ontology_uri.\"\"\"\n    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n    return {\n        entry[\"ontology_uri\"]\n        for entry in data.get(\"type_to_fabio\", {}).values()\n        if isinstance(entry.get(\"ontology_uri\"), str)\n        and entry[\"ontology_uri\"].startswith(DOCO_NS)\n    }\n\n\ndef from_type2class(path: Path) -> set:\n    \"\"\"\n    Collect doco IRIs from <term>.class values.\n\n    Each entry's class field is a list whose elements may be strings or\n    nested lists; values can contain stray quotes (e.g. '\"doco:Section').\n    Uses a regex to extract doco:<LocalName> from any string token.\n    \"\"\"\n    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n    iris = set()\n    for entry in data.values():\n        for item in (entry.get(\"class\") or []):\n            for token in (item if isinstance(item, list) else [item]):\n                if isinstance(token, str):\n                    for local in DOCO_CURIE.findall(token):\n                        iris.add(DOCO_NS + local)\n    return iris\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--type2fabio\",\n        default=str(DEFAULT_TYPE2FABIO),\n        metavar=\"FILE\",\n        help=\"ddb-type2fabio.json (default: %(default)s)\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--type2class\",\n        default=str(DEFAULT_TYPE2CLASS),\n        metavar=\"FILE\",\n        help=\"type2class.json (default: %(default)s)\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--out\",\n        default=str(DEFAULT_OUT),\n        metavar=\"FILE\",\n        help=\"Output terms file (default: %(default)s)\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    out_path = Path(args.out)\n    new_iris: set = set()\n\n    p1 = Path(args.type2fabio)\n    if p1.exists():\n        iris1 = from_type2fabio(p1)\n        print(f\"  {p1.name}: {len(iris1)} doco IRI(s)\")\n        new_iris |= iris1\n    else:\n        print(f\"  {p1.name}: not found, skipping\")\n\n    p2 = Path(args.type2class)\n    if p2.exists():\n        iris2 = from_type2class(p2)\n        print(f\"  {p2.name}: {len(iris2)} doco IRI(s)\")\n        new_iris |= iris2\n    else:\n        print(f\"  {p2.name}: not found, skipping\")\n\n    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    merged = dedup_merge(read_existing(out_path), sorted(new_iris))\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(merged) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    print(f\"  {out_path.name}: {len(merged)} IRIs total\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-03-18T17:57:35.299Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/scripts/gen_htype_doco_mapping.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"\ngen_htype_doco_mapping.py\n\nMatch htype label_en values against DoCO ontology class labels using four\nstrategies and write a ranked mapping table.\n\nMatching strategies (applied in order; first hit wins per htype):\n  1. exact        — case-insensitive exact match\n  2. levenshtein  — RapidFuzz ratio >= 88 %\n  3. translated   — translate label_de (Helsinki-NLP/opus-mt-de-en), then\n                    exact match against DoCO labels\n  4. embedding    — cosine similarity of sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2\n                    embeddings; top candidate reported regardless of threshold\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/gen_htype_doco_mapping.py\n    python scripts/gen_htype_doco_mapping.py \\\\\n        --htypes   data/htype.csv \\\\\n        --doco     data/schemas/doco.owl \\\\\n        --out-csv  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/htype_to_doco_mapping.csv \\\\\n        --out-json ~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/htype_to_doco_mapping.json \\\\\n        --hf-cache data/hf-cache\n\nInputs:\n    data/htype.csv              htype_code, label_de, label_en\n    data/schemas/doco.owl       DoCO ontology (RDF/XML or Turtle)\n\nOutputs:\n    htype_to_doco_mapping.csv / .json\n\nColumns:\n    htype_code, label_de, label_en, match_method, confidence,\n    doco_class, doco_label, doco_uri, translated_en\n\nDependencies:\n    rdflib, rapidfuzz, sentence-transformers, transformers (HF), deep-translator\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport json\nimport os\nimport warnings\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# Suppress noisy warnings from torch / urllib3\nwarnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\")\nos.environ.setdefault(\"TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM\", \"false\")\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Argument parsing\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nWORKING_DIR  = Path.home() / \"Documents/claude/goethe-faust\"\nDEFAULT_OUT  = Path.home() / \"Documents/claude/mocho/output\"\n\ndef parse_args():\n    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\n    p.add_argument(\"--htypes\",   default=str(WORKING_DIR / \"data/htype.csv\"))\n    p.add_argument(\"--doco\",     default=str(WORKING_DIR / \"data/schemas/doco.owl\"))\n    p.add_argument(\"--out-csv\",  default=str(DEFAULT_OUT / \"htype_to_doco_mapping.csv\"))\n    p.add_argument(\"--out-json\", default=str(DEFAULT_OUT / \"htype_to_doco_mapping.json\"))\n    p.add_argument(\"--hf-cache\", default=str(WORKING_DIR / \"data/hf-cache\"),\n                   help=\"Hugging Face cache directory\")\n    p.add_argument(\"--embed-threshold\", type=float, default=0.0,\n                   help=\"Min cosine similarity for embedding match (default: report best regardless)\")\n    p.add_argument(\"--lev-threshold\", type=float, default=88.0,\n                   help=\"Min Levenshtein ratio %% for fuzzy match (default: %(default)s)\")\n    return p.parse_args()\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# DoCO label extraction\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef load_doco_labels(owl_path: str) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return list of {local, label, uri} for all DoCO classes.\"\"\"\n    from rdflib import Graph, RDFS, RDF, OWL, Namespace\n    DOCO = Namespace(\"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\")\n    g = Graph()\n    g.parse(owl_path)\n    rows = []\n    for subj, _, label in g.triples((None, RDFS.label, None)):\n        uri = str(subj)\n        if not uri.startswith(str(DOCO)):\n            continue\n        local = uri.split(\"/\")[-1]\n        rows.append({\"local\": local, \"label\": str(label), \"uri\": uri})\n    return sorted(rows, key=lambda r: r[\"label\"])\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Strategy 1: exact match\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef match_exact(query: str, candidates: list[dict]) -> dict | None:\n    q = query.strip().lower()\n    for c in candidates:\n        if c[\"label\"].lower() == q:\n            return {\"method\": \"exact\", \"confidence\": 1.0, **c}\n    return None\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Strategy 2: Levenshtein (RapidFuzz)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef match_levenshtein(query: str, candidates: list[dict], threshold: float) -> dict | None:\n    from rapidfuzz import fuzz\n    q = query.strip().lower()\n    best, best_score = None, 0.0\n    for c in candidates:\n        score = fuzz.ratio(q, c[\"label\"].lower())\n        if score > best_score:\n            best_score = score\n            best = c\n    if best and best_score >= threshold:\n        return {\"method\": \"levenshtein\", \"confidence\": round(best_score / 100, 4), **best}\n    return None\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Strategy 3: translate label_de → English, then exact match\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_translator = None\n\ndef get_translator(hf_cache: str):\n    global _translator\n    if _translator is None:\n        from transformers import MarianMTModel, MarianTokenizer\n        model_name = \"Helsinki-NLP/opus-mt-de-en\"\n        print(\"  Loading translation model …\")\n        tokenizer = MarianTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name, cache_dir=hf_cache)\n        model     = MarianMTModel.from_pretrained(model_name, cache_dir=hf_cache)\n        _translator = (tokenizer, model)\n    return _translator\n\ndef translate_de_en(texts: list[str], hf_cache: str) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Batch-translate German texts to English.\"\"\"\n    tokenizer, model = get_translator(hf_cache)\n    inputs = tokenizer(texts, return_tensors=\"pt\", padding=True, truncation=True)\n    outputs = model.generate(**inputs)\n    return [tokenizer.decode(o, skip_special_tokens=True) for o in outputs]\n\ndef match_translated(label_de: str, candidates: list[dict], hf_cache: str,\n                     translation_cache: dict) -> tuple[dict | None, str]:\n    \"\"\"Translate label_de if not cached, then exact-match against DoCO labels.\"\"\"\n    if label_de not in translation_cache:\n        translation_cache[label_de] = translate_de_en([label_de], hf_cache)[0]\n    translated = translation_cache[label_de]\n    hit = match_exact(translated, candidates)\n    if hit:\n        return {**hit, \"method\": \"translated\"}, translated\n    return None, translated\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Strategy 4: sentence embedding cosine similarity\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_embed_model = None\n\ndef get_embed_model(hf_cache: str):\n    global _embed_model\n    if _embed_model is None:\n        from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer\n        print(\"  Loading embedding model …\")\n        _embed_model = SentenceTransformer(\n            \"sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2\",\n            cache_folder=hf_cache,\n        )\n    return _embed_model\n\ndef match_embedding(query: str, candidates: list[dict], hf_cache: str,\n                    candidate_embeddings, threshold: float) -> dict | None:\n    import numpy as np\n    model = get_embed_model(hf_cache)\n    q_emb = model.encode([query.strip()], normalize_embeddings=True)[0]\n    sims  = candidate_embeddings @ q_emb          # dot product = cosine (normalized)\n    idx   = int(np.argmax(sims))\n    score = float(sims[idx])\n    if score >= threshold:\n        c = candidates[idx]\n        return {\"method\": \"embedding\", \"confidence\": round(score, 4), **c}\n    return None\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Main\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef main():\n    args = parse_args()\n\n    hf_cache   = args.hf_cache\n    lev_thresh = args.lev_threshold\n    emb_thresh = args.embed_threshold\n\n    # Load inputs\n    print(\"Loading DoCO labels …\")\n    candidates = load_doco_labels(args.doco)\n    print(f\"  {len(candidates)} DoCO classes\")\n\n    htypes = []\n    with open(args.htypes, newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n            htypes.append(row)\n    print(f\"  {len(htypes)} htype rows\")\n\n    # Pre-compute candidate embeddings once\n    print(\"Computing DoCO label embeddings …\")\n    model = get_embed_model(hf_cache)\n    import numpy as np\n    cand_labels     = [c[\"label\"] for c in candidates]\n    cand_embeddings = model.encode(cand_labels, normalize_embeddings=True,\n                                   show_progress_bar=False)\n\n    translation_cache: dict[str, str] = {}\n    rows = []\n\n    print(\"Matching …\")\n    for ht in htypes:\n        code     = ht[\"htype_code\"].strip()\n        label_de = ht[\"label_de\"].strip()\n        label_en = ht[\"label_en\"].strip()\n\n        translated_en = \"\"\n        hit = None\n\n        # 1. Exact\n        hit = match_exact(label_en, candidates)\n\n        # 2. Levenshtein\n        if not hit:\n            hit = match_levenshtein(label_en, candidates, lev_thresh)\n\n        # 3. Translated + exact\n        if not hit:\n            hit, translated_en = match_translated(label_de, candidates,\n                                                   hf_cache, translation_cache)\n\n        # 4. Embedding (always runs; fills translated_en if not already set)\n        if not hit:\n            if not translated_en:\n                if label_de not in translation_cache:\n                    translation_cache[label_de] = translate_de_en(\n                        [label_de], hf_cache)[0]\n                translated_en = translation_cache[label_de]\n            hit = match_embedding(label_en, candidates, hf_cache,\n                                  cand_embeddings, emb_thresh)\n\n        row = {\n            \"htype_code\":   code,\n            \"label_de\":     label_de,\n            \"label_en\":     label_en,\n            \"match_method\": hit[\"method\"]     if hit else \"no_match\",\n            \"confidence\":   hit[\"confidence\"] if hit else 0.0,\n            \"doco_class\":   hit[\"local\"]      if hit else \"\",\n            \"doco_label\":   hit[\"label\"]      if hit else \"\",\n            \"doco_uri\":     hit[\"uri\"]        if hit else \"\",\n            \"translated_en\": translated_en,\n        }\n        rows.append(row)\n        status = f\"{row['match_method']:12s}  {row['confidence']:.2f}  {row['doco_class']}\"\n        print(f\"  {code}  {label_en:<30s}  →  {status}\")\n\n    # Write outputs\n    out_csv  = Path(args.out_csv)\n    out_json = Path(args.out_json)\n    out_csv.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    fieldnames = [\"htype_code\", \"label_de\", \"label_en\", \"match_method\",\n                  \"confidence\", \"doco_class\", \"doco_label\", \"doco_uri\",\n                  \"translated_en\"]\n    with open(out_csv, \"w\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)\n        writer.writeheader()\n        writer.writerows(rows)\n\n    summary = {m: sum(1 for r in rows if r[\"match_method\"] == m)\n               for m in (\"exact\", \"levenshtein\", \"translated\", \"embedding\", \"no_match\")}\n    out_json.write_text(\n        json.dumps({\"summary\": summary, \"mappings\": rows}, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),\n        encoding=\"utf-8\",\n    )\n\n    print(f\"\\nWrote {out_csv}\")\n    print(f\"Wrote {out_json}\")\n    print(\"Summary:\", summary)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```
